// loadUserThemes loads themes from the user config directory
func (tm *ThemeManager) loadUserThemes() {
	themesDir := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes")

	// Create themes directory if it doesn't exist
	if err := os.MkdirAll(themesDir, 0755); err != nil {
		return
	}

	// Read theme files
	entries, err := os.ReadDir(themesDir)
	if err != nil {
		return
	}

	for _, entry := range entries {
		if !entry.IsDir() && filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) == ".json" {
			themePath := filepath.Join(themesDir, entry.Name())
			if theme, err := tm.loadThemeFromFile(themePath); err == nil {
				tm.themes[theme.Name] = theme
			}
		}
	}
}

// loadThemeFromFile loads a theme from a JSON file
func (tm *ThemeManager) loadThemeFromFile(filename string) (*Theme, error) {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(filename)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	var theme Theme
	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &theme); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	return &theme, nil
}

// SaveTheme saves a theme to the user config directory
func (tm *ThemeManager) SaveTheme(theme *Theme) error {
	themesDir := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes")
	if err := os.MkdirAll(themesDir, 0755); err != nil {
		return err
	}

	filename := filepath.Join(themesDir, theme.Name+".json")
	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(theme, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	return os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
}

// GetTheme returns a theme by name
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetTheme(name string) (*Theme, bool) {
	theme, exists := tm.themes[name]
	return theme, exists
}

// GetCurrentTheme returns the currently active theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetCurrentTheme() *Theme {
	if theme, exists := tm.themes[tm.currentTheme]; exists {
		return theme
	}
	// Fallback to quantum theme
	return tm.themes["quantum"]
}

// SetCurrentTheme sets the current theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) SetCurrentTheme(name string) bool {
	if _, exists := tm.themes[name]; exists {
		tm.currentTheme = name
		return true
	}
	return false
}

// GetThemeNames returns all available theme names
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetThemeNames() []string {
	var names []string
	for name := range tm.themes {
		names = append(names, name)
	}
	return names
}

// GetThemeList returns all available themes
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetThemeList() []*Theme {
	var themes []*Theme
	for _, theme := range tm.themes {
		themes = append(themes, theme)
	}
	return themes
}

// CreateCustomTheme creates a new custom theme based on an existing theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) CreateCustomTheme(baseName, newName, description string) (*Theme, error) {
	baseTheme, exists := tm.themes[baseName]
	if !exists {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("base theme '%s' not found", baseName)
	}

	// Create a copy of the base theme
	newTheme := *baseTheme
	newTheme.Name = newName
	newTheme.Description = description
	newTheme.Author = "Custom"
	newTheme.Version = "1.0.0"

	tm.themes[newName] = &newTheme
	return &newTheme, nil
}

// DeleteTheme removes a user theme (built-in themes cannot be deleted)
func (tm *ThemeManager) DeleteTheme(name string) error {
	// Check if it's a built-in theme
	builtinThemes := []string{"quantum", "cyberpunk", "matrix", "neon", "classic"}
	for _, builtin := range builtinThemes {
		if name == builtin {
			return fmt.Errorf("cannot delete built-in theme '%s'", name)
		}
	}

	// Remove from memory
	delete(tm.themes, name)

	// Remove file
	filename := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes", name+".json")
	if err := os.Remove(filename); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
		return err
	}

	// If it was the current theme, switch to quantum
	if tm.currentTheme == name {
		tm.currentTheme = "quantum"
	}

	return nil
}

// ExportTheme exports a theme to a JSON file
func (tm *ThemeManager) ExportTheme(name, filename string) error {
	theme, exists := tm.themes[name]
	if !exists {
		return fmt.Errorf("theme '%s' not found", name)
	}

	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(theme, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	return os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
}

// ImportTheme imports a theme from a JSON file
func (tm *ThemeManager) ImportTheme(filename string) error {
	theme, err := tm.loadThemeFromFile(filename)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	tm.themes[theme.Name] = theme
	return tm.SaveTheme(theme)
}

// ApplyTheme applies a theme to the current application state
func (tm *ThemeManager) ApplyTheme(name string) error {
	if !tm.SetCurrentTheme(name) {
		return fmt.Errorf("theme '%s' not found", name)
	}

	// Save current theme preference
	return tm.saveCurrentTheme()
}

// saveCurrentTheme saves the current theme preference to config
func (tm *ThemeManager) saveCurrentTheme() error {
	configFile := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "current_theme")
	return os.WriteFile(configFile, []byte(tm.currentTheme), 0644)
}

// LoadCurrentTheme loads the saved theme preference
func (tm *ThemeManager) LoadCurrentTheme() {
	configFile := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "current_theme")
	if data, err := os.ReadFile(configFile); err == nil {
		themeName := string(data)
		if _, exists := tm.themes[themeName]; exists {
			tm.currentTheme = themeName
		}
	}
}

// GetLipglossColors converts theme colors to lipgloss colors for easy use
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetLipglossColors() map[string]lipgloss.Color {
	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
	return map[string]lipgloss.Color{
		"primary":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Primary),
		"secondary":          lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Secondary),
		"accent":             lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Accent),
		"success":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Success),
		"warning":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Warning),
		"danger":             lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Danger),
		"background":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Background),
		"background_light":   lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.BackgroundLight),
		"background_dark":    lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.BackgroundDark),
		"text":               lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Text),
		"text_muted":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.TextMuted),
		"text_faint":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.TextFaint),
		"glow":               lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Glow),
		"pulse":              lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Pulse),
		"terminal_bg":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Background),
		"terminal_fg":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Foreground),
		"terminal_cursor":    lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Cursor),
		"terminal_selection": lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Selection),
		"ai_user":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.User),
		"ai_assistant":       lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.Assistant),
		"ai_system":          lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.System),
		"ai_error":           lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.Error),
		"system_cpu":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.CPU),
		"system_memory":      lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Memory),
		"system_network":     lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Network),
		"system_disk":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Disk),
	}
}

// GetBorderStyle returns the appropriate lipgloss border style for the theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetBorderStyle() lipgloss.Border {
	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
	switch theme.Styles.BorderStyle {
	case "rounded":
		return lipgloss.RoundedBorder()
	case "thick":
		return lipgloss.ThickBorder()
	case "double":
		return lipgloss.DoubleBorder()
	default:
		return lipgloss.NormalBorder()
	}
}

// IsEffectEnabled checks if a visual effect is enabled in the current theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) IsEffectEnabled(effect string) bool {
	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
	switch effect {
	case "orbs":
		return theme.Effects.EnableOrbs
	case "light_rays":
		return theme.Effects.EnableLightRays
	case "matrix":
		return theme.Effects.EnableMatrix
	case "energy":
		return theme.Effects.EnableEnergy
	default:
		return false
	}
}

// GetEffectSettings returns effect settings for the current theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetEffectSettings() ThemeEffects {
	return tm.GetCurrentTheme().Effects
}

   201→// checkCommandAvailable checks if a command is available in PATH
   202→func checkCommandAvailable(command string) bool {

   209→}

   211→// generatePrompt creates a shell prompt string
   212→func generatePrompt(dir string) string {
   213→	// Get just the directory name for a cleaner prompt
   214→	dirName := filepath.Base(dir)
   215→	if dirName == "." || dirName == "/" {
   216→		dirName = dir
   217→	}
   218→	return fmt.Sprintf("quantum:%s$ ", dirName)
   219→}
   220→
   221→// ToolComponent interface implementation
   222→
   223→// Init initializes the bash terminal component
   224→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
   225→	return tea.Batch(
   226→		bt.tickCmd(),
   227→	)
   228→}
   229→
   230→// Update handles messages for the bash terminal
   231→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   232→	var cmds []tea.Cmd
   233→
   234→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
   235→	case tea.KeyMsg:
   236→		if !bt.active {
   237→			return bt, nil
   238→		}
   239→
   240→		if cmd := bt.handleKeyMsg(msg); cmd != nil {
   241→			cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
   242→		}
   243→
   244→	case cmdOutputMsg:
   245→		bt.addOutput(msg.output, OutputResult, lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0"))
   246→
   247→	case cmdCompleteMsg:
   248→		bt.isExecuting = false
   249→		if msg.error != nil {
   250→			bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("Command failed: %v", msg.error), OutputError, lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
   251→		} else {
   252→			bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("Command completed (exit code: %d)", msg.exitCode), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#10b981"))
   253→		}
   254→
   255→	case cmdStartMsg:
   256→		bt.isExecuting = true
   257→		bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("Executing: %s", msg.command), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
   258→
   259→	case tickMsg:
   260→		bt.updateVisualEffects()
   261→		cmds = append(cmds, bt.tickCmd())
   262→	}
   263→
   264→	return bt, tea.Batch(cmds...)
   265→}
   266→
   267→// View renders the bash terminal interface
   268→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) View() string {
   269→	if !bt.active || bt.width == 0 || bt.height == 0 {
   270→		return ""
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	if bt.showAIPanel {
   274→		return bt.renderWithAIPanel()
   275→	}
   276→
   277→	return bt.renderTerminalOnly()
   278→}

   283→	bt.height = height

   300→
   301→// Key handling
   302→
   303→// handleKeyMsg processes key messages for the terminal
   304→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleKeyMsg(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
   305→	switch msg.String() {
   306→	case "!":
   307→		// Toggle AI panel
   308→		bt.showAIPanel = !bt.showAIPanel
   309→		return nil
   310→
   311→	case "ctrl+c":
   312→		// Interrupt running command
   313→		if bt.isExecuting && bt.runningCmd != nil {
   314→			bt.runningCmd.Process.Signal(syscall.SIGINT)
   315→			bt.isExecuting = false
   316→			bt.addOutput("^C", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
   317→		}
   318→		return nil
   319→
   320→	case "ctrl+l":
   321→		// Clear terminal
   322→		bt.output = []OutputLine{}
   323→		bt.scrollOffset = 0
   324→		return nil
   325→
   326→	case "enter":
   327→		if bt.showAIPanel && bt.aiInput != "" {
   328→			return bt.handleAIInput()
   329→		}
   330→		return bt.handleCommandInput()
   331→
   332→	case "up":
   333→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   334→			// Handle AI panel navigation
   335→			return nil
   336→		}
   337→		return bt.handleHistoryUp()
   338→
   339→	case "down":
   340→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   341→			// Handle AI panel navigation
   342→			return nil
   343→		}
   344→		return bt.handleHistoryDown()
   345→
   346→	case "left":
   347→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   348→			if bt.aiCursorPos > 0 {
   349→				bt.aiCursorPos--
   350→			}
   351→		} else {
   352→			if bt.cursorPos > 0 {
   353→				bt.cursorPos--
   354→			}
   355→		}
   356→		return nil
   357→
   358→	case "right":
   359→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   360→			if bt.aiCursorPos < len(bt.aiInput) {
   361→				bt.aiCursorPos++
   362→			}
   363→		} else {
   364→			if bt.cursorPos < len(bt.input) {
   365→				bt.cursorPos++
   366→			}
   367→		}
   368→		return nil
   369→
   370→	case "backspace":
   371→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   372→			if bt.aiCursorPos > 0 {
   373→				bt.aiInput = bt.aiInput[:bt.aiCursorPos-1] + bt.aiInput[bt.aiCursorPos:]
   374→				bt.aiCursorPos--
   375→			}
   376→		} else {
   377→			if bt.cursorPos > 0 {
   378→				bt.input = bt.input[:bt.cursorPos-1] + bt.input[bt.cursorPos:]
   379→				bt.cursorPos--
   380→			}
   381→		}
   382→		return nil
   383→
   384→	case "delete":
   385→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   386→			if bt.aiCursorPos < len(bt.aiInput) {
   387→				bt.aiInput = bt.aiInput[:bt.aiCursorPos] + bt.aiInput[bt.aiCursorPos+1:]
   388→			}
   389→		} else {
   390→			if bt.cursorPos < len(bt.input) {
   391→				bt.input = bt.input[:bt.cursorPos] + bt.input[bt.cursorPos+1:]
   392→			}
   393→		}
   394→		return nil
   395→
   396→	case "home", "ctrl+a":
   397→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   398→			bt.aiCursorPos = 0
   399→		} else {
   400→			bt.cursorPos = 0
   401→		}
   402→		return nil
   403→
   404→	case "end", "ctrl+e":
   405→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   406→			bt.aiCursorPos = len(bt.aiInput)
   407→		} else {
   408→			bt.cursorPos = len(bt.input)
   409→		}
   410→		return nil
   411→
   412→	case "page_up":
   413→		bt.scrollUp()
   414→		return nil
   415→
   416→	case "page_down":
   417→		bt.scrollDown()
   418→		return nil
   419→
   420→	case "tab":
   421→		// Tab completion
   422→		if !bt.showAIPanel {
   423→			return bt.handleTabCompletion()
   424→		}
   425→		return nil
   426→
   427→	default:
   428→		// Regular character input
   429→		if len(msg.String()) == 1 {
   430→			char := msg.String()
   431→			if bt.showAIPanel {
   432→				bt.aiInput = bt.aiInput[:bt.aiCursorPos] + char + bt.aiInput[bt.aiCursorPos:]
   433→				bt.aiCursorPos++
   434→			} else {
   435→				bt.input = bt.input[:bt.cursorPos] + char + bt.input[bt.cursorPos:]
   436→				bt.cursorPos++
   437→			}
   438→		}
   439→		return nil
   440→	}
   441→}
   442→
   443→// Command execution
   444→
   445→// handleCommandInput processes command input and execution
   446→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleCommandInput() tea.Cmd {
   447→	if bt.input == "" {
   448→		bt.addOutput(bt.prompt, OutputCommand, lipgloss.Color("#6366f1"))
   449→		return nil
   450→	}
   451→
   452→	command := strings.TrimSpace(bt.input)
   453→
   454→	// Add to history
   455→	bt.history = append(bt.history, command)
   456→	if len(bt.history) > 100 {
   457→		bt.history = bt.history[1:]
   458→	}
   459→	bt.historyPos = -1
   460→
   461→	// Display command
   462→	bt.addOutput(bt.prompt+command, OutputCommand, lipgloss.Color("#6366f1"))
   463→
   464→	// Handle built-in commands
   465→	if cmd := bt.handleBuiltinCommand(command); cmd != nil {
   466→		bt.input = ""
   467→		bt.cursorPos = 0
   468→		return cmd
   469→	}
   470→
   471→	// Execute external command
   472→	bt.input = ""
   473→	bt.cursorPos = 0
   474→	return bt.executeCommand(command)
   475→}
   476→
   477→// handleBuiltinCommand handles built-in terminal commands
   478→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleBuiltinCommand(command string) tea.Cmd {
   479→	parts := strings.Fields(command)
   480→	if len(parts) == 0 {
   481→		return nil
   482→	}
   483→
   484→	switch parts[0] {
   485→	case "help":
   486→		bt.showHelp()
   487→		return nil
   488→
   489→	case "clear", "cls":
   490→		bt.output = []OutputLine{}
   491→		bt.scrollOffset = 0
   492→		return nil
   493→
   494→	case "cd":
   495→		return bt.changeDirectory(parts)
   496→
   497→	case "pwd":
   498→		bt.addOutput(bt.currentDir, OutputResult, lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0"))
   499→		return nil
   500→
   501→	case "history":
   502→		bt.showHistory()
   503→		return nil
   504→
   505→	case "ai":
   506→		return bt.handleAICommand(parts[1:])
   507→
   508→	case "theme":
   509→		return bt.handleThemeCommand(parts[1:])
   510→
   511→	case "exit", "quit":
   512→		// This would typically quit the application
   513→		bt.addOutput("Use 'q' or Ctrl+C to quit the application", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
   514→		return nil
   515→
   516→	default:
   517→		return nil // Not a built-in command
   518→	}
   519→}
   520→
   521→// executeCommand executes an external command
   522→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) executeCommand(command string) tea.Cmd {
   523→	return func() tea.Msg {
   524→		// Parse command and arguments
   525→		parts := strings.Fields(command)
   526→		if len(parts) == 0 {
   527→			return cmdCompleteMsg{exitCode: 1, error: fmt.Errorf("empty command")}
   528→		}
   529→
   530→		// Create command
   531→		cmd := exec.Command(parts[0], parts[1:]...)
   532→		cmd.Dir = bt.currentDir
   533→
   534→		// Set up pipes
   535→		stdout, err := cmd.StdoutPipe()
   536→		if err != nil {
   537→			return cmdCompleteMsg{exitCode: 1, error: err}
   538→		}
   539→		stderr, err := cmd.StderrPipe()
   540→		if err != nil {
   541→			return cmdCompleteMsg{exitCode: 1, error: err}
   542→		}
   543→
   544→		// Start command
   545→		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
   546→			return cmdCompleteMsg{exitCode: 1, error: err}
   547→		}
   548→
   549→		bt.runningCmd = cmd
   550→
   551→		// Read output in goroutines
   552→		go bt.readCommandOutput(stdout, false)
   553→		go bt.readCommandOutput(stderr, true)
   554→
   555→		// Wait for completion
   556→		err = cmd.Wait()
   557→		exitCode := 0
   558→		if err != nil {
   559→			if exitError, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
   560→				exitCode = exitError.ExitCode()
   561→			} else {
   562→				exitCode = 1
   563→			}
   564→		}
   565→
   566→		return cmdCompleteMsg{exitCode: exitCode, error: err}
   567→	}
   568→}
   569→
   570→// readCommandOutput reads output from command stdout/stderr
   571→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) readCommandOutput(pipe io.ReadCloser, isError bool) {
   572→	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(pipe)
   573→	for scanner.Scan() {
   574→		line := scanner.Text()
   575→		color := lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")
   576→		outputType := OutputResult
   577→		if isError {
   578→			color = lipgloss.Color("#ef4444")
   579→			outputType = OutputError
   580→		}
   581→		bt.addOutput(line, outputType, color)
   582→	}
   583→}
   584→
   585→// AI Assistant functionality
   586→
   587→// handleAIInput processes AI assistant input
   588→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleAIInput() tea.Cmd {
   589→	if bt.aiInput == "" {
   590→		return nil
   591→	}
   592→
   593→	input := strings.TrimSpace(bt.aiInput)
   594→
   595→	// Add user message to AI history
   596→	bt.aiHistory = append(bt.aiHistory, AIMessage{
   597→		Role:      "user",
   598→		Content:   input,
   599→		Timestamp: time.Now(),
   600→		Agent:     bt.selectedAgent,
   601→	})
   602→
   603→	// Clear input
   604→	bt.aiInput = ""
   605→	bt.aiCursorPos = 0
   606→
   607→	// Find selected agent
   608→	var agent *AIAgent
   609→	for _, a := range bt.availableAgents {
   610→		if a.Command == bt.selectedAgent {
   611→			agent = &a
   612→			break
   613→		}
   614→	}
   615→
   616→	if agent == nil || !agent.Available {
   617→		bt.aiHistory = append(bt.aiHistory, AIMessage{
   618→			Role:      "assistant",
   619→			Content:   "Sorry, the selected AI agent is not available.",
   620→			Timestamp: time.Now(),
   621→			Agent:     bt.selectedAgent,
   622→		})
   623→		return nil
   624→	}
   625→
   626→	// Execute AI command
   627→	return bt.executeAICommand(agent, input)
   628→}
   629→
   630→// executeAICommand executes an AI assistant command
   631→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) executeAICommand(agent *AIAgent, input string) tea.Cmd {
   632→	return func() tea.Msg {
   633→		// Construct AI command
   634→		var cmdParts []string
   635→		switch agent.Command {
   636→		case "claude-code":
   637→			cmdParts = []string{"claude-code", input}
   638→		case "opencode":
   639→			cmdParts = []string{"opencode", input}
   640→		case "gemini":
   641→			cmdParts = []string{"gemini", input}
   642→		case "gh copilot":
   643→			cmdParts = []string{"gh", "copilot", "suggest", input}
   644→		case "aider":
   645→			cmdParts = []string{"aider", "--message", input}
   646→		case "sgpt":
   647→			cmdParts = []string{"sgpt", input}
   648→		default:
   649→			return nil
   650→		}
   651→
   652→		// Execute command
   653→		cmd := exec.Command(cmdParts[0], cmdParts[1:]...)
   654→		cmd.Dir = bt.currentDir
   655→
   656→		output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
   657→		if err != nil {
   658→			bt.aiHistory = append(bt.aiHistory, AIMessage{
   659→				Role:      "assistant",
   660→				Content:   fmt.Sprintf("Error: %v\n%s", err, string(output)),
   661→				Timestamp: time.Now(),
   662→				Agent:     agent.Command,
   663→			})
   664→		} else {
   665→			bt.aiHistory = append(bt.aiHistory, AIMessage{
   666→				Role:      "assistant",
   667→				Content:   string(output),
   668→				Timestamp: time.Now(),
   669→				Agent:     agent.Command,
   670→			})
   671→		}
   672→
   673→		return nil
   674→	}
   675→}
   676→
   677→// handleAICommand handles the 'ai' built-in command
   678→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleAICommand(args []string) tea.Cmd {
   679→	if len(args) == 0 {
   680→		bt.addOutput("Available AI agents:", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
   681→		for _, agent := range bt.availableAgents {
   682→			status := "❌ Not available"
   683→			if agent.Available {
   684→				status = "✅ Available"
   685→			}
   686→			bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("  %s %s - %s %s", agent.Icon, agent.Name, agent.Description, status), OutputInfo, agent.Color)
   687→		}
   688→		return nil
   689→	}
   690→
   691→	switch args[0] {
   692→	case "select":
   693→		if len(args) > 1 {
   694→			for _, agent := range bt.availableAgents {
   695→				if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(agent.Name), strings.ToLower(args[1])) ||
   696→					strings.Contains(agent.Command, args[1]) {
   697→					bt.selectedAgent = agent.Command
   698→					bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("Selected AI agent: %s %s", agent.Icon, agent.Name), OutputInfo, agent.Color)
   699→					return nil
   700→				}
   701→			}
   702→			bt.addOutput("AI agent not found", OutputError, lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
   703→		} else {
   704→			bt.addOutput("Usage: ai select <agent_name>", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
   705→		}
   706→
   707→	case "panel":
   708→		bt.showAIPanel = !bt.showAIPanel
   709→		status := "hidden"
   710→		if bt.showAIPanel {
   711→			status = "shown"
   712→		}
   713→		bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("AI panel %s", status), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6"))
   714→
   715→	default:
   716→		bt.addOutput("AI commands: select <agent>, panel", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
   717→	}
   718→
   719→	return nil
   720→}
   721→
   722→// Built-in command handlers
   723→
   724→// showHelp displays help information
   725→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) showHelp() {
   726→	help := []string{
   727→		"Quantum Terminal - Built-in Commands:",
   728→		"",
   729→		"Navigation:",
   730→		"  cd <dir>     - Change directory",
   731→		"  pwd          - Print working directory",
   732→		"  ls           - List directory contents (external)",
   733→		"",
   734→		"Terminal:",
   735→		"  clear/cls    - Clear terminal",
   736→		"  history      - Show command history",
   737→		"  help         - Show this help",
   738→		"",
   739→		"AI Assistant:",
   740→		"  ai           - Show available AI agents",
   741→		"  ai select    - Select AI agent",
   742→		"  ai panel     - Toggle AI panel",
   743→		"  !            - Toggle AI panel (hotkey)",
   744→		"",
   745→		"Theme:",
   746→		"  theme list   - Show available themes",
   747→		"  theme set    - Set theme",
   748→		"",
   749→		"Keyboard Shortcuts:",
   750→		"  Ctrl+C       - Interrupt command",
   751→		"  Ctrl+L       - Clear terminal",
   752→		"  Tab          - Auto-complete",
   753→		"  ↑/↓          - Command history",
   754→		"  Page Up/Down - Scroll output",
   755→		"",
   756→		"External commands are executed in the current directory.",
   757→	}
   758→
   759→	for _, line := range help {
   760→		color := lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")
   761→		if strings.HasSuffix(line, ":") {
   762→			color = lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")
   763→		}
   764→		bt.addOutput(line, OutputInfo, color)
   765→	}
   766→}
   767→
   768→// changeDirectory handles the cd command
   769→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) changeDirectory(parts []string) tea.Cmd {
   770→	var targetDir string
   771→	if len(parts) == 1 {
   772→		// cd with no arguments goes to home
   773→		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   774→		if err != nil {
   775→			bt.addOutput("Error getting home directory", OutputError, lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
   776→			return nil
   777→		}
   778→		targetDir = home
   779→	} else {
   780→		targetDir = parts[1]
   781→	}
   782→
   783→	// Handle relative paths
   784→	if !filepath.IsAbs(targetDir) {
   785→		targetDir = filepath.Join(bt.currentDir, targetDir)
   786→	}
   787→
   788→	// Clean the path
   789→	targetDir = filepath.Clean(targetDir)
   790→
   791→	// Check if directory exists
   792→	if info, err := os.Stat(targetDir); err != nil {
   793→		bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("cd: %s: No such file or directory", parts[1]), OutputError, lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
   794→		return nil
   795→	} else if !info.IsDir() {
   796→		bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("cd: %s: Not a directory", parts[1]), OutputError, lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
   797→		return nil
   798→	}
   799→
   800→	// Change directory
   801→	bt.currentDir = targetDir
   802→	bt.prompt = generatePrompt(bt.currentDir)
   803→	bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("Changed directory to: %s", bt.currentDir), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#10b981"))
   804→
   805→	return nil
   806→}
   807→
   808→// showHistory displays command history
   809→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) showHistory() {
   810→	bt.addOutput("Command History:", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
   811→	for i, cmd := range bt.history {
   812→		bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("%3d  %s", i+1, cmd), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0"))
   813→	}
   814→}
   815→
   816→// handleThemeCommand handles theme-related commands
   817→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleThemeCommand(args []string) tea.Cmd {
   818→	if len(args) == 0 {
   819→		bt.addOutput("Theme commands: list, set <theme>", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
   820→		return nil
   821→	}
   822→
   823→	switch args[0] {
   824→	case "list":
   825→		themes := []string{"quantum", "cyberpunk", "matrix", "neon", "classic"}
   826→		bt.addOutput("Available themes:", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
   827→		for _, theme := range themes {
   828→			bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("  %s", theme), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0"))
   829→		}
   830→
   831→	case "set":
   832→		if len(args) > 1 {
   833→			bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("Theme set to: %s", args[1]), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#10b981"))
   834→			// TODO: Implement theme switching
   835→		} else {
   836→			bt.addOutput("Usage: theme set <theme_name>", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
   837→		}
   838→
   839→	default:
   840→		bt.addOutput("Unknown theme command", OutputError, lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
   841→	}
   842→
   843→	return nil
   844→}
   845→
   846→// History navigation
   847→
   848→// handleHistoryUp navigates up in command history
   849→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleHistoryUp() tea.Cmd {
   850→	if len(bt.history) == 0 {
   851→		return nil
   852→	}
   853→
   854→	if bt.historyPos == -1 {
   855→		bt.historyPos = len(bt.history) - 1
   856→	} else if bt.historyPos > 0 {
   857→		bt.historyPos--
   858→	}
   859→
   860→	bt.input = bt.history[bt.historyPos]
   861→	bt.cursorPos = len(bt.input)
   862→	return nil
   863→}
   864→
   865→// handleHistoryDown navigates down in command history
   866→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleHistoryDown() tea.Cmd {
   867→	if len(bt.history) == 0 || bt.historyPos == -1 {
   868→		return nil
   869→	}
   870→
   871→	if bt.historyPos < len(bt.history)-1 {
   872→		bt.historyPos++
   873→		bt.input = bt.history[bt.historyPos]
   874→		bt.cursorPos = len(bt.input)
   875→	} else {
   876→		bt.historyPos = -1
   877→		bt.input = ""
   878→		bt.cursorPos = 0
   879→	}
   880→
   881→	return nil
   882→}
   883→
   884→// Tab completion
   885→
   886→// handleTabCompletion handles tab completion for commands and files
   887→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) handleTabCompletion() tea.Cmd {
   888→	if bt.input == "" {
   889→		return nil
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	// Simple file completion for now
   893→	parts := strings.Fields(bt.input)
   894→	if len(parts) == 0 {
   895→		return nil
   896→	}
   897→
   898→	lastPart := parts[len(parts)-1]
   899→
   900→	// Get directory to search in
   901→	searchDir := bt.currentDir
   902→	if strings.Contains(lastPart, "/") {
   903→		searchDir = filepath.Dir(filepath.Join(bt.currentDir, lastPart))
   904→		lastPart = filepath.Base(lastPart)
   905→	}
   906→
   907→	// Find matching files
   908→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(searchDir)
   909→	if err != nil {
   910→		return nil
   911→	}
   912→
   913→	var matches []string
   914→	for _, entry := range entries {
   915→		if strings.HasPrefix(entry.Name(), lastPart) {
   916→			matches = append(matches, entry.Name())
   917→		}
   918→	}
   919→
   920→	if len(matches) == 1 {
   921→		// Complete the match
   922→		newInput := strings.Join(parts[:len(parts)-1], " ")
   923→		if len(newInput) > 0 {
   924→			newInput += " "
   925→		}
   926→		newInput += matches[0]
   927→		bt.input = newInput
   928→		bt.cursorPos = len(bt.input)
   929→	} else if len(matches) > 1 {
   930→		// Show matches
   931→		bt.addOutput("Possible completions:", OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
   932→		for _, match := range matches {
   933→			bt.addOutput(fmt.Sprintf("  %s", match), OutputInfo, lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0"))
   934→		}
   935→	}
   936→
   937→	return nil
   938→}
   939→
   940→// Output management
   941→
   942→// addOutput adds a line to the terminal output
   943→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) addOutput(text string, outputType OutputType, color lipgloss.Color) {
   944→	// Split long lines
   945→	maxWidth := bt.width - 4
   946→	if maxWidth < 40 {
   947→		maxWidth = 40
   948→	}
   949→
   950→	lines := bt.wrapText(text, maxWidth)
   951→	for _, line := range lines {
   952→		bt.output = append(bt.output, OutputLine{
   953→			Text:      line,
   954→			Timestamp: time.Now(),
   955→			Type:      outputType,
   956→			Color:     color,
   957→		})
   958→	}
   959→
   960→	// Limit output size
   961→	if len(bt.output) > bt.maxOutputSize {
   962→		bt.output = bt.output[len(bt.output)-bt.maxOutputSize:]
   963→	}
   964→
   965→	// Auto-scroll to bottom
   966→	bt.scrollToBottom()
   967→}
   968→
   969→// wrapText wraps text to fit within the specified width
   970→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) wrapText(text string, width int) []string {
   971→	if len(text) <= width {
   972→		return []string{text}
   973→	}
   974→
   975→	var lines []string
   976→	for len(text) > width {
   977→		// Find the last space before the width limit
   978→		breakPoint := width
   979→		for i := width - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
   980→			if text[i] == ' ' {
   981→				breakPoint = i
   982→				break
   983→			}
   984→		}
   985→
   986→		lines = append(lines, text[:breakPoint])
   987→		text = strings.TrimSpace(text[breakPoint:])
   988→	}
   989→
   990→	if len(text) > 0 {
   991→		lines = append(lines, text)
   992→	}
   993→
   994→	return lines
   995→}
   996→
   997→// Scrolling
   998→
   999→// scrollUp scrolls the output up
  1000→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) scrollUp() {
  1001→	if bt.scrollOffset > 0 {
  1002→		bt.scrollOffset--
  1003→	}
  1004→}
  1005→
  1006→// scrollDown scrolls the output down
  1007→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) scrollDown() {
  1008→	maxScroll := len(bt.output) - (bt.height - 5)
  1009→	if maxScroll < 0 {
  1010→		maxScroll = 0
  1011→	}
  1012→	if bt.scrollOffset < maxScroll {
  1013→		bt.scrollOffset++
  1014→	}
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// scrollToBottom scrolls to the bottom of the output
  1018→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) scrollToBottom() {
  1019→	maxScroll := len(bt.output) - (bt.height - 5)
  1020→	if maxScroll < 0 {
  1021→		maxScroll = 0
  1022→	}
  1023→	bt.scrollOffset = maxScroll
  1024→}

  1032→	bt.glowIntensity = 0.3 + 0.2*math.Sin(bt.pulsePhase)

  1067→// renderWithAIPanel renders the terminal with the AI assistant panel

  1085→// renderHeader renders the terminal header
  1086→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) renderHeader() string {
  1087→	title := "⚡ QUANTUM BASH TERMINAL"
  1088→	if bt.showAIPanel {

  1090→	}
  1091→
  1092→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1093→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
  1094→		Bold(true).
  1095→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1096→		Width(bt.width)
  1097→
  1098→	timeStr := time.Now().Format("15:04:05")
  1099→	timeStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1100→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).
  1101→		Align(lipgloss.Right).
  1102→		Width(bt.width)

  1106→
  1107→// renderOutput renders the terminal output area
  1108→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) renderOutput() string {
  1109→	var s strings.Builder
  1110→
  1111→	outputHeight := bt.height - 6 // Leave space for header, input, status
  1112→	if outputHeight < 5 {
  1113→		outputHeight = 5
  1114→	}
  1115→
  1116→	startIdx := bt.scrollOffset
  1117→	endIdx := startIdx + outputHeight
  1118→	if endIdx > len(bt.output) {
  1119→		endIdx = len(bt.output)
  1120→	}
  1121→
  1122→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
  1123→		line := bt.output[i]
  1124→
  1125→		// Add timestamp for important messages
  1126→		prefix := ""
  1127→		if line.Type == OutputError || line.Type == OutputAI {
  1128→			prefix = line.Timestamp.Format("15:04 ")
  1129→		}
  1130→
  1131→		styledLine := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1132→			Foreground(line.Color).
  1133→			Render(prefix + line.Text)
  1134→
  1135→		s.WriteString(styledLine + "\n")
  1136→	}
  1137→
  1138→	// Fill remaining space
  1139→	for i := endIdx - startIdx; i < outputHeight; i++ {
  1140→		s.WriteString("\n")
  1141→	}
  1142→
  1143→	return s.String()
  1144→}

  1146→// renderInputLine renders the command input line
  1147→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) renderInputLine() string {
  1148→	var s strings.Builder
  1149→
  1150→	// Separator line
  1151→	separator := strings.Repeat("─", bt.width)
  1152→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).Render(separator) + "\n")
  1153→
  1154→	// Input line
  1155→	promptStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1156→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
  1157→		Bold(true)
  1158→
  1159→	inputStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1160→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0"))
  1161→
  1162→	// Add cursor
  1163→	displayInput := bt.input
  1164→	if bt.cursorPos < len(displayInput) {
  1165→		displayInput = displayInput[:bt.cursorPos] + "█" + displayInput[bt.cursorPos+1:]
  1166→	} else {
  1167→		displayInput += "█"
  1168→	}
  1169→
  1170→	s.WriteString(promptStyle.Render(bt.prompt) + inputStyle.Render(displayInput))
  1171→
  1172→	return s.String()
  1173→}

  1175→// renderStatusBar renders the status bar

  1190→
  1191→	// Directory

  1199→	status := strings.Join(statusParts, " • ")

  1256→// renderAgentSelector renders the AI agent selector
  1257→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) renderAgentSelector() string {
  1258→	var selectedAgent *AIAgent
  1259→	for _, agent := range bt.availableAgents {
  1260→		if agent.Command == bt.selectedAgent {
  1261→			selectedAgent = &agent
  1262→			break
  1263→		}
  1264→	}
  1265→
  1266→	if selectedAgent == nil {
  1267→		return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1268→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#ef4444")).
  1269→			Width(bt.aiPanelWidth).
  1270→			Render("No agent selected")
  1271→	}
  1272→
  1273→	status := "❌ Unavailable"
  1274→	if selectedAgent.Available {
  1275→		status = "✅ Available"
  1276→	}
  1277→
  1278→	return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1279→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  1280→		BorderForeground(selectedAgent.Color).
  1281→		Padding(0, 1).
  1282→		Width(bt.aiPanelWidth - 2).
  1283→		Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s %s\n%s\n%s",
  1284→			selectedAgent.Icon, selectedAgent.Name,
  1285→			selectedAgent.Description, status))
  1286→}
  1287→
  1288→// renderAIChat renders the AI chat history
  1289→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) renderAIChat(height int) string {
  1290→	var s strings.Builder
  1291→
  1292→	// Chat separator
  1293→	separator := strings.Repeat("─", bt.aiPanelWidth)
  1294→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).Render(separator) + "\n")
  1295→
  1296→	if len(bt.aiHistory) == 0 {
  1297→		// No chat history
  1298→		placeholder := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1299→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).
  1300→			Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1301→			Width(bt.aiPanelWidth).
  1302→			Height(height - 2).
  1303→			Render("No conversation yet.\nType a message below to start.")
  1304→		s.WriteString(placeholder)
  1305→	} else {
  1306→		// Show recent messages
  1307→		startIdx := len(bt.aiHistory) - height + 2
  1308→		if startIdx < 0 {
  1309→			startIdx = 0
  1310→		}
  1311→
  1312→		for i := startIdx; i < len(bt.aiHistory) && i < startIdx+height-2; i++ {
  1313→			msg := bt.aiHistory[i]
  1314→
  1315→			var style lipgloss.Style
  1316→			var prefix string
  1317→
  1318→			if msg.Role == "user" {
  1319→				style = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"))
  1320→				prefix = "👤 You: "
  1321→			} else {
  1322→				style = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981"))
  1323→				prefix = "🤖 AI: "
  1324→			}
  1325→
  1326→			// Wrap message content
  1327→			maxWidth := bt.aiPanelWidth - len(prefix) - 2
  1328→			if maxWidth < 20 {
  1329→				maxWidth = 20
  1330→			}
  1331→			wrappedLines := bt.wrapText(msg.Content, maxWidth)
  1332→
  1333→			for j, line := range wrappedLines {
  1334→				if j == 0 {
  1335→					s.WriteString(style.Render(prefix+line) + "\n")
  1336→				} else {
  1337→					s.WriteString(style.Render(strings.Repeat(" ", len(prefix))+line) + "\n")
  1338→				}
  1339→			}
  1340→		}
  1341→	}
  1342→
  1343→	return s.String()
  1344→}
  1345→
  1346→// renderAIInputLine renders the AI input line
  1347→func (bt *BashTerminalModel) renderAIInputLine() string {
  1348→	var s strings.Builder
  1349→
  1350→	// Separator
  1351→	separator := strings.Repeat("─", bt.aiPanelWidth)
  1352→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).Render(separator) + "\n")
  1353→
  1354→	// Input prompt
  1355→	promptStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1356→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).
  1357→		Bold(true)

  1359→	inputStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().

  1361→
  1362→	// Add cursor
  1363→	displayInput := bt.aiInput
  1364→	if bt.aiCursorPos < len(displayInput) {
  1365→		displayInput = displayInput[:bt.aiCursorPos] + "█" + displayInput[bt.aiCursorPos+1:]
  1366→	} else {

  1368→	}
  1369→
  1370→	s.WriteString(promptStyle.Render("AI> ") + inputStyle.Render(displayInput))
  1371→
  1372→	return s.String()
  1373→}

   270→// MetricHistory holds historical data for a specific metric
   271→type MetricHistory struct {
   272→	Name        string
   273→	Unit        string

   276→	MinValue    float64
   277→	MaxValue    float64
   278→	LastUpdated time.Time
   279→}
   280→
   281→// HistoryManager manages historical data collection and storage
   282→type HistoryManager struct {
   283→	metrics         map[string]*MetricHistory
   284→	retentionPeriod time.Duration
   285→	cleanupInterval time.Duration
   286→	lastCleanup     time.Time
   287→}
   288→
   289→// NewHistoryManager creates a new history manager
   290→func NewHistoryManager() *HistoryManager {
   291→	hm := &HistoryManager{
   292→		metrics:         make(map[string]*MetricHistory),
   293→		retentionPeriod: 12 * time.Hour,   // Keep 12 hours of data
   294→		cleanupInterval: 30 * time.Minute, // Cleanup every 30 minutes
   295→		lastCleanup:     time.Now(),
   296→	}
   297→	hm.initializeMetrics()
   298→	return hm
   299→}
   300→
   301→// initializeMetrics sets up default metric histories
   302→func (hm *HistoryManager) initializeMetrics() {
   303→	metrics := []struct {
   304→		name      string
   305→		unit      string
   306→		maxPoints int
   307→	}{
   308→		{"cpu_usage", "%", 720},            // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   309→		{"memory_usage", "%", 720},         // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   310→		{"load_average", "", 720},          // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   311→		{"disk_usage", "%", 288},           // 12 hours at 2.5-minute intervals
   312→		{"network_in", "MB/s", 720},        // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   313→		{"network_out", "MB/s", 720},       // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   314→		{"process_count", "count", 720},    // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   315→		{"connection_count", "count", 720}, // 12 hours at 1-minute intervals
   316→	}
   317→
   318→	for _, metric := range metrics {
   319→		hm.metrics[metric.name] = &MetricHistory{
   320→			Name:        metric.name,
   321→			Unit:        metric.unit,
   322→			MaxPoints:   metric.maxPoints,
   323→			DataPoints:  make([]HistoricalDataPoint, 0, metric.maxPoints),
   324→			MinValue:    math.Inf(1),  // Positive infinity
   325→			MaxValue:    math.Inf(-1), // Negative infinity
   326→			LastUpdated: time.Now(),
   327→		}
   328→	}
   329→}
   330→
   331→// AddDataPoint adds a new data point to a metric's history
   332→func (hm *HistoryManager) AddDataPoint(metricName string, value float64, timestamp time.Time) {
   333→	metric, exists := hm.metrics[metricName]
   334→	if !exists {
   335→		return
   336→	}
   337→
   338→	dataPoint := HistoricalDataPoint{
   339→		Timestamp: timestamp,
   340→		Value:     value,
   341→		Label:     fmt.Sprintf("%.2f", value),
   342→	}
   343→
   344→	// Add the data point
   345→	metric.DataPoints = append(metric.DataPoints, dataPoint)
   346→
   347→	// Update min/max values
   348→	if value < metric.MinValue {
   349→		metric.MinValue = value
   350→	}
   351→	if value > metric.MaxValue {
   352→		metric.MaxValue = value
   353→	}
   354→
   355→	// Keep only the most recent points
   356→	if len(metric.DataPoints) > metric.MaxPoints {
   357→		metric.DataPoints = metric.DataPoints[1:]
   358→	}
   359→
   360→	metric.LastUpdated = timestamp
   361→
   362→	// Perform cleanup if needed
   363→	if time.Since(hm.lastCleanup) > hm.cleanupInterval {
   364→		hm.cleanup()
   365→	}
   366→}
   367→
   368→// cleanup removes old data points beyond the retention period
   369→func (hm *HistoryManager) cleanup() {
   370→	cutoff := time.Now().Add(-hm.retentionPeriod)
   371→
   372→	for _, metric := range hm.metrics {
   373→		// Find the first data point within retention period
   374→		cutoffIndex := 0
   375→		for i, point := range metric.DataPoints {
   376→			if point.Timestamp.After(cutoff) {
   377→				cutoffIndex = i
   378→				break
   379→			}
   380→		}
   381→
   382→		// Remove old data points
   383→		if cutoffIndex > 0 {
   384→			metric.DataPoints = metric.DataPoints[cutoffIndex:]
   385→
   386→			// Recalculate min/max values
   387→			metric.MinValue = math.Inf(1)
   388→			metric.MaxValue = math.Inf(-1)
   389→			for _, point := range metric.DataPoints {
   390→				if point.Value < metric.MinValue {
   391→					metric.MinValue = point.Value
   392→				}
   393→				if point.Value > metric.MaxValue {
   394→					metric.MaxValue = point.Value
   395→				}
   396→			}
   397→		}
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	hm.lastCleanup = time.Now()
   401→}
   402→
   403→// GetMetricHistory returns the history for a specific metric
   404→func (hm *HistoryManager) GetMetricHistory(metricName string) *MetricHistory {
   405→	if metric, exists := hm.metrics[metricName]; exists {
   406→		// Return a copy to prevent external modification
   407→		historyCopy := &MetricHistory{
   408→			Name:        metric.Name,
   409→			Unit:        metric.Unit,
   410→			MaxPoints:   metric.MaxPoints,
   411→			DataPoints:  make([]HistoricalDataPoint, len(metric.DataPoints)),
   412→			MinValue:    metric.MinValue,
   413→			MaxValue:    metric.MaxValue,
   414→			LastUpdated: metric.LastUpdated,
   415→		}
   416→		copy(historyCopy.DataPoints, metric.DataPoints)
   417→		return historyCopy
   418→	}
   419→	return nil
   420→}

   422→// GetAllMetrics returns a list of all available metric names
   423→func (hm *HistoryManager) GetAllMetrics() []string {
   424→	metrics := make([]string, 0, len(hm.metrics))
   425→	for name := range hm.metrics {
   426→		metrics = append(metrics, name)
   427→	}
   428→	sort.Strings(metrics)
   429→	return metrics
   430→}
   431→
   432→// UpdateSystemMetrics updates historical data with current system metrics
   433→func (hm *HistoryManager) UpdateSystemMetrics(stats SystemStats, processes []Process, connections []NetworkConnection, interfaces []NetworkInterface) {
   434→	now := time.Now()
   435→
   436→	// CPU usage (average)
   437→	if len(stats.CPUUsage) > 0 {
   438→		avgCPU := 0.0
   439→		for _, usage := range stats.CPUUsage {
   440→			avgCPU += usage
   441→		}
   442→		avgCPU /= float64(len(stats.CPUUsage))
   443→		hm.AddDataPoint("cpu_usage", avgCPU, now)
   444→	}
   445→
   446→	// Memory usage
   447→	if stats.MemTotal > 0 {
   448→		memUsage := float64(stats.MemUsed) / float64(stats.MemTotal) * 100
   449→		hm.AddDataPoint("memory_usage", memUsage, now)
   450→	}
   451→
   452→	// Load average
   453→	hm.AddDataPoint("load_average", stats.LoadAvg[0], now)
   454→
   455→	// Process count
   456→	hm.AddDataPoint("process_count", float64(len(processes)), now)
   457→
   458→	// Connection count
   459→	hm.AddDataPoint("connection_count", float64(len(connections)), now)
   460→
   461→	// Network throughput (simplified - would need rate calculation for real implementation)
   462→	totalBytesIn := uint64(0)
   463→	totalBytesOut := uint64(0)
   464→	for _, iface := range interfaces {
   465→		totalBytesIn += iface.BytesIn
   466→		totalBytesOut += iface.BytesOut
   467→	}
   468→
   469→	// Convert to MB/s (this is simplified - real implementation would track deltas)
   470→	hm.AddDataPoint("network_in", float64(totalBytesIn)/(1024*1024), now)
   471→	hm.AddDataPoint("network_out", float64(totalBytesOut)/(1024*1024), now)
   472→}

   530→// ToolComponent interface implementation
   531→
   532→// Init initializes the system monitor component
   533→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
   534→	return tea.Batch(
   535→		sm.fetchProcessesCmd(),
   536→		sm.fetchNetworkDataCmd(),
   537→		sm.fetchSystemAlertsCmd(),
   538→		sm.tickCmd(),
   539→	)
   540→}
   541→
   542→// Update handles messages for the system monitor
   543→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   544→	var cmds []tea.Cmd
   545→
   546→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
   547→	case tea.KeyMsg:
   548→		if !sm.active {
   549→			return sm, nil
   550→		}
   551→
   552→		if cmd := sm.handleKeyMsg(msg); cmd != nil {
   553→			cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
   554→		}
   555→
   556→	case processDataMsg:
   557→		sm.updateProcessData(msg)
   558→
   559→	case networkDataMsg:
   560→		sm.updateNetworkData(msg)
   561→
   562→	case alertDataMsg:
   563→		sm.updateAlertData(msg)
   564→
   565→	case tickMsg:
   566→		sm.updateVisualEffects()
   567→
   568→		// Fetch new data periodically
   569→		if sm.frame%40 == 0 { // 2 seconds
   570→			cmds = append(cmds, sm.fetchProcessesCmd())
   571→		}
   572→		if sm.frame%50 == 0 { // 2.5 seconds
   573→			cmds = append(cmds, sm.fetchNetworkDataCmd())
   574→		}
   575→		if sm.frame%80 == 0 { // 4 seconds
   576→			cmds = append(cmds, sm.fetchSystemAlertsCmd())
   577→		}
   578→
   579→		cmds = append(cmds, sm.tickCmd())
   580→	}
   581→
   582→	return sm, tea.Batch(cmds...)
   583→}

   619→// Helper methods
   620→
   621→// handleKeyMsg processes key messages for the system monitor
   622→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) handleKeyMsg(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
   623→	if sm.showKillDialog {
   624→		switch msg.String() {
   625→		case "y", "Y":
   626→			sm.killProcess(sm.killPID)
   627→			sm.showKillDialog = false
   628→			return sm.fetchProcessesCmd()
   629→		case "n", "N", "esc":
   630→			sm.showKillDialog = false
   631→			return nil
   632→		}
   633→		return nil
   634→	}
   635→
   636→	if sm.searchMode {
   637→		switch msg.String() {
   638→		case "esc":
   639→			sm.searchMode = false
   640→			sm.searchQuery = ""
   641→			sm.filteredProcs = sm.processes
   642→			return nil
   643→		case "enter":
   644→			sm.searchMode = false
   645→			return nil
   646→		case "backspace":
   647→			if len(sm.searchQuery) > 0 {
   648→				sm.searchQuery = sm.searchQuery[:len(sm.searchQuery)-1]
   649→				sm.filteredProcs = sm.filterProcesses(sm.processes, sm.searchQuery)
   650→			}
   651→			return nil
   652→		default:
   653→			if len(msg.String()) == 1 {
   654→				sm.searchQuery += msg.String()
   655→				sm.filteredProcs = sm.filterProcesses(sm.processes, sm.searchQuery)
   656→			}
   657→			return nil
   658→		}
   659→	}
   660→
   661→	switch msg.String() {
   662→	case "k":
   663→		if len(sm.filteredProcs) > 0 && sm.selectedRow < len(sm.filteredProcs) {
   664→			sm.killPID = sm.filteredProcs[sm.selectedRow].PID
   665→			sm.showKillDialog = true
   666→		}
   667→		return nil
   668→
   669→	case "/":
   670→		sm.searchMode = true
   671→		sm.searchQuery = ""
   672→		return nil
   673→
   674→	case "up", "j":
   675→		if sm.selectedRow > 0 {
   676→			sm.selectedRow--
   677→			if sm.selectedRow < sm.scrollOffset {
   678→				sm.scrollOffset = sm.selectedRow
   679→			}
   680→		}
   681→		return nil
   682→
   683→	case "down":
   684→		if sm.selectedRow < len(sm.filteredProcs)-1 {
   685→			sm.selectedRow++
   686→			visibleRows := sm.height - 15
   687→			if sm.selectedRow >= sm.scrollOffset+visibleRows {
   688→				sm.scrollOffset = sm.selectedRow - visibleRows + 1
   689→			}
   690→		}
   691→		return nil
   692→
   693→	case "c":
   694→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByCPU)
   695→		sm.sortProcesses()
   696→		return nil
   697→
   698→	case "m":
   699→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByMemory)
   700→		sm.sortProcesses()
   701→		return nil
   702→
   703→	case "p":
   704→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByPID)
   705→		sm.sortProcesses()
   706→		return nil
   707→
   708→	case "n":
   709→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByName)
   710→		sm.sortProcesses()
   711→		return nil
   712→
   713→	case "u":
   714→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByUser)
   715→		sm.sortProcesses()
   716→		return nil
   717→
   718→	case "s":
   719→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByState)
   720→		sm.sortProcesses()
   721→		return nil
   722→
   723→	case "t":
   724→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByThreads)
   725→		sm.sortProcesses()
   726→		return nil
   727→
   728→	case "r":
   729→		sm.sortColumn = string(SortByRuntime)
   730→		sm.sortProcesses()
   731→		return nil
   732→
   733→	case "R":
   734→		// Toggle sort direction
   735→		sm.sortReverse = !sm.sortReverse
   736→		sm.sortProcesses()
   737→		return nil
   738→
   739→	case "A":
   740→		// Show alert management interface
   741→		return sm.showAlertManagement()
   742→
   743→	case "T":
   744→		// Show threshold configuration
   745→		return sm.showThresholdConfig()
   746→
   747→	case "H":
   748→		// Show alert history
   749→		return sm.showAlertHistory()
   750→	}
   751→
   752→	return nil
   753→}
   754→
   755→// Data fetching commands
   756→

   800→// Data update methods
   801→
   802→// updateProcessData updates process and system stats data
   803→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) updateProcessData(msg processDataMsg) {
   804→	sm.processes = msg.processes
   805→	sm.systemStats = msg.systemStats
   806→
   807→	// Update historical data using the history manager
   808→	sm.historyManager.UpdateSystemMetrics(sm.systemStats, sm.processes, sm.connections, sm.interfaces)
   809→
   810→	// Update legacy histories for backward compatibility
   811→	for i, usage := range msg.systemStats.CPUUsage {
   812→		if i < len(sm.cpuHistory) {
   813→			sm.cpuHistory[i] = append(sm.cpuHistory[i][1:], usage)
   814→		}
   815→	}
   816→
   817→	memUsagePercent := float64(msg.systemStats.MemUsed) / float64(msg.systemStats.MemTotal) * 100
   818→	sm.memHistory = append(sm.memHistory[1:], memUsagePercent)
   819→
   820→	sm.sortProcesses()
   821→	sm.filteredProcs = sm.filterProcesses(sm.processes, sm.searchQuery)
   822→
   823→	if sm.selectedRow >= len(sm.filteredProcs) {
   824→		sm.selectedRow = max(0, len(sm.filteredProcs)-1)
   825→	}
   826→}
   827→
   828→// updateNetworkData updates network connection and interface data
   829→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) updateNetworkData(msg networkDataMsg) {
   830→	sm.connections = msg.connections
   831→	sm.interfaces = msg.interfaces
   832→
   833→	// Update network traffic history
   834→	totalTraffic := 0.0
   835→	for _, iface := range sm.interfaces {
   836→		totalTraffic += float64(iface.BytesIn+iface.BytesOut) / 1024 / 1024 // MB
   837→	}
   838→	sm.networkTraffic = append(sm.networkTraffic[1:], totalTraffic)
   839→}

   846→// Visual effects methods
   847→
   848→// updateVisualEffects updates the visual effects state
   849→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) updateVisualEffects() {
   850→	sm.frame++
   851→	sm.pulsePhase += 0.05
   852→	sm.glowIntensity = 0.3 + 0.2*math.Sin(sm.pulsePhase)
   853→
   854→	// Update orbs
   855→	for i := range sm.orbs {
   856→		orb := &sm.orbs[i]
   857→		orb.x += orb.vx
   858→		orb.y += orb.vy
   859→
   860→		// Gentle bouncing
   861→		if orb.x <= 0 || orb.x >= float64(sm.width) {
   862→			orb.vx *= -0.8
   863→			orb.x = math.Max(0, math.Min(float64(sm.width), orb.x))
   864→		}
   865→		if orb.y <= 0 || orb.y >= float64(sm.height) {
   866→			orb.vy *= -0.8
   867→			orb.y = math.Max(0, math.Min(float64(sm.height), orb.y))
   868→		}
   869→
   870→		orb.intensity = 0.3 + 0.5*math.Sin(float64(sm.frame)*0.02*orb.pulsePeriod+orb.pulseOffset)
   871→	}
   872→
   873→	// Update light rays
   874→	for i := range sm.lightRays {
   875→		ray := &sm.lightRays[i]
   876→		ray.angle += 0.01
   877→		ray.intensity = 0.2 + 0.4*math.Sin(float64(sm.frame)*0.03+float64(i))
   878→	}
   879→
   880→	// Update matrix rain
   881→	for i := range sm.matrixRain {
   882→		drop := &sm.matrixRain[i]
   883→		drop.y += drop.speed
   884→		if drop.y > float64(sm.height) {
   885→			drop.y = -float64(drop.length)
   886→			drop.x = float64(sm.width * int(drop.x) / sm.width) // Keep within bounds
   887→		}
   888→	}
   889→
   890→	// Update energy field
   891→	for i := range sm.energyField {
   892→		node := &sm.energyField[i]
   893→		node.pulsePhase += 0.02
   894→		node.energy = 0.4 + 0.4*math.Sin(node.pulsePhase)
   895→	}
   896→}

   898→// initializeVisualEffects sets up the visual effects
   899→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) initializeVisualEffects() {
   900→	// Initialize orbs with elegant colors
   901→	colors := []lipgloss.Color{
   902→		lipgloss.Color("#6366f1"), // primary
   903→		lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6"), // secondary
   904→		lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4"), // accent
   905→		lipgloss.Color("#10b981"), // success
   906→		lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"), // warning
   907→	}
   908→
   909→	for i := range sm.orbs {
   910→		sm.orbs[i] = orb{
   911→			x:           float64(i * 15), // Spread them out initially
   912→			y:           float64(i * 5),
   913→			vx:          (float64(i%2)*2 - 1) * 0.2,
   914→			vy:          (float64(i%3)*2 - 1) * 0.15,
   915→			radius:      2.0 + float64(i%3),
   916→			intensity:   0.5,
   917→			color:       colors[i%len(colors)],
   918→			pulsePeriod: 2.0 + float64(i%4),
   919→			pulseOffset: float64(i) * 1.57, // π/2 offset
   920→		}
   921→	}
   922→
   923→	// Initialize other effects similarly...
   924→	// (Simplified for brevity - full implementation would include all effects)
   925→}

   932→	return b

   934→

   943→// Rendering methods for historical data visualization
   944→
   945→// renderHistoricalOverview renders a compact overview of historical metrics with sparklines
   946→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderHistoricalOverview() string {
   947→	var s strings.Builder
   948→
   949→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   950→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   951→		Bold(true).
   952→		Render("📊 HISTORICAL METRICS")
   953→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
   954→
   955→	// Key metrics to display
   956→	metrics := []struct {
   957→		name  string
   958→		label string
   959→		unit  string
   960→		color lipgloss.Color
   961→	}{
   962→		{"cpu_usage", "CPU Usage", "%", lipgloss.Color("#ef4444")},
   963→		{"memory_usage", "Memory Usage", "%", lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")},
   964→		{"load_average", "Load Average", "", lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")},
   965→		{"process_count", "Process Count", "", lipgloss.Color("#10b981")},
   966→		{"connection_count", "Network Connections", "", lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")},
   967→	}
   968→
   969→	for _, metric := range metrics {
   970→		history := sm.historyManager.GetMetricHistory(metric.name)
   971→		if history == nil || len(history.DataPoints) == 0 {
   972→			continue
   973→		}
   974→
   975→		// Get latest value
   976→		latest := history.DataPoints[len(history.DataPoints)-1]
   977→
   978→		// Create sparkline
   979→		sparkline := sm.renderSparkline(metric.name, 30)
   980→
   981→		// Format the metric line
   982→		labelStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   983→			Foreground(metric.color).
   984→			Bold(true).
   985→			Width(18)
   986→
   987→		valueStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   988→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
   989→			Width(10)
   990→
   991→		sparklineStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   992→			Foreground(metric.color)
   993→
   994→		line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s %s",
   995→			labelStyle.Render(metric.label+":"),
   996→			valueStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%.1f%s", latest.Value, metric.unit)),
   997→			sparklineStyle.Render(sparkline),
   998→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Render(
   999→				fmt.Sprintf("(%.1f-%.1f)", history.MinValue, history.MaxValue)))
  1000→
  1001→		s.WriteString(line + "\n")
  1002→	}
  1003→
  1004→	return s.String()
  1005→}
  1006→
  1007→// renderSparkline creates a sparkline chart for a metric
  1008→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderSparkline(metricName string, width int) string {
  1009→	history := sm.historyManager.GetMetricHistory(metricName)
  1010→	if history == nil || len(history.DataPoints) == 0 {
  1011→		return strings.Repeat("▁", width)
  1012→	}
  1013→
  1014→	// Sample data points to fit the width
  1015→	dataPoints := sm.sampleDataPoints(history.DataPoints, width)
  1016→
  1017→	// Sparkline characters from lowest to highest
  1018→	sparkChars := []string{"▁", "▂", "▃", "▄", "▅", "▆", "▇", "█"}
  1019→
  1020→	var sparkline strings.Builder
  1021→
  1022→	// Calculate range for normalization
  1023→	minVal := history.MinValue
  1024→	maxVal := history.MaxValue
  1025→	if minVal == maxVal {
  1026→		maxVal = minVal + 1 // Avoid division by zero
  1027→	}
  1028→
  1029→	for _, point := range dataPoints {
  1030→		// Normalize value to 0-7 range for sparkline characters
  1031→		normalized := (point.Value - minVal) / (maxVal - minVal)
  1032→		index := int(normalized * float64(len(sparkChars)-1))
  1033→		if index < 0 {
  1034→			index = 0
  1035→		}
  1036→		if index >= len(sparkChars) {
  1037→			index = len(sparkChars) - 1
  1038→		}
  1039→		sparkline.WriteString(sparkChars[index])
  1040→	}
  1041→
  1042→	return sparkline.String()
  1043→}
  1044→
  1045→// sampleDataPoints samples data points to fit a target width
  1046→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) sampleDataPoints(dataPoints []HistoricalDataPoint, targetWidth int) []HistoricalDataPoint {
  1047→	if len(dataPoints) <= targetWidth {
  1048→		return dataPoints
  1049→	}
  1050→
  1051→	sampled := make([]HistoricalDataPoint, targetWidth)
  1052→	step := float64(len(dataPoints)) / float64(targetWidth)
  1053→
  1054→	for i := 0; i < targetWidth; i++ {
  1055→		index := int(float64(i) * step)
  1056→		if index >= len(dataPoints) {
  1057→			index = len(dataPoints) - 1
  1058→		}
  1059→		sampled[i] = dataPoints[index]
  1060→	}
  1061→
  1062→	return sampled
  1063→}
  1064→
  1065→// renderASCIIChart renders a full ASCII chart for a specific metric
  1066→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderASCIIChart(metricName string, width, height int) string {
  1067→	history := sm.historyManager.GetMetricHistory(metricName)
  1068→	if history == nil || len(history.DataPoints) == 0 {
  1069→		return sm.renderNoDataChart(metricName, width, height)
  1070→	}
  1071→
  1072→	return sm.createASCIIChart(history, width, height)
  1073→}
  1074→
  1075→// renderNoDataChart renders a chart when no data is available
  1076→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderNoDataChart(metricName string, width, height int) string {
  1077→	var s strings.Builder
  1078→
  1079→	title := strings.Title(strings.ReplaceAll(metricName, "_", " "))
  1080→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1081→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
  1082→		Bold(true).
  1083→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1084→		Width(width)
  1085→
  1086→	s.WriteString(titleStyle.Render(title) + "\n")
  1087→	s.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", width) + "\n")
  1088→
  1089→	// Center the "No data available" message
  1090→	noDataMsg := "No data available"
  1091→	padding := (width - len(noDataMsg)) / 2
  1092→	if padding < 0 {
  1093→		padding = 0
  1094→	}
  1095→
  1096→	for i := 0; i < height-4; i++ {
  1097→		if i == height/2-2 {
  1098→			s.WriteString(strings.Repeat(" ", padding) + noDataMsg + "\n")
  1099→		} else {
  1100→			s.WriteString("\n")
  1101→		}
  1102→	}
  1103→
  1104→	s.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", width))
  1105→
  1106→	return s.String()
  1107→}
  1108→
  1109→// createASCIIChart creates a detailed ASCII chart from historical data
  1110→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) createASCIIChart(history *MetricHistory, width, height int) string {
  1111→	var s strings.Builder
  1112→
  1113→	// Title
  1114→	title := strings.Title(strings.ReplaceAll(history.Name, "_", " "))
  1115→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1116→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
  1117→		Bold(true).
  1118→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1119→		Width(width)
  1120→
  1121→	s.WriteString(titleStyle.Render(title) + "\n")
  1122→
  1123→	// Chart area dimensions (leave space for title, axes, and labels)
  1124→	chartWidth := width - 10  // Leave space for Y-axis labels
  1125→	chartHeight := height - 6 // Leave space for title and X-axis
  1126→
  1127→	if chartWidth < 10 || chartHeight < 3 {
  1128→		return sm.renderNoDataChart(history.Name, width, height)
  1129→	}
  1130→
  1131→	// Sample data to fit chart width
  1132→	dataPoints := sm.sampleDataPoints(history.DataPoints, chartWidth)
  1133→
  1134→	// Calculate value range
  1135→	minVal := history.MinValue
  1136→	maxVal := history.MaxValue
  1137→	if minVal == maxVal {
  1138→		maxVal = minVal + 1
  1139→	}
  1140→
  1141→	// Create chart grid
  1142→	chart := make([][]string, chartHeight)
  1143→	for i := range chart {
  1144→		chart[i] = make([]string, chartWidth)
  1145→		for j := range chart[i] {
  1146→			chart[i][j] = " "
  1147→		}
  1148→	}
  1149→
  1150→	// Plot data points
  1151→	for i, point := range dataPoints {
  1152→		if i >= chartWidth {
  1153→			break
  1154→		}
  1155→
  1156→		// Normalize value to chart height
  1157→		normalized := (point.Value - minVal) / (maxVal - minVal)
  1158→		y := int(float64(chartHeight-1) * (1.0 - normalized)) // Invert Y axis
  1159→
  1160→		if y < 0 {
  1161→			y = 0
  1162→		}
  1163→		if y >= chartHeight {
  1164→			y = chartHeight - 1
  1165→		}
  1166→
  1167→		// Use different characters for different intensities
  1168→		chart[y][i] = "█"
  1169→
  1170→		// Add connecting line to previous point if possible
  1171→		if i > 0 {
  1172→			prevNormalized := (dataPoints[i-1].Value - minVal) / (maxVal - minVal)
  1173→			prevY := int(float64(chartHeight-1) * (1.0 - prevNormalized))
  1174→
  1175→			if prevY < 0 {
  1176→				prevY = 0
  1177→			}
  1178→			if prevY >= chartHeight {
  1179→				prevY = chartHeight - 1
  1180→			}
  1181→
  1182→			// Draw connecting line
  1183→			startY := prevY
  1184→			endY := y
  1185→			if startY > endY {
  1186→				startY, endY = endY, startY
  1187→			}
  1188→
  1189→			for lineY := startY; lineY <= endY; lineY++ {
  1190→				if chart[lineY][i-1] == " " {
  1191→					if lineY == startY || lineY == endY {
  1192→						chart[lineY][i-1] = "▆"
  1193→					} else {
  1194→						chart[lineY][i-1] = "▄"
  1195→					}
  1196→				}
  1197→			}
  1198→		}
  1199→	}
  1200→
  1201→	// Render the chart
  1202→	s.WriteString("┌" + strings.Repeat("─", chartWidth) + "┐\n")
  1203→
  1204→	for i, row := range chart {
  1205→		// Y-axis label
  1206→		progress := float64(i) / float64(chartHeight-1)
  1207→		value := maxVal - (progress * (maxVal - minVal))
  1208→		label := fmt.Sprintf("%6.1f", value)
  1209→
  1210→		s.WriteString(label + "│")
  1211→		for _, cell := range row {
  1212→			s.WriteString(cell)
  1213→		}
  1214→		s.WriteString("│\n")
  1215→	}
  1216→
  1217→	s.WriteString("└" + strings.Repeat("─", chartWidth) + "┘\n")
  1218→
  1219→	// X-axis labels (time)
  1220→	if len(dataPoints) > 0 {
  1221→		firstTime := dataPoints[0].Timestamp.Format("15:04")
  1222→		lastTime := dataPoints[len(dataPoints)-1].Timestamp.Format("15:04")
  1223→
  1224→		timeLabel := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s%s",
  1225→			firstTime,
  1226→			strings.Repeat(" ", chartWidth-len(firstTime)-len(lastTime)),
  1227→			lastTime)
  1228→
  1229→		s.WriteString("        " + timeLabel + "\n")
  1230→	}
  1231→
  1232→	// Chart info
  1233→	infoStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1234→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).
  1235→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1236→		Width(width)
  1237→
  1238→	info := fmt.Sprintf("Range: %.1f - %.1f %s | Points: %d | Updated: %s",
  1239→		history.MinValue, history.MaxValue, history.Unit,
  1240→		len(history.DataPoints), history.LastUpdated.Format("15:04:05"))
  1241→
  1242→	s.WriteString(infoStyle.Render(info))
  1243→
  1244→	return s.String()
  1245→}
  1246→
  1247→// renderHistoricalDataView renders the full historical data view
  1248→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderHistoricalDataView() string {
  1249→	if sm.width < 80 || sm.height < 20 {
  1250→		return sm.renderHistoricalOverview()
  1251→	}
  1252→
  1253→	var s strings.Builder
  1254→
  1255→	// Title
  1256→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1257→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
  1258→		Bold(true).
  1259→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1260→		Width(sm.width).
  1261→		Render("📊 HISTORICAL DATA VISUALIZATION")
  1262→
  1263→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  1264→
  1265→	// Split screen into multiple charts
  1266→	chartWidth := (sm.width - 6) / 2
  1267→	chartHeight := (sm.height - 8) / 2
  1268→
  1269→	if chartWidth < 30 || chartHeight < 8 {
  1270→		return sm.renderHistoricalOverview()
  1271→	}
  1272→
  1273→	// Top row: CPU and Memory
  1274→	cpuChart := sm.renderASCIIChart("cpu_usage", chartWidth, chartHeight)
  1275→	memChart := sm.renderASCIIChart("memory_usage", chartWidth, chartHeight)
  1276→
  1277→	topRow := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, cpuChart, "  ", memChart)
  1278→	s.WriteString(topRow + "\n")
  1279→
  1280→	// Bottom row: Load Average and Process Count
  1281→	loadChart := sm.renderASCIIChart("load_average", chartWidth, chartHeight)
  1282→	procChart := sm.renderASCIIChart("process_count", chartWidth, chartHeight)
  1283→
  1284→	bottomRow := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, loadChart, "  ", procChart)
  1285→	s.WriteString(bottomRow)
  1286→
  1287→	return s.String()
  1288→}

  1309→// renderSystemPanel renders the current system status panel
  1310→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderSystemPanel() string {
  1311→	var s strings.Builder
  1312→
  1313→	// Title with icon
  1314→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1315→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
  1316→		Bold(true).
  1317→		Render("⬢ SYSTEM STATUS")
  1318→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  1319→
  1320→	// CPU information
  1321→	if len(sm.systemStats.CPUUsage) > 0 {
  1322→		avgCPU := 0.0
  1323→		for _, usage := range sm.systemStats.CPUUsage {
  1324→			avgCPU += usage
  1325→		}
  1326→		avgCPU /= float64(len(sm.systemStats.CPUUsage))
  1327→
  1328→		cpuLabel := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).Render("CPU Usage:")
  1329→		cpuBar := sm.renderElegantBar(avgCPU, 100, 20, lipgloss.Color("#10b981"), lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
  1330→		cpuText := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Render(fmt.Sprintf("%.1f%%", avgCPU))
  1331→
  1332→		s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s %s\n\n", cpuLabel, cpuBar, cpuText))
  1333→	}
  1334→
  1335→	// Memory information
  1336→	if sm.systemStats.MemTotal > 0 {
  1337→		memUsage := float64(sm.systemStats.MemUsed) / float64(sm.systemStats.MemTotal) * 100
  1338→
  1339→		memLabel := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).Render("Memory Usage:")
  1340→		memBar := sm.renderElegantBar(memUsage, 100, 20, lipgloss.Color("#10b981"), lipgloss.Color("#ef4444"))
  1341→		memText := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Render(fmt.Sprintf("%.1f%% (%s/%s)",
  1342→			memUsage, sm.formatBytes(sm.systemStats.MemUsed), sm.formatBytes(sm.systemStats.MemTotal)))
  1343→
  1344→		s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s\n%s\n\n", memLabel, memBar, memText))
  1345→	}
  1346→
  1347→	return s.String()
  1348→}
  1349→
  1350→// renderProcessList renders an abbreviated process list
  1351→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderProcessList() string {
  1352→	var s strings.Builder
  1353→
  1354→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1355→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).
  1356→		Bold(true).
  1357→		Render("⚡ TOP PROCESSES")
  1358→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  1359→
  1360→	// Show top 10 processes by CPU usage
  1361→	maxProcs := min(len(sm.filteredProcs), 10)
  1362→	for i := 0; i < maxProcs; i++ {
  1363→		proc := sm.filteredProcs[i]
  1364→
  1365→		line := fmt.Sprintf("%-8d %-12s %6.1f%% %6.1f%% %s",
  1366→			proc.PID, proc.User, proc.CPU, proc.Memory, proc.Name)
  1367→
  1368→		if len(line) > sm.width-4 {
  1369→			line = line[:sm.width-7] + "..."
  1370→		}
  1371→
  1372→		s.WriteString(line + "\n")
  1373→	}
  1374→
  1375→	return s.String()
  1376→}
  1377→
  1378→// renderElegantBar renders a progress bar with gradient colors
  1379→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderElegantBar(value, max float64, width int, lowColor, highColor lipgloss.Color) string {
  1380→	if max == 0 {
  1381→		return ""
  1382→	}
  1383→
  1384→	ratio := value / max
  1385→	filled := int(ratio * float64(width))
  1386→	if filled > width {
  1387→		filled = width
  1388→	}
  1389→
  1390→	// Create gradient effect
  1391→	var segments []string
  1392→	for i := 0; i < width; i++ {
  1393→		if i < filled {
  1394→			if ratio > 0.7 {
  1395→				segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(highColor).Render("█"))
  1396→			} else if ratio > 0.4 {
  1397→				segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")).Render("█"))
  1398→			} else {
  1399→				segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lowColor).Render("█"))
  1400→			}
  1401→		} else {
  1402→			segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).Render("░"))
  1403→		}
  1404→	}
  1405→
  1406→	return strings.Join(segments, "")
  1407→}

  1409→// formatBytes formats byte values in human-readable format
  1410→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) formatBytes(bytes uint64) string {
  1411→	const unit = 1024
  1412→	if bytes < unit {
  1413→		return fmt.Sprintf("%d B", bytes)
  1414→	}
  1415→	div, exp := int64(unit), 0
  1416→	for n := bytes / unit; n >= unit; n /= unit {
  1417→		div *= unit
  1418→		exp++
  1419→	}
  1420→	return fmt.Sprintf("%.1f %cB", float64(bytes)/float64(div), "KMGTPE"[exp])
  1421→}

  1427→	return content

  1432→// getProcessesAndStats retrieves current process list and system statistics
  1433→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getProcessesAndStats() ([]Process, SystemStats) {
  1434→	processes := sm.getProcesses()
  1435→	stats := sm.getSystemStats()
  1436→	return processes, stats
