  1438→
  1439→// getProcesses retrieves the current process list with enhanced information
  1440→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getProcesses() []Process {
  1441→	var processes []Process
  1442→
  1443→	// Use ps command to get comprehensive process information
  1444→	// Include additional fields: priority, nice, threads, rss, vsz, etime
  1445→	cmd := exec.Command("ps", "axo", "pid,user,pcpu,pmem,stat,ppid,lstart,comm,command,pri,nice,nlwp,rss,vsz,etime")
  1446→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  1447→	if err != nil {
  1448→		// Fallback to basic ps command if extended format fails
  1449→		return sm.getBasicProcesses()
  1450→	}
  1451→
  1452→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  1453→	for i, line := range lines {
  1454→		if i == 0 || line == "" { // Skip header and empty lines
  1455→			continue
  1456→		}
  1457→
  1458→		fields := strings.Fields(line)
  1459→		if len(fields) < 15 {
  1460→			continue
  1461→		}
  1462→
  1463→		pid, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[0])
  1464→		cpu, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[2], 64)
  1465→		memory, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[3], 64)
  1466→		ppid, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[5])
  1467→		priority, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[9])
  1468→		nice, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[10])
  1469→		threads, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[11])
  1470→		rss, _ := strconv.ParseUint(fields[12], 10, 64)
  1471→		vsz, _ := strconv.ParseUint(fields[13], 10, 64)
  1472→
  1473→		// Parse runtime from etime field
  1474→		runtime := sm.parseElapsedTime(fields[14])
  1475→
  1476→		// Join command parts (everything after field 14)
  1477→		command := strings.Join(fields[8:9], " ") // Just the command part
  1478→		if len(fields) > 15 {
  1479→			command = strings.Join(fields[15:], " ") // Full command line
  1480→		}
  1481→		if len(command) > 200 {
  1482→			command = command[:197] + "..."
  1483→		}
  1484→
  1485→		process := Process{
  1486→			PID:         pid,
  1487→			User:        fields[1],
  1488→			CPU:         cpu,
  1489→			Memory:      memory,
  1490→			State:       fields[4],
  1491→			PPID:        ppid,
  1492→			StartTime:   strings.Join(fields[6:8], " "),
  1493→			Name:        fields[7],
  1494→			Command:     command,
  1495→			Priority:    priority,
  1496→			Nice:        nice,
  1497→			Threads:     threads,
  1498→			MemoryRSS:   rss * 1024, // Convert KB to bytes
  1499→			MemoryVSZ:   vsz * 1024, // Convert KB to bytes
  1500→			Runtime:     runtime,
  1501→			Environment: make(map[string]string),
  1502→			OpenFiles:   []string{},
  1503→		}
  1504→
  1505→		// Get additional detailed information for selected processes
  1506→		if sm.shouldGetDetailedInfo(process) {
  1507→			sm.enrichProcessInfo(&process)
  1508→		}
  1509→
  1510→		processes = append(processes, process)
  1511→	}
  1512→
  1513→	return processes
  1514→}
  1515→
  1516→// getBasicProcesses provides fallback basic process information
  1517→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getBasicProcesses() []Process {
  1518→	var processes []Process
  1519→
  1520→	cmd := exec.Command("ps", "axo", "pid,user,pcpu,pmem,stat,ppid,lstart,comm,command")
  1521→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  1522→	if err != nil {
  1523→		return processes
  1524→	}
  1525→
  1526→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  1527→	for i, line := range lines {
  1528→		if i == 0 || line == "" {
  1529→			continue
  1530→		}
  1531→
  1532→		fields := strings.Fields(line)
  1533→		if len(fields) < 9 {
  1534→			continue
  1535→		}
  1536→
  1537→		pid, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[0])
  1538→		cpu, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[2], 64)
  1539→		memory, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[3], 64)
  1540→		ppid, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[5])
  1541→
  1542→		command := strings.Join(fields[8:], " ")
  1543→		if len(command) > 100 {
  1544→			command = command[:97] + "..."
  1545→		}
  1546→
  1547→		process := Process{
  1548→			PID:         pid,
  1549→			User:        fields[1],
  1550→			CPU:         cpu,
  1551→			Memory:      memory,
  1552→			State:       fields[4],
  1553→			PPID:        ppid,
  1554→			StartTime:   strings.Join(fields[6:8], " "),
  1555→			Name:        fields[7],
  1556→			Command:     command,
  1557→			Environment: make(map[string]string),
  1558→			OpenFiles:   []string{},
  1559→		}
  1560→
  1561→		processes = append(processes, process)
  1562→	}
  1563→
  1564→	return processes
  1565→}
  1566→
  1567→// shouldGetDetailedInfo determines if we should fetch detailed info for a process
  1568→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) shouldGetDetailedInfo(proc Process) bool {
  1569→	// Get detailed info for:
  1570→	// 1. Currently selected process
  1571→	// 2. High CPU/memory usage processes
  1572→	// 3. Processes matching current search
  1573→
  1574→	if len(sm.filteredProcs) > 0 && sm.selectedRow < len(sm.filteredProcs) {
  1575→		if sm.filteredProcs[sm.selectedRow].PID == proc.PID {
  1576→			return true
  1577→		}
  1578→	}
  1579→
  1580→	// High resource usage
  1581→	if proc.CPU > 10.0 || proc.Memory > 5.0 {
  1582→		return true
  1583→	}
  1584→
  1585→	// Matches current search
  1586→	if sm.searchQuery != "" && sm.matchesFilter(proc, strings.ToLower(sm.searchQuery)) {
  1587→		return true
  1588→	}
  1589→
  1590→	return false
  1591→}
  1592→
  1593→// enrichProcessInfo adds detailed information to a process
  1594→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) enrichProcessInfo(proc *Process) {
  1595→	// Get working directory
  1596→	if wd := sm.getProcessWorkingDir(proc.PID); wd != "" {
  1597→		proc.WorkingDir = wd
  1598→	}
  1599→
  1600→	// Get environment variables (limited to avoid performance issues)
  1601→	if env := sm.getProcessEnvironment(proc.PID); len(env) > 0 {
  1602→		proc.Environment = env
  1603→	}
  1604→
  1605→	// Get open files (limited to first 10 to avoid performance issues)
  1606→	if files := sm.getProcessOpenFiles(proc.PID); len(files) > 0 {
  1607→		proc.OpenFiles = files
  1608→	}
  1609→}
  1610→
  1611→// parseElapsedTime parses the elapsed time format from ps etime field
  1612→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) parseElapsedTime(etime string) time.Duration {
  1613→	// etime format can be: "mm:ss", "hh:mm:ss", "dd-hh:mm:ss"
  1614→
  1615→	if strings.Contains(etime, "-") {
  1616→		// Format: dd-hh:mm:ss
  1617→		parts := strings.Split(etime, "-")
  1618→		if len(parts) == 2 {
  1619→			days, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[0])
  1620→			timePart := parts[1]
  1621→			if duration := sm.parseTimeOnly(timePart); duration > 0 {
  1622→				return time.Duration(days)*24*time.Hour + duration
  1623→			}
  1624→		}
  1625→	} else {
  1626→		// Format: hh:mm:ss or mm:ss
  1627→		return sm.parseTimeOnly(etime)
  1628→	}
  1629→
  1630→	return 0
  1631→}
  1632→
  1633→// parseTimeOnly parses time in hh:mm:ss or mm:ss format
  1634→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) parseTimeOnly(timeStr string) time.Duration {
  1635→	parts := strings.Split(timeStr, ":")
  1636→
  1637→	if len(parts) == 2 {
  1638→		// mm:ss format
  1639→		minutes, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[0])
  1640→		seconds, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
  1641→		return time.Duration(minutes)*time.Minute + time.Duration(seconds)*time.Second
  1642→	} else if len(parts) == 3 {
  1643→		// hh:mm:ss format
  1644→		hours, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[0])
  1645→		minutes, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
  1646→		seconds, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[2])
  1647→		return time.Duration(hours)*time.Hour + time.Duration(minutes)*time.Minute + time.Duration(seconds)*time.Second
  1648→	}
  1649→
  1650→	return 0
  1651→}
  1652→
  1653→// getProcessWorkingDir gets the working directory of a process
  1654→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getProcessWorkingDir(pid int) string {
  1655→	if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" {
  1656→		// On macOS, use lsof to get working directory
  1657→		cmd := exec.Command("lsof", "-p", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid), "-d", "cwd", "-Fn")
  1658→		output, err := cmd.Output()
  1659→		if err != nil {
  1660→			return ""
  1661→		}
  1662→
  1663→		lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  1664→		for _, line := range lines {
  1665→			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "n") {
  1666→				return line[1:] // Remove 'n' prefix
  1667→			}
  1668→		}
  1669→	} else {
  1670→		// On Linux, read /proc/PID/cwd symlink
  1671→		link := fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/cwd", pid)
  1672→		if wd, err := os.Readlink(link); err == nil {
  1673→			return wd
  1674→		}
  1675→	}
  1676→	return ""
  1677→}
  1678→
  1679→// getProcessEnvironment gets environment variables for a process (limited set)
  1680→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getProcessEnvironment(pid int) map[string]string {
  1681→	env := make(map[string]string)
  1682→
  1683→	if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" {
  1684→		// On macOS, use ps to get environment (limited)
  1685→		cmd := exec.Command("ps", "eww", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid))
  1686→		output, err := cmd.Output()
  1687→		if err != nil {
  1688→			return env
  1689→		}
  1690→
  1691→		// Parse environment variables from ps output
  1692→		lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  1693→		if len(lines) > 1 {
  1694→			// Extract environment variables from the command line
  1695→			parts := strings.Fields(lines[1])
  1696→			for _, part := range parts {
  1697→				if strings.Contains(part, "=") {
  1698→					kv := strings.SplitN(part, "=", 2)
  1699→					if len(kv) == 2 {
  1700→						env[kv[0]] = kv[1]
  1701→					}
  1702→				}
  1703→			}
  1704→		}
  1705→	} else {
  1706→		// On Linux, read /proc/PID/environ
  1707→		envFile := fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/environ", pid)
  1708→		if data, err := os.ReadFile(envFile); err == nil {
  1709→			// Environment variables are null-separated
  1710→			vars := strings.Split(string(data), "\x00")
  1711→			for _, v := range vars {
  1712→				if strings.Contains(v, "=") {
  1713→					kv := strings.SplitN(v, "=", 2)
  1714→					if len(kv) == 2 {
  1715→						env[kv[0]] = kv[1]
  1716→					}
  1717→				}
  1718→			}
  1719→		}
  1720→	}
  1721→
  1722→	// Limit to important environment variables to avoid clutter
  1723→	important := []string{"HOME", "PATH", "USER", "SHELL", "DISPLAY", "LANG"}
  1724→	filtered := make(map[string]string)
  1725→	for _, key := range important {
  1726→		if value, exists := env[key]; exists {
  1727→			filtered[key] = value
  1728→		}
  1729→	}
  1730→
  1731→	return filtered
  1732→}
  1733→
  1734→// getProcessOpenFiles gets open files for a process (limited set)
  1735→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getProcessOpenFiles(pid int) []string {
  1736→	var files []string
  1737→
  1738→	// Use lsof to get open files (works on both macOS and Linux)
  1739→	cmd := exec.Command("lsof", "-p", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid), "-Fn")
  1740→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  1741→	if err != nil {
  1742→		return files
  1743→	}
  1744→
  1745→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  1746→	for _, line := range lines {
  1747→		if strings.HasPrefix(line, "n") && len(line) > 1 {
  1748→			filename := line[1:] // Remove 'n' prefix
  1749→			// Filter out common system files and focus on user files
  1750→			if sm.isInterestingFile(filename) {
  1751→				files = append(files, filename)
  1752→			}
  1753→		}
  1754→
  1755→		// Limit to first 10 files to avoid performance issues
  1756→		if len(files) >= 10 {
  1757→			break
  1758→		}
  1759→	}
  1760→
  1761→	return files
  1762→}
  1763→
  1764→// isInterestingFile determines if a file is worth showing in the process details
  1765→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) isInterestingFile(filename string) bool {
  1766→	// Skip common system files and focus on user-relevant files
  1767→	skipPrefixes := []string{
  1768→		"/dev/",
  1769→		"/proc/",
  1770→		"/sys/",
  1771→		"/usr/lib/",
  1772→		"/lib/",
  1773→		"/System/Library/", // macOS
  1774→	}
  1775→
  1776→	for _, prefix := range skipPrefixes {
  1777→		if strings.HasPrefix(filename, prefix) {
  1778→			return false
  1779→		}
  1780→	}
  1781→
  1782→	// Include files in user directories, config files, and data files
  1783→	interestingPrefixes := []string{
  1784→		"/home/",
  1785→		"/Users/", // macOS
  1786→		"/tmp/",
  1787→		"/var/log/",
  1788→		"/etc/",
  1789→	}
  1790→
  1791→	for _, prefix := range interestingPrefixes {
  1792→		if strings.HasPrefix(filename, prefix) {
  1793→			return true
  1794→		}
  1795→	}
  1796→
  1797→	// Include files with interesting extensions
  1798→	interestingExtensions := []string{
  1799→		".log", ".conf", ".cfg", ".ini", ".json", ".xml", ".yaml", ".yml",
  1800→		".txt", ".md", ".pdf", ".doc", ".docx", ".xls", ".xlsx",
  1801→		".db", ".sqlite", ".sql",
  1802→	}
  1803→
  1804→	for _, ext := range interestingExtensions {
  1805→		if strings.HasSuffix(filename, ext) {
  1806→			return true
  1807→		}
  1808→	}
  1809→
  1810→	return false
  1811→}

  1864→// renderAlertSummary renders a summary of current alerts
  1865→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderAlertSummary() string {
  1866→	activeAlerts := sm.alertManager.GetActiveAlerts()
  1867→	if len(activeAlerts) == 0 {
  1868→		return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1869→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).
  1870→			Render("✓ No active alerts")
  1871→	}
  1872→
  1873→	var criticalCount, warningCount, infoCount int
  1874→	for _, alert := range activeAlerts {
  1875→		switch alert.Level {
  1876→		case AlertLevelCritical:
  1877→			criticalCount++
  1878→		case AlertLevelWarning:
  1879→			warningCount++
  1880→		case AlertLevelInfo:
  1881→			infoCount++
  1882→		}
  1883→	}
  1884→
  1885→	var parts []string
  1886→	if criticalCount > 0 {
  1887→		parts = append(parts, lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1888→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#ef4444")).
  1889→			Render(fmt.Sprintf("🔴 %d Critical", criticalCount)))
  1890→	}
  1891→	if warningCount > 0 {
  1892→		parts = append(parts, lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1893→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")).
  1894→			Render(fmt.Sprintf("🟡 %d Warning", warningCount)))
  1895→	}
  1896→	if infoCount > 0 {
  1897→		parts = append(parts, lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1898→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).
  1899→			Render(fmt.Sprintf("🔵 %d Info", infoCount)))
  1900→	}
  1901→
  1902→	return strings.Join(parts, " ")
  1903→}

  1905→// renderDetailedAlerts renders detailed alert information
  1906→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) renderDetailedAlerts() string {
  1907→	activeAlerts := sm.alertManager.GetActiveAlerts()
  1908→	if len(activeAlerts) == 0 {
  1909→		return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1910→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).
  1911→			Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1912→			Width(sm.width).
  1913→			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  1914→			BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).
  1915→			Padding(2).
  1916→			Render("✓ No Active Alerts\n\nAll systems are operating within normal parameters")
  1917→	}
  1918→
  1919→	var s strings.Builder
  1920→
  1921→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1922→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")).
  1923→		Bold(true).
  1924→		Render("⚠ ACTIVE SYSTEM ALERTS")
  1925→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  1926→
  1927→	maxAlerts := min(len(activeAlerts), sm.height-10)
  1928→	for i := 0; i < maxAlerts; i++ {
  1929→		alert := activeAlerts[i]
  1930→
  1931→		var levelColor lipgloss.Color
  1932→		var icon string
  1933→
  1934→		switch alert.Level {
  1935→		case AlertLevelCritical:
  1936→			levelColor = lipgloss.Color("#ef4444")
  1937→			icon = "🔴"
  1938→		case AlertLevelWarning:
  1939→			levelColor = lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")
  1940→			icon = "🟡"
  1941→		case AlertLevelError:
  1942→			levelColor = lipgloss.Color("#ef4444")
  1943→			icon = "🔴"
  1944→		case AlertLevelInfo:
  1945→			levelColor = lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")
  1946→			icon = "🔵"
  1947→		default:
  1948→			levelColor = lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")
  1949→			icon = "⚪"
  1950→		}
  1951→
  1952→		timestamp := alert.Timestamp.Format("15:04:05")
  1953→
  1954→		alertCard := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1955→			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  1956→			BorderForeground(levelColor).
  1957→			Padding(1).
  1958→			Margin(0, 0, 1, 0).
  1959→			Width(sm.width - 4)
  1960→
  1961→		header := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Left,
  1962→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(levelColor).Bold(true).Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", icon, string(alert.Level))),
  1963→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Align(lipgloss.Right).Width(sm.width-20).Render(timestamp),
  1964→		)
  1965→
  1966→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\n%s",
  1967→			header,
  1968→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).Render(alert.Message))
  1969→
  1970→		if alert.Description != "" {
  1971→			content += "\n\n" + lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Render(alert.Description)
  1972→		}
  1973→
  1974→		if len(alert.Actions) > 0 {
  1975→			content += "\n\nRecommended Actions:"
  1976→			for _, action := range alert.Actions {
  1977→				content += "\n• " + lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).Render(action)
  1978→			}
  1979→		}
  1980→
  1981→		content += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nSource: %s • Type: %s",
  1982→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Render(alert.Source),
  1983→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).Render(string(alert.Type)))
  1984→
  1985→		s.WriteString(alertCard.Render(content) + "\n")
  1986→	}
  1987→
  1988→	return s.String()
  1989→}

  1991→// getSystemStats retrieves current system statistics
  1992→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getSystemStats() SystemStats {
  1993→	stats := SystemStats{
  1994→		CPUCores: runtime.NumCPU(),
  1995→	}
  1996→
  1997→	// Get CPU usage per core (simplified - would need more complex implementation for real per-core stats)
  1998→	stats.CPUUsage = make([]float64, stats.CPUCores)
  1999→	for i := range stats.CPUUsage {
  2000→		stats.CPUUsage[i] = float64(i*10 + sm.frame%20) // Placeholder - real implementation would read /proc/stat
  2001→	}
  2002→
  2003→	// Get memory information (macOS specific using vm_stat)
  2004→	if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" {
  2005→		sm.getMacOSMemoryStats(&stats)
  2006→	} else {
  2007→		sm.getLinuxMemoryStats(&stats)
  2008→	}
  2009→
  2010→	// Get load average
  2011→	sm.getLoadAverage(&stats)
  2012→
  2013→	// Get uptime
  2014→	sm.getUptime(&stats)
  2015→
  2016→	return stats
  2017→}

  2019→// getMacOSMemoryStats gets memory statistics on macOS
  2020→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getMacOSMemoryStats(stats *SystemStats) {
  2021→	cmd := exec.Command("vm_stat")
  2022→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  2023→	if err != nil {
  2024→		return
  2025→	}
  2026→
  2027→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  2028→	var pageSize uint64 = 4096 // Default page size
  2029→	var freePages, activePages, inactivePages, wiredPages uint64
  2030→
  2031→	for _, line := range lines {
  2032→		if strings.Contains(line, "page size of") {
  2033→			parts := strings.Fields(line)
  2034→			if len(parts) >= 8 {
  2035→				if size, err := strconv.ParseUint(parts[7], 10, 64); err == nil {
  2036→					pageSize = size
  2037→				}
  2038→			}
  2039→		} else if strings.Contains(line, "Pages free:") {
  2040→			parts := strings.Fields(line)
  2041→			if len(parts) >= 3 {
  2042→				if pages, err := strconv.ParseUint(strings.TrimSuffix(parts[2], "."), 10, 64); err == nil {
  2043→					freePages = pages
  2044→				}
  2045→			}
  2046→		} else if strings.Contains(line, "Pages active:") {
  2047→			parts := strings.Fields(line)
  2048→			if len(parts) >= 3 {
  2049→				if pages, err := strconv.ParseUint(strings.TrimSuffix(parts[2], "."), 10, 64); err == nil {
  2050→					activePages = pages
  2051→				}
  2052→			}
  2053→		} else if strings.Contains(line, "Pages inactive:") {
  2054→			parts := strings.Fields(line)
  2055→			if len(parts) >= 3 {
  2056→				if pages, err := strconv.ParseUint(strings.TrimSuffix(parts[2], "."), 10, 64); err == nil {
  2057→					inactivePages = pages
  2058→				}
  2059→			}
  2060→		} else if strings.Contains(line, "Pages wired down:") {
  2061→			parts := strings.Fields(line)
  2062→			if len(parts) >= 4 {
  2063→				if pages, err := strconv.ParseUint(strings.TrimSuffix(parts[3], "."), 10, 64); err == nil {
  2064→					wiredPages = pages
  2065→				}
  2066→			}
  2067→		}
  2068→	}
  2069→
  2070→	totalPages := freePages + activePages + inactivePages + wiredPages
  2071→	usedPages := activePages + inactivePages + wiredPages
  2072→
  2073→	stats.MemTotal = totalPages * pageSize
  2074→	stats.MemUsed = usedPages * pageSize
  2075→}
  2076→
  2077→// getLinuxMemoryStats gets memory statistics on Linux
  2078→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getLinuxMemoryStats(stats *SystemStats) {
  2079→	// Read /proc/meminfo
  2080→	data, err := os.ReadFile("/proc/meminfo")
  2081→	if err != nil {
  2082→		return
  2083→	}
  2084→
  2085→	lines := strings.Split(string(data), "\n")
  2086→	for _, line := range lines {
  2087→		fields := strings.Fields(line)
  2088→		if len(fields) < 2 {
  2089→			continue
  2090→		}
  2091→
  2092→		value, err := strconv.ParseUint(fields[1], 10, 64)
  2093→		if err != nil {
  2094→			continue
  2095→		}
  2096→
  2097→		// Convert from KB to bytes
  2098→		value *= 1024
  2099→
  2100→		switch fields[0] {
  2101→		case "MemTotal:":
  2102→			stats.MemTotal = value
  2103→		case "MemAvailable:":
  2104→			stats.MemUsed = stats.MemTotal - value
  2105→		case "SwapTotal:":
  2106→			stats.SwapTotal = value
  2107→		case "SwapFree:":
  2108→			stats.SwapUsed = stats.SwapTotal - value
  2109→		}
  2110→	}
  2111→}

  2113→// getLoadAverage gets the system load average
  2114→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getLoadAverage(stats *SystemStats) {
  2115→	if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" {

  2117→		output, err := cmd.Output()
  2118→		if err != nil {
  2119→			return
  2120→		}
  2121→
  2122→		// Parse output like "{ 1.23 2.34 3.45 }"
  2123→		line := strings.TrimSpace(string(output))
  2124→		line = strings.Trim(line, "{}")
  2125→		fields := strings.Fields(line)
  2126→
  2127→		if len(fields) >= 3 {
  2128→			if load, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[0], 64); err == nil {
  2129→				stats.LoadAvg[0] = load
  2130→			}
  2131→			if load, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[1], 64); err == nil {
  2132→				stats.LoadAvg[1] = load
  2133→			}
  2134→			if load, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[2], 64); err == nil {
  2135→				stats.LoadAvg[2] = load
  2136→			}
  2137→		}
  2138→	} else {
  2139→		// Linux: read /proc/loadavg
  2140→		data, err := os.ReadFile("/proc/loadavg")
  2141→		if err != nil {
  2142→			return
  2143→		}
  2144→
  2145→		fields := strings.Fields(string(data))
  2146→		if len(fields) >= 3 {
  2147→			if load, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[0], 64); err == nil {
  2148→				stats.LoadAvg[0] = load
  2149→			}
  2150→			if load, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[1], 64); err == nil {
  2151→				stats.LoadAvg[1] = load
  2152→			}
  2153→			if load, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[2], 64); err == nil {
  2154→				stats.LoadAvg[2] = load
  2155→			}
  2156→		}
  2157→	}
  2158→}

  2160→// getUptime gets the system uptime
  2161→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getUptime(stats *SystemStats) {
  2162→	if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" {
  2163→		cmd := exec.Command("sysctl", "-n", "kern.boottime")
  2164→		output, err := cmd.Output()
  2165→		if err != nil {
  2166→			return
  2167→		}
  2168→
  2169→		// Parse output like "{ sec = 1234567890, usec = 123456 } ..."
  2170→		line := strings.TrimSpace(string(output))
  2171→		if strings.Contains(line, "sec = ") {
  2172→			start := strings.Index(line, "sec = ") + 6
  2173→			end := strings.Index(line[start:], ",")
  2174→			if end > 0 {
  2175→				if bootTime, err := strconv.ParseInt(line[start:start+end], 10, 64); err == nil {
  2176→					stats.Uptime = time.Since(time.Unix(bootTime, 0))
  2177→				}
  2178→			}
  2179→		}
  2180→	} else {
  2181→		// Linux: read /proc/uptime
  2182→		data, err := os.ReadFile("/proc/uptime")
  2183→		if err != nil {
  2184→			return
  2185→		}
  2186→
  2187→		fields := strings.Fields(string(data))
  2188→		if len(fields) >= 1 {
  2189→			if uptime, err := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[0], 64); err == nil {
  2190→				stats.Uptime = time.Duration(uptime * float64(time.Second))
  2191→			}
  2192→		}
  2193→	}
  2194→}

  2196→// getNetworkConnections retrieves current network connections
  2197→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getNetworkConnections() []NetworkConnection {
  2198→	var connections []NetworkConnection
  2199→
  2200→	// Use netstat to get network connections
  2201→	cmd := exec.Command("netstat", "-an")
  2202→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  2203→	if err != nil {
  2204→		return connections
  2205→	}
  2206→
  2207→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  2208→	for _, line := range lines {
  2209→		fields := strings.Fields(line)
  2210→		if len(fields) < 6 {
  2211→			continue
  2212→		}
  2213→
  2214→		// Skip header lines
  2215→		if fields[0] == "Proto" || fields[0] == "Active" {
  2216→			continue
  2217→		}
  2218→
  2219→		protocol := fields[0]
  2220→		localAddr := fields[3]
  2221→		remoteAddr := fields[4]
  2222→		state := ""
  2223→		if len(fields) > 5 {
  2224→			state = fields[5]
  2225→		}
  2226→
  2227→		// Parse addresses
  2228→		localParts := strings.Split(localAddr, ":")
  2229→		remoteParts := strings.Split(remoteAddr, ":")
  2230→
  2231→		var localIP, localPort, remoteIP, remotePort string
  2232→		if len(localParts) >= 2 {
  2233→			localIP = strings.Join(localParts[:len(localParts)-1], ":")
  2234→			localPort = localParts[len(localParts)-1]
  2235→		}
  2236→		if len(remoteParts) >= 2 {
  2237→			remoteIP = strings.Join(remoteParts[:len(remoteParts)-1], ":")
  2238→			remotePort = remoteParts[len(remoteParts)-1]
  2239→		}
  2240→
  2241→		connection := NetworkConnection{
  2242→			Protocol:    protocol,
  2243→			LocalAddr:   localIP,
  2244→			LocalPort:   localPort,
  2245→			RemoteAddr:  remoteIP,
  2246→			RemotePort:  remotePort,
  2247→			State:       state,
  2248→			PID:         0,  // Would need additional lookup
  2249→			ProcessName: "", // Would need additional lookup
  2250→		}
  2251→
  2252→		connections = append(connections, connection)
  2253→	}
  2254→
  2255→	return connections
  2256→}
  2257→
  2258→// getNetworkInterfaces retrieves network interface statistics
  2259→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getNetworkInterfaces() []NetworkInterface {
  2260→	var interfaces []NetworkInterface
  2261→
  2262→	if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" {
  2263→		// Use netstat -ib for interface statistics on macOS
  2264→		cmd := exec.Command("netstat", "-ib")
  2265→		output, err := cmd.Output()
  2266→		if err != nil {
  2267→			return interfaces
  2268→		}
  2269→
  2270→		lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  2271→		for i, line := range lines {
  2272→			if i == 0 || line == "" { // Skip header
  2273→				continue
  2274→			}
  2275→
  2276→			fields := strings.Fields(line)
  2277→			if len(fields) < 10 {
  2278→				continue
  2279→			}
  2280→
  2281→			name := fields[0]
  2282→			if name == "Name" { // Skip header
  2283→				continue
  2284→			}
  2285→
  2286→			bytesIn, _ := strconv.ParseUint(fields[6], 10, 64)
  2287→			bytesOut, _ := strconv.ParseUint(fields[9], 10, 64)
  2288→			packetsIn, _ := strconv.ParseUint(fields[4], 10, 64)
  2289→			packetsOut, _ := strconv.ParseUint(fields[7], 10, 64)
  2290→
  2291→			iface := NetworkInterface{
  2292→				Name:       name,
  2293→				BytesIn:    bytesIn,
  2294→				BytesOut:   bytesOut,
  2295→				PacketsIn:  packetsIn,
  2296→				PacketsOut: packetsOut,
  2297→				Speed:      0, // Would need additional lookup
  2298→			}
  2299→
  2300→			interfaces = append(interfaces, iface)
  2301→		}
  2302→	}
  2303→
  2304→	return interfaces
  2305→}

  2307→// AlertManager methods
  2308→
  2309→// CheckThresholds evaluates current system metrics against thresholds
  2310→func (am *AlertManager) CheckThresholds(stats SystemStats, processes []Process, connections []NetworkConnection) {
  2311→	now := time.Now()
  2312→
  2313→	// Check CPU usage
  2314→	if len(stats.CPUUsage) > 0 {
  2315→		avgCPU := 0.0
  2316→		for _, usage := range stats.CPUUsage {
  2317→			avgCPU += usage
  2318→		}
  2319→		avgCPU /= float64(len(stats.CPUUsage))
  2320→
  2321→		am.checkMetricThreshold("cpu_usage", AlertTypeCPU, avgCPU,
  2322→			am.thresholds.CPU.Warning, am.thresholds.CPU.Critical,
  2323→			"CPU Usage", "%", now)
  2324→	}
  2325→
  2326→	// Check memory usage
  2327→	if stats.MemTotal > 0 {
  2328→		memUsage := float64(stats.MemUsed) / float64(stats.MemTotal) * 100
  2329→		am.checkMetricThreshold("memory_usage", AlertTypeMemory, memUsage,
  2330→			am.thresholds.Memory.Warning, am.thresholds.Memory.Critical,
  2331→			"Memory Usage", "%", now)
  2332→	}
  2333→
  2334→	// Check load average
  2335→	loadAvg := stats.LoadAvg[0]
  2336→	am.checkMetricThreshold("load_average", AlertTypeSystem, loadAvg,
  2337→		am.thresholds.LoadAverage.Warning, am.thresholds.LoadAverage.Critical,
  2338→		"Load Average", "", now)
  2339→
  2340→	// Check process count
  2341→	processCount := float64(len(processes))
  2342→	am.checkMetricThreshold("process_count", AlertTypeProcess, processCount,
  2343→		float64(am.thresholds.ProcessCount.Warning), float64(am.thresholds.ProcessCount.Critical),
  2344→		"Process Count", "processes", now)
  2345→
  2346→	// Check network connections
  2347→	connectionCount := float64(len(connections))
  2348→	am.checkMetricThreshold("connection_count", AlertTypeNetwork, connectionCount,
  2349→		float64(am.thresholds.NetworkConnections.Warning), float64(am.thresholds.NetworkConnections.Critical),
  2350→		"Network Connections", "connections", now)
  2351→
  2352→	// Check for zombie processes
  2353→	zombieCount := 0
  2354→	for _, proc := range processes {
  2355→		if proc.State == "Z" {
  2356→			zombieCount++
  2357→		}
  2358→	}
  2359→	if zombieCount > 0 {
  2360→		am.createAlert("zombie_processes", AlertTypeProcess, AlertLevelWarning,
  2361→			fmt.Sprintf("Found %d zombie processes", zombieCount),
  2362→			"Zombie processes indicate processes that have completed but haven't been cleaned up by their parent",
  2363→			float64(zombieCount), 0, "processes", now)
  2364→	}
  2365→
  2366→	// Check for high memory processes
  2367→	for _, proc := range processes {
  2368→		if proc.Memory > 20.0 { // Process using more than 20% memory
  2369→			am.createAlert(fmt.Sprintf("high_memory_process_%d", proc.PID), AlertTypeProcess, AlertLevelWarning,
  2370→				fmt.Sprintf("Process %s (PID %d) using %.1f%% memory", proc.Name, proc.PID, proc.Memory),
  2371→				"High memory usage by individual process may indicate memory leak or inefficient resource usage",
  2372→				proc.Memory, 20.0, "%", now)
  2373→		}
  2374→	}
  2375→}
  2376→
  2377→// checkMetricThreshold checks a metric against warning and critical thresholds
  2378→func (am *AlertManager) checkMetricThreshold(id string, alertType AlertType, value, warning, critical float64, name, unit string, timestamp time.Time) {
  2379→	var level AlertLevel
  2380→	var threshold float64
  2381→	var message string
  2382→
  2383→	if value >= critical {
  2384→		level = AlertLevelCritical
  2385→		threshold = critical
  2386→		message = fmt.Sprintf("CRITICAL: %s is %.1f%s (threshold: %.1f%s)", name, value, unit, critical, unit)
  2387→	} else if value >= warning {
  2388→		level = AlertLevelWarning
  2389→		threshold = warning
  2390→		message = fmt.Sprintf("WARNING: %s is %.1f%s (threshold: %.1f%s)", name, value, unit, warning, unit)
  2391→	} else {
  2392→		// Value is below warning threshold, resolve any existing alert
  2393→		am.resolveAlert(id, timestamp)
  2394→		return
  2395→	}
  2396→
  2397→	description := am.getAlertDescription(alertType, level)
  2398→	actions := am.getRecommendedActions(alertType, level)
  2399→
  2400→	alert := &SystemAlert{
  2401→		ID:          id,
  2402→		Type:        alertType,
  2403→		Level:       level,
  2404→		Message:     message,
  2405→		Description: description,
  2406→		Timestamp:   timestamp,
  2407→		Source:      "SystemMonitor",
  2408→		Value:       value,
  2409→		Threshold:   threshold,
  2410→		Unit:        unit,
  2411→		Resolved:    false,
  2412→		Actions:     actions,
  2413→	}
  2414→
  2415→	am.activeAlerts[id] = alert
  2416→
  2417→	// Send notification
  2418→	select {
  2419→	case am.notifications <- *alert:
  2420→	default:
  2421→		// Channel full, skip notification
  2422→	}
  2423→}
  2424→
  2425→// createAlert creates a new alert
  2426→func (am *AlertManager) createAlert(id string, alertType AlertType, level AlertLevel, message, description string, value, threshold float64, unit string, timestamp time.Time) {
  2427→	actions := am.getRecommendedActions(alertType, level)
  2428→
  2429→	alert := &SystemAlert{
  2430→		ID:          id,
  2431→		Type:        alertType,
  2432→		Level:       level,
  2433→		Message:     message,
  2434→		Description: description,
  2435→		Timestamp:   timestamp,
  2436→		Source:      "SystemMonitor",
  2437→		Value:       value,
  2438→		Threshold:   threshold,
  2439→		Unit:        unit,
  2440→		Resolved:    false,
  2441→		Actions:     actions,
  2442→	}
  2443→
  2444→	am.activeAlerts[id] = alert
  2445→
  2446→	// Send notification
  2447→	select {
  2448→	case am.notifications <- *alert:
  2449→	default:
  2450→		// Channel full, skip notification
  2451→	}
  2452→}
  2453→
  2454→// resolveAlert marks an alert as resolved
  2455→func (am *AlertManager) resolveAlert(id string, timestamp time.Time) {
  2456→	if alert, exists := am.activeAlerts[id]; exists {
  2457→		alert.Resolved = true
  2458→		alert.ResolvedAt = &timestamp
  2459→
  2460→		// Move to history
  2461→		am.addToHistory(*alert)
  2462→		delete(am.activeAlerts, id)
  2463→	}
  2464→}
  2465→
  2466→// addToHistory adds an alert to the history
  2467→func (am *AlertManager) addToHistory(alert SystemAlert) {
  2468→	am.alertHistory = append(am.alertHistory, alert)
  2469→
  2470→	// Keep history size under limit
  2471→	if len(am.alertHistory) > am.maxHistory {
  2472→		am.alertHistory = am.alertHistory[1:]
  2473→	}
  2474→}

  2478→	descriptions := map[AlertType]map[AlertLevel]string{

  2486→		},
  2487→		AlertTypeSystem: {
  2488→			AlertLevelWarning:  "High system load detected. More processes are waiting for CPU time than available cores.",
  2489→			AlertLevelCritical: "Critical system load detected. System responsiveness is severely impacted.",
  2490→		},
  2491→		AlertTypeProcess: {

  2500→
  2501→	if typeMap, exists := descriptions[alertType]; exists {

  2544→// GetActiveAlerts returns all currently active alerts
  2545→func (am *AlertManager) GetActiveAlerts() []SystemAlert {
  2546→	alerts := make([]SystemAlert, 0, len(am.activeAlerts))
  2547→	for _, alert := range am.activeAlerts {
  2548→		alerts = append(alerts, *alert)
  2549→	}
  2550→
  2551→	// Sort by timestamp (newest first)
  2552→	sort.Slice(alerts, func(i, j int) bool {
  2553→		return alerts[i].Timestamp.After(alerts[j].Timestamp)
  2554→	})
  2555→
  2556→	return alerts
  2557→}
  2558→
  2559→// GetAlertHistory returns the alert history
  2560→func (am *AlertManager) GetAlertHistory() []SystemAlert {
  2561→	// Return a copy to prevent external modification
  2562→	history := make([]SystemAlert, len(am.alertHistory))
  2563→	copy(history, am.alertHistory)
  2564→
  2565→	// Sort by timestamp (newest first)
  2566→	sort.Slice(history, func(i, j int) bool {
  2567→		return history[i].Timestamp.After(history[j].Timestamp)
  2568→	})
  2569→
  2570→	return history
  2571→}

  2573→// GetNotificationChannel returns the notification channel
  2574→func (am *AlertManager) GetNotificationChannel() <-chan SystemAlert {
  2575→	return am.notifications
  2576→}

  2588→// getSystemAlerts generates system alerts using the alert manager
  2589→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) getSystemAlerts() []SystemAlert {

  2591→	sm.alertManager.CheckThresholds(sm.systemStats, sm.processes, sm.connections)

  2596→
  2597→// ProcessSortType represents different ways to sort processes
  2598→type ProcessSortType string
  2599→
  2600→const (
  2601→	SortByPID       ProcessSortType = "PID"
  2602→	SortByName      ProcessSortType = "Name"
  2603→	SortByUser      ProcessSortType = "User"
  2604→	SortByCPU       ProcessSortType = "CPU"
  2605→	SortByMemory    ProcessSortType = "Memory"
  2606→	SortByState     ProcessSortType = "State"
  2607→	SortByPPID      ProcessSortType = "PPID"
  2608→	SortByStartTime ProcessSortType = "StartTime"
  2609→	SortByThreads   ProcessSortType = "Threads"
  2610→	SortByPriority  ProcessSortType = "Priority"
  2611→	SortByRuntime   ProcessSortType = "Runtime"
  2612→)
  2613→
  2614→// ProcessFilter holds filtering criteria for processes
  2615→type ProcessFilter struct {
  2616→	NamePattern     string
  2617→	UserPattern     string
  2618→	CommandPattern  string
  2619→	StateFilter     string
  2620→	MinCPU          float64
  2621→	MaxCPU          float64
  2622→	MinMemory       float64
  2623→	MaxMemory       float64
  2624→	ShowKernelProcs bool
  2625→}
  2626→
  2627→// Process management methods
  2628→
  2629→// sortProcesses sorts the process list based on current sort settings with enhanced criteria
  2630→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) sortProcesses() {
  2631→	sort.Slice(sm.processes, func(i, j int) bool {
  2632→		var less bool
  2633→		switch ProcessSortType(sm.sortColumn) {
  2634→		case SortByPID:
  2635→			less = sm.processes[i].PID < sm.processes[j].PID
  2636→		case SortByName:
  2637→			less = strings.ToLower(sm.processes[i].Name) < strings.ToLower(sm.processes[j].Name)
  2638→		case SortByUser:
  2639→			less = strings.ToLower(sm.processes[i].User) < strings.ToLower(sm.processes[j].User)
  2640→		case SortByCPU:
  2641→			less = sm.processes[i].CPU < sm.processes[j].CPU
  2642→		case SortByMemory:
  2643→			less = sm.processes[i].Memory < sm.processes[j].Memory
  2644→		case SortByState:
  2645→			less = sm.processes[i].State < sm.processes[j].State
  2646→		case SortByPPID:
  2647→			less = sm.processes[i].PPID < sm.processes[j].PPID
  2648→		case SortByStartTime:
  2649→			less = sm.processes[i].StartTime < sm.processes[j].StartTime
  2650→		case SortByThreads:
  2651→			less = sm.processes[i].Threads < sm.processes[j].Threads
  2652→		case SortByPriority:
  2653→			less = sm.processes[i].Priority < sm.processes[j].Priority
  2654→		case SortByRuntime:
  2655→			less = sm.processes[i].Runtime < sm.processes[j].Runtime
  2656→		default:
  2657→			less = sm.processes[i].CPU < sm.processes[j].CPU
  2658→		}
  2659→
  2660→		if sm.sortReverse {
  2661→			return !less
  2662→		}
  2663→		return less
  2664→	})
  2665→}
  2666→
  2667→// filterProcesses filters processes based on search query with enhanced matching
  2668→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) filterProcesses(processes []Process, query string) []Process {
  2669→	if query == "" {
  2670→		return processes
  2671→	}
  2672→
  2673→	var filtered []Process
  2674→	query = strings.ToLower(query)
  2675→
  2676→	for _, proc := range processes {
  2677→		if sm.matchesFilter(proc, query) {
  2678→			filtered = append(filtered, proc)
  2679→		}
  2680→	}
  2681→
  2682→	return filtered
  2683→}
  2684→
  2685→// matchesFilter checks if a process matches the given filter query
  2686→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) matchesFilter(proc Process, query string) bool {
  2687→	// Support different filter formats:
  2688→	// - Simple text search: "firefox"
  2689→	// - Field-specific search: "user:root", "cpu:>50", "state:R"
  2690→	// - Combined search: "user:root firefox"
  2691→
  2692→	if strings.Contains(query, ":") {
  2693→		return sm.matchesAdvancedFilter(proc, query)
  2694→	}
  2695→
  2696→	// Simple text search across multiple fields
  2697→	searchFields := []string{
  2698→		strings.ToLower(proc.Name),
  2699→		strings.ToLower(proc.Command),
  2700→		strings.ToLower(proc.User),
  2701→		strings.ToLower(proc.State),
  2702→		fmt.Sprintf("%d", proc.PID),
  2703→		fmt.Sprintf("%d", proc.PPID),
  2704→	}
  2705→
  2706→	for _, field := range searchFields {
  2707→		if strings.Contains(field, query) {
  2708→			return true
  2709→		}
  2710→	}
  2711→
  2712→	return false
  2713→}
  2714→
  2715→// matchesAdvancedFilter handles advanced filtering with field-specific queries
  2716→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) matchesAdvancedFilter(proc Process, query string) bool {
  2717→	parts := strings.Fields(query)
  2718→
  2719→	for _, part := range parts {
  2720→		if strings.Contains(part, ":") {
  2721→			// Field-specific filter
  2722→			fieldParts := strings.SplitN(part, ":", 2)
  2723→			if len(fieldParts) != 2 {
  2724→				continue
  2725→			}
  2726→
  2727→			field := strings.ToLower(fieldParts[0])
  2728→			value := strings.ToLower(fieldParts[1])
  2729→
  2730→			if !sm.matchesFieldFilter(proc, field, value) {
  2731→				return false
  2732→			}
  2733→		} else {
  2734→			// Regular text search
  2735→			if !sm.matchesFilter(proc, part) {
  2736→				return false
  2737→			}
  2738→		}
  2739→	}
  2740→
  2741→	return true
  2742→}
  2743→
  2744→// matchesFieldFilter checks if a process matches a specific field filter
  2745→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) matchesFieldFilter(proc Process, field, value string) bool {
  2746→	switch field {
  2747→	case "user", "u":
  2748→		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.User), value)
  2749→	case "name", "n":
  2750→		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.Name), value)
  2751→	case "command", "cmd", "c":
  2752→		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.Command), value)
  2753→	case "state", "s":
  2754→		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.State), value)
  2755→	case "pid":
  2756→		if pid, err := strconv.Atoi(value); err == nil {
  2757→			return proc.PID == pid
  2758→		}
  2759→	case "ppid":
  2760→		if ppid, err := strconv.Atoi(value); err == nil {
  2761→			return proc.PPID == ppid
  2762→		}
  2763→	case "cpu":
  2764→		return sm.matchesNumericFilter(proc.CPU, value)
  2765→	case "memory", "mem":
  2766→		return sm.matchesNumericFilter(proc.Memory, value)
  2767→	case "threads", "t":
  2768→		return sm.matchesNumericFilter(float64(proc.Threads), value)
  2769→	case "priority", "pri":
  2770→		return sm.matchesNumericFilter(float64(proc.Priority), value)
  2771→	}
  2772→
  2773→	return false
  2774→}
  2775→
  2776→// matchesNumericFilter handles numeric comparisons (>, <, =, >=, <=)
  2777→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) matchesNumericFilter(actual float64, filter string) bool {
  2778→	if strings.HasPrefix(filter, ">=") {
  2779→		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(filter[2:], 64); err == nil {
  2780→			return actual >= val
  2781→		}
  2782→	} else if strings.HasPrefix(filter, "<=") {
  2783→		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(filter[2:], 64); err == nil {
  2784→			return actual <= val
  2785→		}
  2786→	} else if strings.HasPrefix(filter, ">") {
  2787→		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(filter[1:], 64); err == nil {
  2788→			return actual > val
  2789→		}
  2790→	} else if strings.HasPrefix(filter, "<") {
  2791→		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(filter[1:], 64); err == nil {
  2792→			return actual < val
  2793→		}
  2794→	} else if strings.HasPrefix(filter, "=") {
  2795→		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(filter[1:], 64); err == nil {
  2796→			return math.Abs(actual-val) < 0.01 // Small epsilon for float comparison
  2797→		}
  2798→	} else {
  2799→		// Default to equality check
  2800→		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(filter, 64); err == nil {
  2801→			return math.Abs(actual-val) < 0.01
  2802→		}
  2803→	}
  2804→
  2805→	return false
  2806→}
  2807→
  2808→// killProcess attempts to kill a process by PID
  2809→func (sm *SystemMonitorModel) killProcess(pid int) {
  2810→	if pid <= 0 {
  2811→		return
  2812→	}
  2813→
  2814→	// Send SIGTERM first
  2815→	if process, err := os.FindProcess(pid); err == nil {
  2816→		process.Signal(syscall.SIGTERM)
  2817→	}
  2818→}
  2819→
  2820→
  2821→// ASCII Chart visualization methods
  2822→
  2823→// RenderMetricChart renders an ASCII chart for the specified metric
  2824→func (hm *HistoryManager) RenderMetricChart(metricName string, width, height int) string {
  2825→	metric := hm.GetMetricHistory(metricName)
  2826→	if metric == nil || len(metric.DataPoints) == 0 {
  2827→		return hm.renderPlaceholder(metricName, width, height)
  2828→	}
  2829→	
  2830→	// Extract values for chart rendering
  2831→	values := make([]float64, len(metric.DataPoints))
  2832→	for i, point := range metric.DataPoints {
  2833→		values[i] = point.Value
  2834→	}
  2835→	
  2836→	// Choose color based on metric type
  2837→	var color lipgloss.Color
  2838→	switch metricName {
  2839→	case "cpu_usage":
  2840→		color = lipgloss.Color("#ef4444") // Red for CPU
  2841→	case "memory_usage":
  2842→		color = lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6") // Purple for memory
  2843→	case "load_average":
  2844→		color = lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b") // Yellow for load
  2845→	case "network_in", "network_out":
  2846→		color = lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4") // Cyan for network
  2847→	default:
  2848→		color = lipgloss.Color("#10b981") // Green for others
  2849→	}
  2850→	
  2851→	title := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", strings.Title(strings.ReplaceAll(metricName, "_", " ")), metric.Unit)
  2852→	minVal, maxVal := metric.MinValue, metric.MaxValue
  2853→	
  2854→	// Handle infinite values
  2855→	if math.IsInf(minVal, 1) || math.IsInf(maxVal, -1) {
  2856→		minVal, maxVal = hm.findDataRange(values)
  2857→	}
  2858→	
  2859→	return hm.renderChart(title, values, width, height, color, minVal, maxVal)
  2860→}
  2861→
  2862→// renderChart renders a single-line ASCII chart
  2863→func (hm *HistoryManager) renderChart(title string, data []float64, width, height int, color lipgloss.Color, minVal, maxVal float64) string {
  2864→	var lines []string
  2865→	
  2866→	// Chart title
  2867→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(color).Bold(true)
  2868→	lines = append(lines, titleStyle.Render(title))
  2869→	
  2870→	// Calculate chart dimensions
  2871→	chartWidth := width - 10 // Leave space for Y-axis labels
  2872→	chartHeight := height - 3 // Leave space for title and X-axis
  2873→	
  2874→	if chartHeight < 3 || chartWidth < 10 {
  2875→		return hm.renderPlaceholder(title, width, height)
  2876→	}
  2877→	
  2878→	// Find data range if not provided
  2879→	if maxVal == minVal {
  2880→		minVal, maxVal = hm.findDataRange(data)
  2881→		if maxVal == minVal {
  2882→			maxVal = minVal + 1 // Avoid division by zero
  2883→		}
  2884→	}
  2885→	
  2886→	// Build chart grid
  2887→	chart := make([][]rune, chartHeight)
  2888→	for i := range chart {
  2889→		chart[i] = make([]rune, chartWidth)
  2890→		for j := range chart[i] {
  2891→			chart[i][j] = ' '
  2892→		}
  2893→	}
  2894→	
  2895→	// Plot data points
  2896→	dataLen := len(data)
  2897→	if dataLen > 0 {
  2898→		for x := 0; x < chartWidth && x < dataLen; x++ {
  2899→			dataIndex := dataLen - chartWidth + x
  2900→			if dataIndex >= 0 && dataIndex < len(data) {
  2901→				value := data[dataIndex]
  2902→				
  2903→				// Normalize value to chart height
  2904→				normalizedValue := (value - minVal) / (maxVal - minVal)
  2905→				y := int(float64(chartHeight-1) * (1.0 - normalizedValue))
  2906→				
  2907→				// Clamp y to chart bounds
  2908→				if y < 0 {
  2909→					y = 0
  2910→				}
  2911→				if y >= chartHeight {
  2912→					y = chartHeight - 1
  2913→				}
  2914→				
  2915→				// Set chart character
  2916→				chart[y][x] = '█'
  2917→			}
  2918→		}
  2919→	}
  2920→	
  2921→	// Convert chart to string lines
  2922→	chartStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(color)
  2923→	for i, row := range chart {
  2924→		line := string(row)
  2925→		// Add Y-axis labels for first and last row
  2926→		if i == 0 {
  2927→			line = fmt.Sprintf("%6.1f │%s", maxVal, line)
  2928→		} else if i == len(chart)-1 {
  2929→			line = fmt.Sprintf("%6.1f │%s", minVal, line)
  2930→		} else {
  2931→			line = fmt.Sprintf("       │%s", line)
  2932→		}
  2933→		lines = append(lines, chartStyle.Render(line))
  2934→	}
  2935→	
  2936→	// Add X-axis
  2937→	xAxis := strings.Repeat("─", chartWidth)
  2938→	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("       └%s", xAxis))
  2939→	
  2940→	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
  2941→}
  2942→
  2943→// findDataRange finds the min and max values in a data slice
  2944→func (hm *HistoryManager) findDataRange(data []float64) (float64, float64) {
  2945→	if len(data) == 0 {
  2946→		return 0, 1
  2947→	}
  2948→	
  2949→	minVal, maxVal := data[0], data[0]
  2950→	for _, value := range data {
  2951→		if value < minVal {
  2952→			minVal = value
  2953→		}
  2954→		if value > maxVal {
  2955→			maxVal = value
  2956→		}
  2957→	}
  2958→	
  2959→	// Add some padding
  2960→	padding := (maxVal - minVal) * 0.1
  2961→	if padding == 0 {
  2962→		padding = 1
  2963→	}
  2964→	
  2965→	return minVal - padding, maxVal + padding
  2966→}
  2967→
  2968→// renderPlaceholder renders a placeholder when no data is available
  2969→func (hm *HistoryManager) renderPlaceholder(title string, width, height int) string {
  2970→	style := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  2971→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  2972→		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
  2973→		Padding(1).
  2974→		Width(width - 2).
  2975→		Height(height - 2).
  2976→		Align(lipgloss.Center)
  2977→	
  2978→	content := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\nNo data available\nCollecting metrics...", title)
  2979→	return style.Render(content)
  2980→}
  2981→

    15→// AIAssistantModel implements a dedicated AI assistant interface

    22→	// AI Assistant state

   113→	}
   114→
   115→	// Set default agent to the first available one
   116→	defaultAgent := "gemini" // Default to gemini since it's available
   117→	for _, agent := range agents {

   119→			defaultAgent = agent.Command
   120→			break
   121→		}
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	return &AIAssistantModel{
   125→		active:            false,
   126→		currentAgent:      defaultAgent,
   127→		availableAgents:   agents,
   128→		chatHistory:       make([]AIMessage, 0),
   129→		input:             "",
   130→		cursorPos:         0,
   131→		scrollOffset:      0,
   132→		isProcessing:      false,
   133→		showAgentSelector: false,
   134→		selectedAgentIdx:  0,
   135→		showChatHistory:   true,
   136→		maxHistorySize:    100,
   137→		frame:             0,
   138→		pulsePhase:        0,
   139→		glowIntensity:     0.5,
   140→		autoScroll:        true,
   141→		timestamps:        true,
   142→	}
   143→}
   144→
   145→// Init initializes the AI assistant component
   146→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
   147→	return tea.Batch(
   148→		tea.Tick(50*time.Millisecond, func(t time.Time) tea.Msg {
   149→			return tickMsg(t)
   150→		}),
   151→	)
   152→}
   153→
   154→// Update handles messages for the AI assistant
   155→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   156→	if !ai.active {
   157→		return ai, nil
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
   161→	case tea.KeyMsg:
   162→		return ai.handleKeyPress(msg)
   163→
   164→	case tickMsg:
   165→		ai.frame++
   166→		ai.pulsePhase += 0.1
   167→		ai.glowIntensity = 0.3 + 0.4*float64(math.Sin(ai.pulsePhase))
   168→		return ai, nil
   169→
   170→	case aiResponseMsg:
   171→		ai.isProcessing = false
   172→		if msg.error != nil {
   173→			ai.addMessage("assistant", fmt.Sprintf("Error: %v", msg.error), msg.agent)
   174→		} else {
   175→			ai.addMessage("assistant", msg.response, msg.agent)
   176→		}
   177→		if ai.autoScroll {
   178→			ai.scrollToBottom()
   179→		}
   180→		return ai, nil
   181→
   182→	case aiProcessingMsg:
   183→		ai.isProcessing = msg.processing
   184→		return ai, nil
   185→	}
   186→
   187→	return ai, nil
   188→}
   189→
   190→// handleKeyPress handles keyboard input
   191→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) handleKeyPress(msg tea.KeyMsg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   192→	switch {
   193→	case msg.String() == "tab":
   194→		ai.showAgentSelector = !ai.showAgentSelector
   195→		return ai, nil
   196→
   197→	case msg.String() == "ctrl+h":
   198→		ai.showChatHistory = !ai.showChatHistory
   199→		return ai, nil
   200→
   201→	case msg.String() == "ctrl+c":
   202→		if ai.isProcessing {
   203→			ai.isProcessing = false
   204→			ai.addMessage("system", "Processing cancelled", "system")
   205→		}
   206→		return ai, nil
   207→
   208→	case msg.String() == "ctrl+l":
   209→		ai.chatHistory = make([]AIMessage, 0)
   210→		return ai, nil
   211→
   212→	case ai.showAgentSelector:
   213→		return ai.handleAgentSelection(msg)
   214→
   215→	default:
   216→		return ai.handleChatInput(msg)
   217→	}
   218→}
   219→
   220→// handleAgentSelection handles agent selection interface
   221→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) handleAgentSelection(msg tea.KeyMsg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   222→	switch msg.String() {
   223→	case "up", "k":
   224→		if ai.selectedAgentIdx > 0 {
   225→			ai.selectedAgentIdx--
   226→		}
   227→	case "down", "j":
   228→		if ai.selectedAgentIdx < len(ai.availableAgents)-1 {
   229→			ai.selectedAgentIdx++
   230→		}
   231→	case "enter":
   232→		if ai.selectedAgentIdx < len(ai.availableAgents) {
   233→			ai.currentAgent = ai.availableAgents[ai.selectedAgentIdx].Command
   234→			ai.showAgentSelector = false
   235→			ai.addMessage("system", fmt.Sprintf("Switched to %s", ai.availableAgents[ai.selectedAgentIdx].Name), "system")
   236→		}
   237→	case "esc":
   238→		ai.showAgentSelector = false
   239→	}
   240→	return ai, nil
   241→}
   242→
   243→// handleChatInput handles chat input
   244→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) handleChatInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   245→	switch msg.String() {
   246→	case "enter":
   247→		if ai.input != "" {
   248→			return ai.sendMessage()
   249→		}
   250→	case "backspace":
   251→		if ai.cursorPos > 0 {
   252→			ai.input = ai.input[:ai.cursorPos-1] + ai.input[ai.cursorPos:]
   253→			ai.cursorPos--
   254→		}
   255→	case "left":
   256→		if ai.cursorPos > 0 {
   257→			ai.cursorPos--
   258→		}
   259→	case "right":
   260→		if ai.cursorPos < len(ai.input) {
   261→			ai.cursorPos++
   262→		}
   263→	case "home", "ctrl+a":
   264→		ai.cursorPos = 0
   265→	case "end", "ctrl+e":
   266→		ai.cursorPos = len(ai.input)
   267→	case "up":
   268→		ai.scrollUp()
   269→	case "down":
   270→		ai.scrollDown()
   271→	default:
   272→		if len(msg.String()) == 1 {
   273→			ai.input = ai.input[:ai.cursorPos] + msg.String() + ai.input[ai.cursorPos:]
   274→			ai.cursorPos++
   275→		}
   276→	}
   277→	return ai, nil
   278→}
   279→
   280→// sendMessage sends a message to the AI
   281→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) sendMessage() (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   282→	message := strings.TrimSpace(ai.input)
   283→	if message == "" {
   284→		return ai, nil
   285→	}
   286→
   287→	// Add user message to history
   288→	ai.addMessage("user", message, ai.currentAgent)
   289→
   290→	// Clear input
   291→	ai.input = ""
   292→	ai.cursorPos = 0
   293→
   294→	// Set processing state
   295→	ai.isProcessing = true
   296→
   297→	if ai.autoScroll {
   298→		ai.scrollToBottom()
   299→	}
   300→
   301→	// Send to AI agent
   302→	return ai, ai.processAIRequest(message)
   303→}
   304→
   305→// processAIRequest processes the AI request
   306→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) processAIRequest(message string) tea.Cmd {
   307→	return func() tea.Msg {
   308→		// Find the current agent
   309→		var agent *AIAgent
   310→		for _, a := range ai.availableAgents {
   311→			if a.Command == ai.currentAgent {
   312→				agent = &a
   313→				break
   314→			}
   315→		}
   316→
   317→		if agent == nil || !agent.Available {
   318→			return aiResponseMsg{
   319→				response: "The selected AI agent is not available. Please check your installation or select a different agent.",
   320→				agent:    ai.currentAgent,
   321→				error:    fmt.Errorf("agent not available"),
   322→			}
   323→		}
   324→
   325→		// Construct command based on agent type
   326→		var cmd *exec.Cmd
   327→		switch agent.Command {
   328→		case "claude-code":
   329→			// Claude Code: Try claude command if available
   330→			cmd = exec.Command("claude", message)
   331→		case "gemini":
   332→			// Gemini CLI: Use -p flag for prompt
   333→			cmd = exec.Command("gemini", "-p", message)
   334→		case "gh copilot":
   335→			// GitHub Copilot CLI: Use suggest command for code suggestions
   336→			cmd = exec.Command("gh", "copilot", "suggest", message)
   337→		case "opencode":
   338→			cmd = exec.Command("opencode", message)
   339→		case "aider":
   340→			// Aider: Use --message flag for non-interactive mode
   341→			cmd = exec.Command("aider", "--message", message)
   342→		case "sgpt":
   343→			// Shell GPT: Direct message input
   344→			cmd = exec.Command("sgpt", message)
   345→		default:
   346→			return aiResponseMsg{
   347→				response: "",
   348→				agent:    agent.Command,
   349→				error:    fmt.Errorf("unknown agent command: %s", agent.Command),
   350→			}
   351→		}
   352→
   353→		// Set working directory
   354→		if wd, err := os.Getwd(); err == nil {
   355→			cmd.Dir = wd
   356→		}
   357→
   358→		// Execute command
   359→		output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
   360→		return aiResponseMsg{
   361→			response: string(output),
   362→			agent:    agent.Command,
   363→			error:    err,
   364→		}
   365→	}
   366→}
   367→
   368→// View renders the AI assistant interface
   369→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) View() string {
   370→	if !ai.active {
   371→		return ""
   372→	}
   373→
   374→	var sections []string
   375→
   376→	// Header
   377→	sections = append(sections, ai.renderHeader())
   378→
   379→	// Agent selector or chat interface
   380→	if ai.showAgentSelector {
   381→		sections = append(sections, ai.renderAgentSelector())
   382→	} else {
   383→		// Chat history
   384→		if ai.showChatHistory {
   385→			sections = append(sections, ai.renderChatHistory())
   386→		}
   387→
   388→		// Input area
   389→		sections = append(sections, ai.renderInputArea())
   390→	}
   391→
   392→	// Help footer
   393→	sections = append(sections, ai.renderHelp())
   394→
   395→	return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, sections...)
   396→}
   397→
   398→// renderHeader renders the header section
   399→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) renderHeader() string {
   400→	currentAgentName := "Unknown"
   401→	currentAgentIcon := "🤖"
   402→	currentAgentColor := lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")
   403→
   404→	for _, agent := range ai.availableAgents {
   405→		if agent.Command == ai.currentAgent {
   406→			currentAgentName = agent.Name
   407→			currentAgentIcon = agent.Icon
   408→			currentAgentColor = agent.Color
   409→			break
   410→		}
   411→	}
   412→
   413→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   414→		Foreground(currentAgentColor).
   415→		Bold(true).
   416→		Padding(0, 1)
   417→
   418→	statusStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   419→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8")).
   420→		Italic(true)
   421→
   422→	processingIndicator := ""
   423→	if ai.isProcessing {
   424→		dots := strings.Repeat(".", (ai.frame/10)%4)
   425→		processingIndicator = statusStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" (processing%s)", dots))
   426→	}
   427→
   428→	title := titleStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s AI Assistant - %s", currentAgentIcon, currentAgentName))
   429→
   430→	headerBox := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   431→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   432→		BorderForeground(currentAgentColor).
   433→		Padding(1, 2).
   434→		Width(ai.width - 4)
   435→
   436→	return headerBox.Render(title + processingIndicator)
   437→}
   438→
   439→// renderAgentSelector renders the agent selection interface
   440→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) renderAgentSelector() string {
   441→	var items []string
   442→
   443→	for i, agent := range ai.availableAgents {
   444→		style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Padding(0, 2)
   445→
   446→		if i == ai.selectedAgentIdx {
   447→			style = style.Background(agent.Color).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#000000"))
   448→		} else if !agent.Available {
   449→			style = style.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280"))
   450→		} else {
   451→			style = style.Foreground(agent.Color)
   452→		}
   453→
   454→		status := "✓"
   455→		if !agent.Available {
   456→			status = "✗"
   457→		}
   458→
   459→		item := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s - %s", status, agent.Icon, agent.Name, agent.Description)
   460→		items = append(items, style.Render(item))
   461→	}
   462→
   463→	selectorBox := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   464→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   465→		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
   466→		Padding(1, 2).
   467→		Width(ai.width - 4).
   468→		Height(ai.height - 8)
   469→
   470→	content := lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, items...)
   471→	return selectorBox.Render(content)
   472→}
   473→
   474→// renderChatHistory renders the chat history
   475→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) renderChatHistory() string {
   476→	if len(ai.chatHistory) == 0 {
   477→		emptyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   478→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
   479→			Italic(true).
   480→			Align(lipgloss.Center).
   481→			Padding(2)
   482→
   483→		return emptyStyle.Render("No conversation yet. Type a message to start chatting with your AI assistant!")
   484→	}
   485→
   486→	var messages []string
   487→	visibleHeight := ai.height - 12 // Account for header, input, and help
   488→
   489→	startIdx := ai.scrollOffset
   490→	endIdx := len(ai.chatHistory)
   491→
   492→	if endIdx-startIdx > visibleHeight {
   493→		endIdx = startIdx + visibleHeight
   494→	}
   495→
   496→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx && i < len(ai.chatHistory); i++ {
   497→		msg := ai.chatHistory[i]
   498→		messages = append(messages, ai.renderMessage(msg))
   499→	}
   500→
   501→	chatBox := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   502→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   503→		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#374151")).
   504→		Padding(1, 2).
   505→		Width(ai.width - 4).
   506→		Height(visibleHeight)
   507→
   508→	content := lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, messages...)
   509→	return chatBox.Render(content)
   510→}
   511→
   512→// renderMessage renders a single chat message
   513→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) renderMessage(msg AIMessage) string {
   514→	var style lipgloss.Style
   515→	var prefix string
   516→
   517→	switch msg.Role {
   518→	case "user":
   519→		style = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#3b82f6"))
   520→		prefix = "You: "
   521→	case "assistant":
   522→		style = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981"))
   523→		prefix = "AI: "
   524→	case "system":
   525→		style = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).Italic(true)
   526→		prefix = "System: "
   527→	}
   528→
   529→	timestamp := ""
   530→	if ai.timestamps {
   531→		timestamp = lipgloss.NewStyle().
   532→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
   533→			Render(fmt.Sprintf("[%s] ", msg.Timestamp.Format("15:04:05")))
   534→	}
   535→
   536→	content := style.Render(prefix + msg.Content)
   537→	return timestamp + content
   538→}
   539→
   540→// renderInputArea renders the input area
   541→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) renderInputArea() string {
   542→	cursor := ""
   543→	if ai.cursorPos == len(ai.input) {
   544→		cursor = "│"
   545→	}
   546→
   547→	displayInput := ai.input[:ai.cursorPos] + cursor + ai.input[ai.cursorPos:]
   548→
   549→	inputStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   550→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   551→		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   552→		Padding(0, 2).
   553→		Width(ai.width - 4)
   554→
   555→	placeholder := ""
   556→	if ai.input == "" && !ai.isProcessing {
   557→		placeholder = lipgloss.NewStyle().
   558→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
   559→			Italic(true).
   560→			Render("Type your message here...")
   561→	}
   562→
   563→	content := displayInput + placeholder
   564→	if ai.isProcessing {
   565→		content = lipgloss.NewStyle().
   566→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
   567→			Italic(true).
   568→			Render("Processing your request...")
   569→	}
   570→
   571→	return inputStyle.Render(content)
   572→}
   573→
   574→// renderHelp renders the help footer
   575→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) renderHelp() string {
   576→	helpStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   577→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
   578→		Padding(0, 1)
   579→
   580→	help := "Tab: Select Agent • Ctrl+H: Toggle History • Ctrl+L: Clear • Ctrl+C: Cancel • ↑↓: Scroll • Enter: Send"
   581→	return helpStyle.Render(help)
   582→}
   583→
   584→// Helper methods
   585→
   586→// addMessage adds a message to the chat history
   587→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) addMessage(role, content, agent string) {
   588→	msg := AIMessage{
   589→		Role:      role,
   590→		Content:   content,
   591→		Timestamp: time.Now(),
   592→		Agent:     agent,
   593→	}
   594→
   595→	ai.chatHistory = append(ai.chatHistory, msg)
   596→
   597→	// Limit history size
   598→	if len(ai.chatHistory) > ai.maxHistorySize {
   599→		ai.chatHistory = ai.chatHistory[len(ai.chatHistory)-ai.maxHistorySize:]
   600→	}
   601→}
   602→
   603→// scrollUp scrolls the chat history up
   604→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) scrollUp() {
   605→	if ai.scrollOffset > 0 {
   606→		ai.scrollOffset--
   607→	}
   608→}
   609→
   610→// scrollDown scrolls the chat history down
   611→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) scrollDown() {
   612→	maxScroll := len(ai.chatHistory) - (ai.height - 12)
   613→	if maxScroll < 0 {
   614→		maxScroll = 0
   615→	}
   616→	if ai.scrollOffset < maxScroll {
   617→		ai.scrollOffset++
   618→	}
   619→}
   620→
   621→// scrollToBottom scrolls to the bottom of chat history
   622→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) scrollToBottom() {
   623→	maxScroll := len(ai.chatHistory) - (ai.height - 12)
   624→	if maxScroll < 0 {
   625→		maxScroll = 0
   626→	}
   627→	ai.scrollOffset = maxScroll
   628→}
   629→
   630→// Component interface implementation
   631→
   632→// SetSize sets the component size
   633→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) SetSize(width, height int) {
   634→	ai.width = width
   635→	ai.height = height
   636→}
   637→
   638→// GetViewType returns the view type
   639→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) GetViewType() ViewType {
   640→	return ViewAIAssistant
   641→}
   642→
   643→// IsActive returns whether the component is active
   644→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) IsActive() bool {
   645→	return ai.active
   646→}
   647→
   648→// SetActive sets the active state
   649→func (ai *AIAssistantModel) SetActive(active bool) {
   650→	ai.active = active
   651→	if active {
   652→		// Add welcome message if this is the first time
   653→		if len(ai.chatHistory) == 0 {
   654→			ai.addMessage("system", "Welcome to the AI Assistant! Select an agent with Tab and start chatting.", "system")
   655→		}
   656→	}
   657→}
