   158→// NewBannerGrabber creates a new banner grabber
   159→func NewBannerGrabber() *BannerGrabber {
   160→	return &BannerGrabber{
   161→		timeout: 5 * time.Second,
   162→	}
   163→}
   164→
   165→// GrabBanner attempts to grab a service banner from a port
   166→func (bg *BannerGrabber) GrabBanner(host string, port int) string {
   167→	address := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", host, port)
   168→
   169→	// Create connection with timeout
   170→	conn, err := net.DialTimeout("tcp", address, bg.timeout)
   171→	if err != nil {
   172→		return ""
   173→	}
   174→	defer conn.Close()
   175→
   176→	// Set read deadline
   177→	conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(bg.timeout))
   178→
   179→	// Try to read banner (some services send banner immediately)
   180→	buffer := make([]byte, 1024)
   181→	n, err := conn.Read(buffer)
   182→	if err != nil {
   183→		// If no immediate banner, try sending a probe
   184→		return bg.probeBanner(conn, port)
   185→	}
   186→
   187→	banner := strings.TrimSpace(string(buffer[:n]))
   188→	if len(banner) > 0 {
   189→		return banner
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	// Try probing if no immediate banner
   193→	return bg.probeBanner(conn, port)
   194→}
   195→
   196→// probeBanner sends protocol-specific probes to elicit a banner
   197→func (bg *BannerGrabber) probeBanner(conn net.Conn, port int) string {
   198→	// Set write deadline
   199→	conn.SetWriteDeadline(time.Now().Add(bg.timeout))
   200→
   201→	var probe string
   202→	switch port {
   203→	case 21: // FTP
   204→		probe = "USER anonymous\r\n"
   205→	case 22: // SSH
   206→		probe = "SSH-2.0-Scanner\r\n"
   207→	case 25: // SMTP
   208→		probe = "EHLO scanner\r\n"
   209→	case 80, 8080: // HTTP
   210→		probe = "GET / HTTP/1.0\r\n\r\n"
   211→	case 110: // POP3
   212→		probe = "USER test\r\n"
   213→	case 143: // IMAP
   214→		probe = "A001 CAPABILITY\r\n"
   215→	case 443: // HTTPS
   216→		// For HTTPS, we can't easily grab banner without TLS handshake
   217→		return ""
   218→	default:
   219→		// Generic probe
   220→		probe = "\r\n"
   221→	}
   222→
   223→	// Send probe
   224→	_, err := conn.Write([]byte(probe))
   225→	if err != nil {
   226→		return ""
   227→	}
   228→
   229→	// Read response
   230→	conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(bg.timeout))
   231→	buffer := make([]byte, 1024)
   232→	n, err := conn.Read(buffer)
   233→	if err != nil {
   234→		return ""
   235→	}
   236→
   237→	banner := strings.TrimSpace(string(buffer[:n]))
   238→
   239→	// Clean up banner (remove control characters, limit length)
   240→	banner = bg.cleanBanner(banner)
   241→
   242→	return banner
   243→}
   244→
   245→// cleanBanner cleans and formats a banner string
   246→func (bg *BannerGrabber) cleanBanner(banner string) string {
   247→	if len(banner) == 0 {
   248→		return ""
   249→	}
   250→
   251→	// Remove control characters except newlines and tabs
   252→	cleaned := strings.Map(func(r rune) rune {
   253→		if r < 32 && r != '\n' && r != '\t' {
   254→			return -1
   255→		}
   256→		return r
   257→	}, banner)
   258→
   259→	// Limit length
   260→	if len(cleaned) > 200 {
   261→		cleaned = cleaned[:200] + "..."
   262→	}
   263→
   264→	// Replace multiple whitespace with single space
   265→	cleaned = strings.Join(strings.Fields(cleaned), " ")
   266→
   267→	return cleaned
   268→}

   383→
   384→// ParsePortRange parses a port range string (e.g., "80", "80-443", "1-1000")
   385→func ParsePortRange(s string) (PortRange, error) {
   386→	if strings.Contains(s, "-") {
   387→		parts := strings.Split(s, "-")
   388→		if len(parts) != 2 {
   389→			return PortRange{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid port range format: %s", s)
   390→		}
   391→
   392→		start, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(parts[0]))
   393→		if err != nil {
   394→			return PortRange{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid start port: %s", parts[0])
   395→		}
   396→
   397→		end, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(parts[1]))
   398→		if err != nil {
   399→			return PortRange{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid end port: %s", parts[1])
   400→		}
   401→
   402→		if start > end || start < 1 || end > 65535 {
   403→			return PortRange{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid port range: %d-%d", start, end)
   404→		}
   405→
   406→		return PortRange{Start: start, End: end}, nil
   407→	}
   408→
   409→	port, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(s))
   410→	if err != nil {
   411→		return PortRange{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid port: %s", s)
   412→	}
   413→
   414→	if port < 1 || port > 65535 {
   415→		return PortRange{}, fmt.Errorf("port out of range: %d", port)
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	return PortRange{Start: port, End: port}, nil

   493→// Progress returns the completion percentage (0-100)

   508→	if sp.ScannedPorts == 0 {

   512→	elapsed := sp.ElapsedTime()
   513→	avgTimePerPort := elapsed / time.Duration(sp.ScannedPorts)

   585→// PortScanner handles the actual port scanning operations
   586→type PortScanner struct {
   587→	concurrency   int
   588→	timeout       time.Duration
   589→	serviceDB     *ServiceDatabase
   590→	bannerGrabber *BannerGrabber
   591→	rateLimiter   *RateLimiter
   592→}
   593→
   594→// RateLimiter controls the rate of port scanning operations
   595→type RateLimiter struct {
   596→	tokens   chan struct{}
   597→	interval time.Duration
   598→	ticker   *time.Ticker
   599→	done     chan struct{}
   600→}
   601→
   602→// NewRateLimiter creates a new rate limiter
   603→func NewRateLimiter(maxConcurrent int, interval time.Duration) *RateLimiter {
   604→	rl := &RateLimiter{
   605→		tokens:   make(chan struct{}, maxConcurrent),
   606→		interval: interval,
   607→		done:     make(chan struct{}),
   608→	}
   609→
   610→	// Fill initial tokens
   611→	for i := 0; i < maxConcurrent; i++ {
   612→		rl.tokens <- struct{}{}
   613→	}
   614→
   615→	// Start token replenishment if interval is set
   616→	if interval > 0 {
   617→		rl.ticker = time.NewTicker(interval)
   618→		go rl.replenishTokens()
   619→	}
   620→
   621→	return rl
   622→}
   623→
   624→// Acquire acquires a token, blocking if necessary
   625→func (rl *RateLimiter) Acquire(ctx context.Context) error {
   626→	select {
   627→	case <-rl.tokens:
   628→		return nil
   629→	case <-ctx.Done():
   630→		return ctx.Err()
   631→	case <-rl.done:
   632→		return context.Canceled
   633→	}
   634→}
   635→
   636→// Release releases a token back to the pool
   637→func (rl *RateLimiter) Release() {
   638→	select {
   639→	case rl.tokens <- struct{}{}:
   640→	default:
   641→		// Token pool is full, ignore
   642→	}
   643→}
   644→
   645→// Close stops the rate limiter
   646→func (rl *RateLimiter) Close() {
   647→	close(rl.done)
   648→	if rl.ticker != nil {
   649→		rl.ticker.Stop()
   650→	}
   651→}
   652→
   653→// replenishTokens periodically adds tokens to prevent starvation
   654→func (rl *RateLimiter) replenishTokens() {
   655→	for {
   656→		select {
   657→		case <-rl.ticker.C:
   658→			// Try to add a token if there's space
   659→			select {
   660→			case rl.tokens <- struct{}{}:
   661→			default:
   662→				// Pool is full
   663→			}
   664→		case <-rl.done:
   665→			return
   666→		}
   667→	}
   668→}
   669→
   670→// NewPortScanner creates a new port scanner
   671→func NewPortScanner(concurrency int, timeout time.Duration) *PortScanner {
   672→	// Create rate limiter with a small interval to prevent network flooding
   673→	rateLimitInterval := time.Millisecond * 10 // 10ms between token replenishment
   674→
   675→	return &PortScanner{
   676→		concurrency:   concurrency,
   677→		timeout:       timeout,
   678→		serviceDB:     NewServiceDatabase(),
   679→		bannerGrabber: NewBannerGrabber(),
   680→		rateLimiter:   NewRateLimiter(concurrency, rateLimitInterval),
   681→	}
   682→}

   684→// ScanPorts performs a port scan on the specified targets and ports
   685→func (ps *PortScanner) ScanPorts(ctx context.Context, targets []string, portRanges []PortRange, scanType ScanType, progressChan chan<- ScanProgressUpdate) error {
   686→	// Calculate total ports to scan
   687→	totalPorts := 0
   688→	for _, target := range targets {
   689→		_ = target // Used for calculating total ports per target
   690→		for _, portRange := range portRanges {
   691→			totalPorts += portRange.End - portRange.Start + 1
   692→		}
   693→	}
   694→
   695→	progress := &ScanProgress{
   696→		TotalPorts: totalPorts,
   697→		StartTime:  time.Now(),
   698→		Status:     "Starting scan...",
   699→	}
   700→
   701→	// Send initial progress
   702→	select {
   703→	case progressChan <- ScanProgressUpdate{Progress: progress}:
   704→	case <-ctx.Done():
   705→		return ctx.Err()
   706→	}
   707→
   708→	// Use rate limiter for concurrency control and network flood prevention
   709→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   710→	var mu sync.Mutex
   711→
   712→	// Scan each target and port combination
   713→	for _, target := range targets {
   714→		for _, portRange := range portRanges {
   715→			for port := portRange.Start; port <= portRange.End; port++ {
   716→				select {
   717→				case <-ctx.Done():
   718→					progress.Cancelled = true
   719→					progress.Status = "Scan cancelled"
   720→					progressChan <- ScanProgressUpdate{Progress: progress}
   721→					ps.rateLimiter.Close()
   722→					return ctx.Err()
   723→				default:
   724→					// Acquire rate limit token
   725→					if err := ps.rateLimiter.Acquire(ctx); err != nil {
   726→						progress.Cancelled = true
   727→						progress.Status = "Scan cancelled"
   728→						progressChan <- ScanProgressUpdate{Progress: progress}
   729→						ps.rateLimiter.Close()
   730→						return err
   731→					}
   732→
   733→					wg.Add(1)
   734→					go func(target string, port int) {
   735→						defer wg.Done()
   736→						defer ps.rateLimiter.Release()
   737→
   738→						// Update current scan target
   739→						mu.Lock()
   740→						progress.CurrentTarget = target
   741→						progress.CurrentPort = port
   742→						progress.Status = fmt.Sprintf("Scanning %s:%d", target, port)
   743→						mu.Unlock()
   744→
   745→						// Perform the actual port scan
   746→						result := ps.scanSinglePort(ctx, target, port, scanType)
   747→
   748→						// Update progress
   749→						mu.Lock()
   750→						progress.ScannedPorts++
   751→						switch result.State {
   752→						case PortStateOpen:
   753→							progress.OpenPorts++
   754→						case PortStateClosed:
   755→							progress.ClosedPorts++
   756→						case PortStateFiltered:
   757→							progress.FilteredPorts++
   758→						}
   759→
   760→						// Update estimated end time
   761→						if progress.ScannedPorts > 0 {
   762→							elapsed := time.Since(progress.StartTime)
   763→							avgTimePerPort := elapsed / time.Duration(progress.ScannedPorts)
   764→							remaining := progress.TotalPorts - progress.ScannedPorts
   765→							progress.EstimatedEnd = time.Now().Add(avgTimePerPort * time.Duration(remaining))
   766→						}
   767→						mu.Unlock()
   768→
   769→						// Send progress update
   770→						select {
   771→						case progressChan <- ScanProgressUpdate{Progress: progress, Result: &result}:
   772→						case <-ctx.Done():
   773→							return
   774→						}
   775→					}(target, port)
   776→				}
   777→			}
   778→		}
   779→	}
   780→
   781→	// Wait for all scans to complete
   782→	wg.Wait()
   783→
   784→	// Clean up rate limiter
   785→	ps.rateLimiter.Close()
   786→
   787→	// Check if scan was cancelled after completion
   788→	select {
   789→	case <-ctx.Done():
   790→		progress.Cancelled = true
   791→		progress.Status = "Scan cancelled"
   792→		progressChan <- ScanProgressUpdate{Progress: progress}
   793→		return ctx.Err()
   794→	default:
   795→	}
   796→
   797→	// Send final progress
   798→	progress.Completed = true
   799→	progress.Status = "Scan completed"
   800→	progressChan <- ScanProgressUpdate{Progress: progress}
   801→
   802→	return nil
   803→}
   804→
   805→// scanSinglePort scans a single port on a target
   806→func (ps *PortScanner) scanSinglePort(ctx context.Context, target string, port int, scanType ScanType) ScanResult {
   807→	// Check for cancellation before starting
   808→	select {
   809→	case <-ctx.Done():
   810→		return ScanResult{
   811→			Host:      target,
   812→			Port:      port,
   813→			Protocol:  "tcp",
   814→			State:     PortStateUnknown,
   815→			Timestamp: time.Now(),
   816→			Error:     "Scan cancelled",
   817→		}
   818→	default:
   819→	}
   820→
   821→	result := ScanResult{
   822→		Host:      target,
   823→		Port:      port,
   824→		Protocol:  "tcp", // Default to TCP
   825→		Timestamp: time.Now(),
   826→	}
   827→
   828→	startTime := time.Now()
   829→
   830→	switch scanType {
   831→	case ScanTypeTCP, ScanTypeConnect:
   832→		result = ps.scanTCPPort(ctx, target, port)
   833→	case ScanTypeUDP:
   834→		result.Protocol = "udp"
   835→		result = ps.scanUDPPort(ctx, target, port)
   836→	case ScanTypeSYN:
   837→		result = ps.scanSYNPort(ctx, target, port)
   838→	case ScanTypeComprehensive:
   839→		// Try TCP first, then UDP if TCP is closed
   840→		result = ps.scanTCPPort(ctx, target, port)
   841→		if result.State == PortStateClosed {
   842→			// Check for cancellation before UDP scan
   843→			select {
   844→			case <-ctx.Done():
   845→				result.Error = "Scan cancelled"
   846→				return result
   847→			default:
   848→			}
   849→			udpResult := ps.scanUDPPort(ctx, target, port)
   850→			if udpResult.State == PortStateOpen {
   851→				result = udpResult
   852→			}
   853→		}
   854→	}
   855→
   856→	result.ResponseTime = time.Since(startTime)
   857→
   858→	// Check for cancellation before service detection
   859→	select {
   860→	case <-ctx.Done():
   861→		result.Error = "Scan cancelled"
   862→		return result
   863→	default:
   864→	}
   865→
   866→	// Perform service detection and banner grabbing for open ports
   867→	if result.State == PortStateOpen {
   868→		if serviceInfo, exists := ps.serviceDB.GetService(port); exists {
   869→			result.Service = serviceInfo.Name
   870→		}
   871→
   872→		if banner := ps.bannerGrabber.GrabBanner(target, port); banner != "" {
   873→			result.Banner = banner
   874→			// Try to extract version information from banner
   875→			if version := ps.extractVersionFromBanner(banner, result.Service); version != "" {
   876→				result.Version = version
   877→			}
   878→		}
   879→	}
   880→
   881→	return result
   882→}
   883→
   884→// scanTCPPort performs a TCP connect scan on a port
   885→func (ps *PortScanner) scanTCPPort(ctx context.Context, target string, port int) ScanResult {
   886→	result := ScanResult{
   887→		Host:      target,
   888→		Port:      port,
   889→		Protocol:  "tcp",
   890→		Timestamp: time.Now(),
   891→	}
   892→
   893→	address := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", target, port)
   894→
   895→	// Create a context with timeout
   896→	timeoutCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, ps.timeout)
   897→	defer cancel()
   898→
   899→	// Attempt to connect
   900→	dialer := &net.Dialer{}
   901→	conn, err := dialer.DialContext(timeoutCtx, "tcp", address)
   902→	if err != nil {
   903→		if timeoutCtx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
   904→			result.State = PortStateFiltered
   905→			result.Error = "Connection timeout"
   906→		} else {
   907→			result.State = PortStateClosed
   908→			result.Error = err.Error()
   909→		}
   910→		return result
   911→	}
   912→
   913→	conn.Close()
   914→	result.State = PortStateOpen
   915→	return result
   916→}
   917→
   918→// scanUDPPort performs a UDP scan on a port
   919→func (ps *PortScanner) scanUDPPort(ctx context.Context, target string, port int) ScanResult {
   920→	result := ScanResult{
   921→		Host:      target,
   922→		Port:      port,
   923→		Protocol:  "udp",
   924→		Timestamp: time.Now(),
   925→	}
   926→
   927→	address := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", target, port)
   928→
   929→	// Create a context with timeout
   930→	timeoutCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, ps.timeout)
   931→	defer cancel()
   932→
   933→	// UDP scanning is more complex - we'll use a simple approach
   934→	var d net.Dialer
   935→	conn, err := d.DialContext(timeoutCtx, "udp", address)
   936→	if err != nil {
   937→		result.State = PortStateFiltered
   938→		result.Error = err.Error()
   939→		return result
   940→	}
   941→	defer conn.Close()
   942→
   943→	// Send a probe packet
   944→	_, err = conn.Write([]byte("probe"))
   945→	if err != nil {
   946→		result.State = PortStateFiltered
   947→		result.Error = err.Error()
   948→		return result
   949→	}
   950→
   951→	// Try to read response with timeout
   952→	conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(ps.timeout))
   953→	buffer := make([]byte, 1024)
   954→	_, err = conn.Read(buffer)
   955→	if err != nil {
   956→		// No response doesn't necessarily mean closed for UDP
   957→		result.State = PortStateOpen // Assume open if we can connect
   958→	} else {
   959→		result.State = PortStateOpen
   960→	}
   961→
   962→	return result
   963→}
   964→
   965→// scanSYNPort performs a SYN scan (requires raw sockets - simplified implementation)
   966→func (ps *PortScanner) scanSYNPort(ctx context.Context, target string, port int) ScanResult {
   967→	// SYN scanning requires raw sockets and root privileges
   968→	// For now, fall back to TCP connect scan
   969→	// In a full implementation, this would use raw sockets to send SYN packets
   970→	return ps.scanTCPPort(ctx, target, port)
   971→}
   972→
   973→// extractVersionFromBanner attempts to extract version information from a banner
   974→func (ps *PortScanner) extractVersionFromBanner(banner, service string) string {
   975→	// Simple version extraction - in a real implementation, this would be more sophisticated
   976→	banner = strings.ToLower(banner)
   977→
   978→	// Look for common version patterns
   979→	patterns := []string{
   980→		`version (\d+\.\d+\.\d+)`,
   981→		`v(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)`,
   982→		`(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)`,
   983→		`(\d+\.\d+)`,
   984→	}
   985→
   986→	for _, pattern := range patterns {
   987→		// Simple pattern matching - in a real implementation, use regex
   988→		_ = pattern // TODO: implement proper regex matching
   989→		if strings.Contains(banner, "version") {
   990→			parts := strings.Fields(banner)
   991→			for i, part := range parts {
   992→				if part == "version" && i+1 < len(parts) {
   993→					return parts[i+1]
   994→				}
   995→			}
   996→		}
   997→	}
   998→
   999→	return ""
  1000→}

  1066→// ToolComponent interface implementation
  1067→
  1068→// Init initializes the port scanner component
  1069→func (psm *PortScannerModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
  1070→	return psm.tickCmd()
  1071→}
  1072→
  1073→// Update handles messages for the port scanner
  1074→func (psm *PortScannerModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
  1075→	var cmds []tea.Cmd
  1076→
  1077→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
  1078→	case tea.KeyMsg:
  1079→		if !psm.active {
  1080→			return psm, nil
  1081→		}
  1082→
  1083→		if cmd := psm.handleKeyMsg(msg); cmd != nil {
  1084→			cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
  1085→		}
  1086→
  1087→	case scanProgressMsg:
  1088→		psm.handleScanProgress(msg.update)
  1089→
  1090→	case scanCompleteMsg:
  1091→		psm.handleScanComplete(msg.scan)
  1092→
  1093→	case tickMsg:
  1094→		psm.updateVisualEffects()
  1095→		cmds = append(cmds, psm.tickCmd())
  1096→
  1097→		// Check for scan updates
  1098→		if psm.isScanning && psm.progressChan != nil {
  1099→			select {
  1100→			case update := <-psm.progressChan:
  1101→				cmds = append(cmds, func() tea.Msg {
  1102→					return scanProgressMsg{update: update}
  1103→				})
  1104→			default:
  1105→				// No update available
  1106→			}
  1107→		}
  1108→	}
  1109→
  1110→	return psm, tea.Batch(cmds...)
  1111→}

  1172→// updateVisualEffects updates the visual effects state
  1173→func (psm *PortScannerModel) updateVisualEffects() {
  1174→	psm.frame++
  1175→	psm.pulsePhase += 0.05
  1176→	psm.glowIntensity = 0.3 + 0.2*math.Sin(psm.pulsePhase)
  1177→	psm.animFrame = (psm.animFrame + 1) % len(psm.scanAnimation)
  1178→}
  1179→
  1180→// handleKeyMsg processes key messages for the port scanner
  1181→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleKeyMsg(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
  1182→	key := msg.String()
  1183→
  1184→	// Handle input modes
  1185→	if psm.inputMode != InputModeNone {
  1186→		return psm.handleInputMode(key)
  1187→	}
  1188→
  1189→	// Handle different views
  1190→	if psm.showConfig {
  1191→		return psm.handleConfigKeys(key)
  1192→	}
  1193→
  1194→	if psm.showHistory {
  1195→		return psm.handleHistoryKeys(key)
  1196→	}
  1197→
  1198→	if psm.showDetails {
  1199→		return psm.handleDetailsKeys(key)
  1200→	}
  1201→
  1202→	if psm.showExport {
  1203→		return psm.handleExportKeys(key)
  1204→	}
  1205→
  1206→	if psm.showComparison {
  1207→		return psm.handleComparisonKeys(key)
  1208→	}
  1209→
  1210→	// Main view key handling
  1211→	return psm.handleMainKeys(key)
  1212→}
  1213→
  1214→// handleMainKeys handles key presses in the main view
  1215→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleMainKeys(key string) tea.Cmd {
  1216→	switch key {
  1217→	case "s":
  1218→		if !psm.isScanning {
  1219→			return psm.startScan()
  1220→		}
  1221→		return nil
  1222→
  1223→	case "c":
  1224→		if psm.isScanning {
  1225→			return psm.cancelScan()
  1226→		}
  1227→		return nil
  1228→
  1229→	case "C":
  1230→		psm.showConfig = true
  1231→		return nil
  1232→
  1233→	case "h":
  1234→		psm.showHistory = true
  1235→		return nil
  1236→
  1237→	case "d":
  1238→		if len(psm.scanResults) > 0 && psm.selectedRow < len(psm.scanResults) {
  1239→			psm.selectedResult = &psm.scanResults[psm.selectedRow]
  1240→			psm.showDetails = true
  1241→		}
  1242→		return nil
  1243→
  1244→	case "f":
  1245→		psm.inputMode = InputModeTarget
  1246→		return nil
  1247→
  1248→	case "p":
  1249→		psm.inputMode = InputModePort
  1250→		return nil
  1251→
  1252→	case "up", "k":
  1253→		if psm.selectedRow > 0 {
  1254→			psm.selectedRow--
  1255→			if psm.selectedRow < psm.scrollOffset {
  1256→				psm.scrollOffset = psm.selectedRow
  1257→			}
  1258→		}
  1259→		return nil
  1260→
  1261→	case "down", "j":
  1262→		if psm.selectedRow < len(psm.scanResults)-1 {
  1263→			psm.selectedRow++
  1264→			visibleRows := psm.height - 15
  1265→			if psm.selectedRow >= psm.scrollOffset+visibleRows {
  1266→				psm.scrollOffset = psm.selectedRow - visibleRows + 1
  1267→			}
  1268→		}
  1269→		return nil
  1270→
  1271→	case "H":
  1272→		psm.sortColumn = SortByHost
  1273→		psm.sortResults()
  1274→		return nil
  1275→
  1276→	case "P":
  1277→		psm.sortColumn = SortByPort
  1278→		psm.sortResults()
  1279→		return nil
  1280→
  1281→	case "S":
  1282→		psm.sortColumn = SortByPortState
  1283→		psm.sortResults()
  1284→		return nil
  1285→
  1286→	case "R":
  1287→		psm.sortReverse = !psm.sortReverse
  1288→		psm.sortResults()
  1289→		return nil
  1290→
  1291→	case "r":
  1292→		// Clear results
  1293→		psm.scanResults = make([]ScanResult, 0)
  1294→		psm.selectedRow = 0
  1295→		psm.scrollOffset = 0
  1296→		return nil
  1297→
  1298→	case "e":
  1299→		// Show export dialog
  1300→		if len(psm.scanResults) > 0 {
  1301→			psm.showExport = true
  1302→		}
  1303→		return nil
  1304→
  1305→	case "x":
  1306→		// Quick export with default settings
  1307→		if len(psm.scanResults) > 0 {
  1308→			return psm.exportCurrentResults()
  1309→		}
  1310→		return nil
  1311→
  1312→	case "m":
  1313→		// Show comparison dialog
  1314→		psm.showComparison = true
  1315→		return nil
  1316→	}
  1317→
  1318→	return nil
  1319→}

  1373→// handleHistoryKeys handles key presses in the history view
  1374→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleHistoryKeys(key string) tea.Cmd {
  1375→	switch key {
  1376→	case "esc":
  1377→		psm.showHistory = false
  1378→		return nil
  1379→
  1380→	case "up", "k":
  1381→		if psm.selectedRow > 0 {
  1382→			psm.selectedRow--
  1383→		}
  1384→		return nil
  1385→
  1386→	case "down", "j":
  1387→		if psm.selectedRow < len(psm.scanHistory)-1 {
  1388→			psm.selectedRow++
  1389→		}
  1390→		return nil
  1391→
  1392→	case "enter":
  1393→		if len(psm.scanHistory) > 0 && psm.selectedRow < len(psm.scanHistory) {
  1394→			// Load results from selected history entry
  1395→			scan := psm.scanHistory[psm.selectedRow]
  1396→			psm.scanResults = scan.Results
  1397→			psm.showHistory = false
  1398→			psm.selectedRow = 0
  1399→			psm.scrollOffset = 0
  1400→		}
  1401→		return nil
  1402→
  1403→	case "d":
  1404→		if len(psm.scanHistory) > 0 && psm.selectedRow < len(psm.scanHistory) {
  1405→			// Delete selected history entry
  1406→			psm.scanHistory = append(psm.scanHistory[:psm.selectedRow], psm.scanHistory[psm.selectedRow+1:]...)
  1407→			if psm.selectedRow >= len(psm.scanHistory) && len(psm.scanHistory) > 0 {
  1408→				psm.selectedRow = len(psm.scanHistory) - 1
  1409→			}
  1410→		}
  1411→		return nil
  1412→	}
  1413→
  1414→	return nil
  1415→}

  1429→// handleInputMode handles input mode key presses
  1430→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleInputMode(key string) tea.Cmd {
  1431→	switch key {
  1432→	case "esc":
  1433→		psm.inputMode = InputModeNone
  1434→		return nil
  1435→
  1436→	case "enter":
  1437→		psm.applyInput()
  1438→		psm.inputMode = InputModeNone
  1439→		return nil
  1440→
  1441→	case "backspace":
  1442→		psm.handleBackspace()
  1443→		return nil
  1444→
  1445→	default:
  1446→		if len(key) == 1 {
  1447→			psm.handleCharInput(key)
  1448→		}
  1449→		return nil
  1450→	}
  1451→}
  1452→
  1453→// applyInput applies the current input to the appropriate field
  1454→func (psm *PortScannerModel) applyInput() {
  1455→	switch psm.inputMode {
  1456→	case InputModeTarget:
  1457→		targets := strings.Split(psm.targetInput, ",")
  1458→		psm.targets = make([]string, 0, len(targets))
  1459→		for _, target := range targets {
  1460→			target = strings.TrimSpace(target)
  1461→			if target != "" {
  1462→				psm.targets = append(psm.targets, target)
  1463→			}
  1464→		}
  1465→
  1466→	case InputModePort:
  1467→		ranges := strings.Split(psm.portInput, ",")
  1468→		psm.portRanges = make([]PortRange, 0, len(ranges))
  1469→		for _, rangeStr := range ranges {
  1470→			rangeStr = strings.TrimSpace(rangeStr)
  1471→			if portRange, err := ParsePortRange(rangeStr); err == nil {
  1472→				psm.portRanges = append(psm.portRanges, portRange)
  1473→			}
  1474→		}
  1475→
  1476→	case InputModeTimeout:
  1477→		if timeout, err := strconv.Atoi(psm.timeoutInput); err == nil && timeout > 0 {
  1478→			psm.timeout = time.Duration(timeout) * time.Second
  1479→		}
  1480→
  1481→	case InputModeConcurrency:
  1482→		if concurrency, err := strconv.Atoi(psm.concurrencyInput); err == nil && concurrency > 0 && concurrency <= 1000 {
  1483→			psm.concurrency = concurrency
  1484→		}
  1485→	}
  1486→}
  1487→
  1488→// handleBackspace handles backspace in input mode
  1489→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleBackspace() {
  1490→	switch psm.inputMode {
  1491→	case InputModeTarget:
  1492→		if len(psm.targetInput) > 0 {
  1493→			psm.targetInput = psm.targetInput[:len(psm.targetInput)-1]
  1494→		}
  1495→	case InputModePort:
  1496→		if len(psm.portInput) > 0 {
  1497→			psm.portInput = psm.portInput[:len(psm.portInput)-1]
  1498→		}
  1499→	case InputModeTimeout:
  1500→		if len(psm.timeoutInput) > 0 {
  1501→			psm.timeoutInput = psm.timeoutInput[:len(psm.timeoutInput)-1]
  1502→		}
  1503→	case InputModeConcurrency:
  1504→		if len(psm.concurrencyInput) > 0 {
  1505→			psm.concurrencyInput = psm.concurrencyInput[:len(psm.concurrencyInput)-1]
  1506→		}
  1507→	}
  1508→}
  1509→
  1510→// handleCharInput handles character input in input mode
  1511→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleCharInput(char string) {
  1512→	switch psm.inputMode {
  1513→	case InputModeTarget:
  1514→		psm.targetInput += char
  1515→	case InputModePort:
  1516→		psm.portInput += char
  1517→	case InputModeTimeout:
  1518→		if char >= "0" && char <= "9" {
  1519→			psm.timeoutInput += char
  1520→		}
  1521→	case InputModeConcurrency:
  1522→		if char >= "0" && char <= "9" {
  1523→			psm.concurrencyInput += char
  1524→		}
  1525→	}
  1526→}
  1527→
  1528→// startScan initiates a new port scan
  1529→func (psm *PortScannerModel) startScan() tea.Cmd {
  1530→	if psm.isScanning {
  1531→		return nil
  1532→	}
  1533→
  1534→	// Validate inputs
  1535→	if len(psm.targets) == 0 {
  1536→		return nil
  1537→	}
  1538→
  1539→	if len(psm.portRanges) == 0 {
  1540→		return nil
  1541→	}
  1542→
  1543→	// Clear previous results
  1544→	psm.scanResults = make([]ScanResult, 0)
  1545→	psm.selectedRow = 0
  1546→	psm.scrollOffset = 0
  1547→
  1548→	// Create scanner
  1549→	psm.scanner = NewPortScanner(psm.concurrency, psm.timeout)
  1550→
  1551→	// Create channels
  1552→	psm.resultsChan = make(chan ScanResult, 100)
  1553→	psm.progressChan = make(chan ScanProgressUpdate, 10)
  1554→	psm.errorChan = make(chan error, 10)
  1555→
  1556→	// Create context for cancellation
  1557→	psm.scanContext, psm.scanCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
  1558→
  1559→	// Initialize scan progress
  1560→	psm.currentScan = &ScanProgress{
  1561→		StartTime: time.Now(),
  1562→		Status:    "Initializing scan...",
  1563→	}
  1564→
  1565→	psm.isScanning = true
  1566→	psm.scanCancelled = false
  1567→
  1568→	// Start the scan in a goroutine
  1569→	go func() {
  1570→		err := psm.scanner.ScanPorts(psm.scanContext, psm.targets, psm.portRanges, psm.scanType, psm.progressChan)
  1571→		if err != nil && err != context.Canceled {
  1572→			psm.errorChan <- err
  1573→		}
  1574→	}()
  1575→
  1576→	return nil
  1577→}
  1578→
  1579→// cancelScan cancels the current scan
  1580→func (psm *PortScannerModel) cancelScan() tea.Cmd {
  1581→	if !psm.isScanning || psm.scanCancel == nil {
  1582→		return nil
  1583→	}
  1584→
  1585→	psm.scanCancel()
  1586→	psm.scanCancelled = true
  1587→
  1588→	if psm.currentScan != nil {
  1589→		psm.currentScan.Cancelled = true
  1590→		psm.currentScan.Status = "Cancelling scan..."
  1591→	}
  1592→
  1593→	return nil
  1594→}
  1595→
  1596→// handleScanProgress handles scan progress updates
  1597→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleScanProgress(update ScanProgressUpdate) {
  1598→	if update.Progress != nil {
  1599→		psm.currentScan = update.Progress
  1600→	}
  1601→
  1602→	if update.Result != nil {
  1603→		psm.scanResults = append(psm.scanResults, *update.Result)
  1604→		psm.sortResults()
  1605→	}
  1606→
  1607→	if update.Error != nil {
  1608→		// Handle scan error
  1609→		if psm.currentScan != nil {
  1610→			psm.currentScan.Status = fmt.Sprintf("Error: %s", update.Error.Error())
  1611→		}
  1612→	}
  1613→
  1614→	// Check if scan is complete
  1615→	if psm.currentScan != nil && (psm.currentScan.Completed || psm.currentScan.Cancelled) {
  1616→		psm.finalizeScan()
  1617→	}
  1618→}
  1619→
  1620→// handleScanComplete handles scan completion
  1621→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleScanComplete(scan CompletedScan) {
  1622→	// Add to history
  1623→	psm.scanHistory = append(psm.scanHistory, scan)
  1624→
  1625→	// Keep only the last 50 scans in history
  1626→	if len(psm.scanHistory) > 50 {
  1627→		psm.scanHistory = psm.scanHistory[1:]
  1628→	}
  1629→}
  1630→
  1631→// finalizeScan finalizes the current scan
  1632→func (psm *PortScannerModel) finalizeScan() {
  1633→	if psm.currentScan == nil {
  1634→		return
  1635→	}
  1636→
  1637→	// Create completed scan record
  1638→	completedScan := CompletedScan{
  1639→		ID:            fmt.Sprintf("scan_%d", time.Now().Unix()),
  1640→		Targets:       psm.targets,
  1641→		PortRanges:    psm.portRanges,
  1642→		ScanType:      psm.scanType,
  1643→		StartTime:     psm.currentScan.StartTime,
  1644→		EndTime:       time.Now(),
  1645→		Duration:      time.Since(psm.currentScan.StartTime),
  1646→		TotalPorts:    psm.currentScan.TotalPorts,
  1647→		OpenPorts:     psm.currentScan.OpenPorts,
  1648→		ClosedPorts:   psm.currentScan.ClosedPorts,
  1649→		FilteredPorts: psm.currentScan.FilteredPorts,
  1650→		Results:       psm.scanResults,
  1651→		Cancelled:     psm.currentScan.Cancelled,
  1652→	}
  1653→
  1654→	// Store in persistent history
  1655→	if psm.historyManager != nil {
  1656→		if err := psm.historyManager.StoreScan(completedScan); err != nil {
  1657→			// Log error but don't fail the scan
  1658→			_ = err
  1659→		}
  1660→	}
  1661→
  1662→	// Add to local history
  1663→	psm.scanHistory = append(psm.scanHistory, completedScan)
  1664→
  1665→	// Keep only the last 50 scans in local history
  1666→	if len(psm.scanHistory) > 50 {
  1667→		psm.scanHistory = psm.scanHistory[1:]
  1668→	}
  1669→
  1670→	// Reset scan state
  1671→	psm.isScanning = false
  1672→	psm.currentScan = nil
  1673→	psm.scanContext = nil
  1674→	psm.scanCancel = nil
  1675→
  1676→	// Close channels
  1677→	if psm.resultsChan != nil {
  1678→		close(psm.resultsChan)
  1679→		psm.resultsChan = nil
  1680→	}
  1681→	if psm.progressChan != nil {
  1682→		close(psm.progressChan)
  1683→		psm.progressChan = nil
  1684→	}
  1685→	if psm.errorChan != nil {
  1686→		close(psm.errorChan)
  1687→		psm.errorChan = nil
  1688→	}
  1689→}
  1690→
  1691→// sortResults sorts the scan results based on current sort settings
  1692→func (psm *PortScannerModel) sortResults() {
  1693→	sort.Slice(psm.scanResults, func(i, j int) bool {
  1694→		a, b := psm.scanResults[i], psm.scanResults[j]
  1695→
  1696→		var less bool
  1697→		switch psm.sortColumn {
  1698→		case SortByHost:
  1699→			less = a.Host < b.Host
  1700→		case SortByPort:
  1701→			less = a.Port < b.Port
  1702→		case SortByPortState:
  1703→			less = a.State < b.State
  1704→		case SortByService:
  1705→			less = a.Service < b.Service
  1706→		case SortByResponseTime:
  1707→			less = a.ResponseTime < b.ResponseTime
  1708→		case SortByTimestamp:
  1709→			less = a.Timestamp.Before(b.Timestamp)
  1710→		default:
  1711→			less = a.Port < b.Port
  1712→		}
  1713→
  1714→		if psm.sortReverse {
  1715→			return !less
  1716→		}
  1717→		return less
  1718→	})
  1719→}
  1720→
  1721→// Rendering methods
  1722→
  1723→// renderMainView renders the main port scanner view
  1724→func (psm *PortScannerModel) renderMainView() string {
  1725→	var s strings.Builder
  1726→
  1727→	// Header
  1728→	title := "◈ NETWORK PORT SCANNER"
  1729→	if psm.isScanning {
  1730→		title += fmt.Sprintf(" %s", psm.scanAnimation[psm.animFrame])
  1731→	}
  1732→
  1733→	headerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1734→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).
  1735→		Bold(true)
  1736→	s.WriteString(headerStyle.Render(title) + "\n\n")
  1737→
  1738→	// Scan configuration summary
  1739→	s.WriteString(psm.renderConfigSummary() + "\n")
  1740→
  1741→	// Scan progress (if scanning)
  1742→	if psm.isScanning && psm.currentScan != nil {
  1743→		s.WriteString(psm.renderScanProgress() + "\n")
  1744→	}
  1745→
  1746→	// Results table
  1747→	s.WriteString(psm.renderResultsTable())
  1748→
  1749→	return s.String()
  1750→}
  1751→
  1752→// renderConfigSummary renders a summary of the current scan configuration
  1753→func (psm *PortScannerModel) renderConfigSummary() string {
  1754→	var s strings.Builder
  1755→
  1756→	configStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1757→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  1758→		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).
  1759→		Padding(0, 1).
  1760→		Width(psm.width - 4)
  1761→
  1762→	// Targets
  1763→	targetsStr := strings.Join(psm.targets, ", ")
  1764→	if len(targetsStr) > 50 {
  1765→		targetsStr = targetsStr[:47] + "..."
  1766→	}
  1767→
  1768→	// Port ranges
  1769→	var portRangeStrs []string
  1770→	for _, pr := range psm.portRanges {
  1771→		portRangeStrs = append(portRangeStrs, pr.String())
  1772→	}
  1773→	portsStr := strings.Join(portRangeStrs, ", ")
  1774→	if len(portsStr) > 50 {
  1775→		portsStr = portsStr[:47] + "..."
  1776→	}
  1777→
  1778→	content := fmt.Sprintf("Targets: %s | Ports: %s | Type: %s | Timeout: %v | Concurrency: %d",
  1779→		targetsStr, portsStr, psm.scanType.String(), psm.timeout, psm.concurrency)
  1780→
  1781→	s.WriteString(configStyle.Render(content))
  1782→	return s.String()
  1783→}

  1785→// renderScanProgress renders the scan progress bar and statistics
  1786→func (psm *PortScannerModel) renderScanProgress() string {
  1787→	if psm.currentScan == nil {
  1788→		return ""
  1789→	}
  1790→
  1791→	var s strings.Builder
  1792→
  1793→	progressStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1794→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  1795→		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).
  1796→		Padding(0, 1).
  1797→		Width(psm.width - 4)
  1798→
  1799→	// Progress bar
  1800→	progress := psm.currentScan.Progress()
  1801→	barWidth := 40
  1802→	filledWidth := int(progress * float64(barWidth) / 100)
  1803→
  1804→	progressBar := strings.Repeat("█", filledWidth) + strings.Repeat("░", barWidth-filledWidth)
  1805→
  1806→	// Statistics
  1807→	elapsed := psm.currentScan.ElapsedTime()
  1808→	remaining := psm.currentScan.EstimatedTimeRemaining()
  1809→
  1810→	content := fmt.Sprintf("%s %.1f%% (%d/%d ports)\nOpen: %d | Closed: %d | Filtered: %d\nElapsed: %v | Remaining: %v\nStatus: %s",
  1811→		progressBar, progress, psm.currentScan.ScannedPorts, psm.currentScan.TotalPorts,
  1812→		psm.currentScan.OpenPorts, psm.currentScan.ClosedPorts, psm.currentScan.FilteredPorts,
  1813→		elapsed.Round(time.Second), remaining.Round(time.Second),
  1814→		psm.currentScan.Status)
  1815→
  1816→	s.WriteString(progressStyle.Render(content))
  1817→	return s.String()
  1818→}

  1820→// renderResultsTable renders the scan results table
  1821→func (psm *PortScannerModel) renderResultsTable() string {
  1822→	var s strings.Builder
  1823→
  1824→	// Table header
  1825→	headers := []string{"HOST", "PORT", "STATE", "SERVICE", "VERSION", "RESPONSE"}
  1826→	widths := []int{15, 8, 10, 15, 15, 10}
  1827→
  1828→	// Adjust widths based on available space
  1829→	totalWidth := psm.width - 10
  1830→	if totalWidth > 0 {
  1831→		// Distribute remaining width
  1832→		remaining := totalWidth - 63 // Base width of fixed columns
  1833→		if remaining > 0 {
  1834→			widths[0] += remaining / 3 // HOST
  1835→			widths[3] += remaining / 3 // SERVICE
  1836→			widths[4] += remaining / 3 // VERSION
  1837→		}
  1838→	}
  1839→
  1840→	var headerParts []string
  1841→	for i, header := range headers {
  1842→		style := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1843→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).
  1844→			Bold(true).
  1845→			Width(widths[i]).
  1846→			Align(lipgloss.Left)
  1847→		headerParts = append(headerParts, style.Render(header))
  1848→	}
  1849→
  1850→	s.WriteString(strings.Join(headerParts, " ") + "\n")
  1851→	s.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", psm.width-2) + "\n")
  1852→
  1853→	// Table rows
  1854→	if len(psm.scanResults) == 0 {
  1855→		noResults := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1856→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6b7280")).
  1857→			Align(lipgloss.Center).
  1858→			Width(psm.width - 2).
  1859→			Render("No scan results available. Press 's' to start a scan.")
  1860→		s.WriteString(noResults + "\n")
  1861→		return s.String()
  1862→	}
  1863→
  1864→	visibleRows := psm.height - 12
  1865→	startIdx := psm.scrollOffset
  1866→	endIdx := min(startIdx+visibleRows, len(psm.scanResults))
  1867→
  1868→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
  1869→		if i >= len(psm.scanResults) {
  1870→			break
  1871→		}
  1872→
  1873→		result := psm.scanResults[i]
  1874→		isSelected := i == psm.selectedRow
  1875→
  1876→		// Format fields
  1877→		host := result.Host
  1878→		if len(host) > widths[0] {
  1879→			host = host[:widths[0]-3] + "..."
  1880→		}
  1881→
  1882→		port := fmt.Sprintf("%d", result.Port)
  1883→		state := result.State.String()
  1884→		service := result.Service
  1885→		if len(service) > widths[3] {
  1886→			service = service[:widths[3]-3] + "..."
  1887→		}
  1888→
  1889→		version := result.Version
  1890→		if len(version) > widths[4] {
  1891→			version = version[:widths[4]-3] + "..."
  1892→		}
  1893→
  1894→		response := fmt.Sprintf("%.0fms", float64(result.ResponseTime.Nanoseconds())/1000000)
  1895→
  1896→		// Create row parts with appropriate colors
  1897→		parts := []string{
  1898→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[0]).Render(host),
  1899→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[1]).Render(port),
  1900→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[2]).Foreground(result.State.Color()).Render(state),
  1901→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[3]).Render(service),
  1902→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[4]).Render(version),
  1903→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[5]).Render(response),
  1904→		}
  1905→
  1906→		row := strings.Join(parts, " ")
  1907→
  1908→		if isSelected {
  1909→			row = lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1910→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#0f0f23")).
  1911→				Background(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).
  1912→				Bold(true).
  1913→				Width(psm.width - 2).
  1914→				Render(" " + row + " ")
  1915→		}
  1916→
  1917→		s.WriteString(row + "\n")
  1918→	}
  1919→
  1920→	return s.String()
  1921→}

  2188→// min function is defined in system_monitor.go
  2189→
  2190→// handleExportKeys handles key presses in the export view
  2191→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleExportKeys(key string) tea.Cmd {
  2192→	switch key {
  2193→	case "esc":
  2194→		psm.showExport = false
  2195→		psm.inputMode = InputModeNone
  2196→		return nil
  2197→
  2198→	case "1":
  2199→		psm.exportFormat = ExportFormatJSON
  2200→		return nil
  2201→
  2202→	case "2":
  2203→		psm.exportFormat = ExportFormatCSV
  2204→		return nil
  2205→
  2206→	case "3":
  2207→		psm.exportFormat = ExportFormatXML
  2208→		return nil
  2209→
  2210→	case "4":
  2211→		psm.exportFormat = ExportFormatTXT
  2212→		return nil
  2213→
  2214→	case "f":
  2215→		psm.inputMode = InputModeTarget // Reuse for filename input
  2216→		psm.exportFilename = ""
  2217→		return nil
  2218→
  2219→	case "enter":
  2220→		if psm.inputMode == InputModeTarget {
  2221→			// Apply filename and export
  2222→			psm.inputMode = InputModeNone
  2223→			return psm.exportWithFilename()
  2224→		} else {
  2225→			// Export with default filename
  2226→			return psm.exportCurrentResults()
  2227→		}
  2228→
  2229→	case "backspace":
  2230→		if psm.inputMode == InputModeTarget && len(psm.exportFilename) > 0 {
  2231→			psm.exportFilename = psm.exportFilename[:len(psm.exportFilename)-1]
  2232→		}
  2233→		return nil
  2234→
  2235→	default:
  2236→		if psm.inputMode == InputModeTarget && len(key) == 1 {
  2237→			psm.exportFilename += key
  2238→		}
  2239→		return nil
  2240→	}
  2241→}
  2242→
  2243→// handleComparisonKeys handles key presses in the comparison view
  2244→func (psm *PortScannerModel) handleComparisonKeys(key string) tea.Cmd {
  2245→	switch key {
  2246→	case "esc":
  2247→		psm.showComparison = false
  2248→		return nil
  2249→
  2250→	case "up", "k":
  2251→		if psm.selectedRow > 0 {
  2252→			psm.selectedRow--
  2253→		}
  2254→		return nil
  2255→
  2256→	case "down", "j":
  2257→		historyEntries, _ := psm.historyManager.GetHistory()
  2258→		if psm.selectedRow < len(historyEntries)-1 {
  2259→			psm.selectedRow++
  2260→		}
  2261→		return nil
  2262→
  2263→	case "enter":
  2264→		// Compare with selected history entry
  2265→		historyEntries, err := psm.historyManager.GetHistory()
  2266→		if err != nil || psm.selectedRow >= len(historyEntries) {
  2267→			return nil
  2268→		}
  2269→
  2270→		selectedEntry := historyEntries[psm.selectedRow]
  2271→		return psm.compareWithHistory(selectedEntry.ID)
  2272→	}
  2273→
  2274→	return nil
  2275→}
  2276→
  2277→// exportCurrentResults exports the current scan results
  2278→func (psm *PortScannerModel) exportCurrentResults() tea.Cmd {
  2279→	return func() tea.Msg {
  2280→		err := psm.exporter.ExportResults(psm.scanResults, psm.exportFormat, "")
  2281→		if err != nil {
  2282→			return errMsg(fmt.Errorf("export failed: %w", err))
  2283→		}
  2284→		psm.showExport = false
  2285→		return nil
  2286→	}
  2287→}
  2288→
  2289→// exportWithFilename exports with a custom filename
  2290→func (psm *PortScannerModel) exportWithFilename() tea.Cmd {
  2291→	filename := psm.exportFilename
  2292→	return func() tea.Msg {
  2293→		err := psm.exporter.ExportResults(psm.scanResults, psm.exportFormat, filename)
  2294→		if err != nil {
  2295→			return errMsg(fmt.Errorf("export failed: %w", err))
  2296→		}
  2297→		psm.showExport = false
  2298→		return nil
  2299→	}
  2300→}
  2301→
  2302→// compareWithHistory compares current results with a historical scan
  2303→func (psm *PortScannerModel) compareWithHistory(historyID string) tea.Cmd {
  2304→	return func() tea.Msg {
  2305→		// Create a temporary scan from current results
  2306→		currentScan := CompletedScan{
  2307→			ID:        "current",
  2308→			StartTime: time.Now(),
  2309→			Results:   psm.scanResults,
  2310→		}
  2311→
  2312→		// Get historical scan
  2313→		historicalScan, err := psm.historyManager.GetScan(historyID)
  2314→		if err != nil {
  2315→			return errMsg(fmt.Errorf("failed to load historical scan: %w", err))
  2316→		}
  2317→
  2318→		// Perform comparison
  2319→		comparison := psm.comparator.compareScanData(currentScan, *historicalScan)
  2320→
  2321→		// Generate and display report (this would need UI implementation)
  2322→		report := psm.comparator.GenerateComparisonReport(comparison)
  2323→		_ = report // TODO: Display in UI
  2324→
  2325→		psm.showComparison = false
  2326→		return nil
  2327→	}
  2328→}
  2329→
  2330→// renderExportView renders the export dialog
  2331→func (psm *PortScannerModel) renderExportView() string {
  2332→	if !psm.showExport {
  2333→		return ""
  2334→	}
  2335→
  2336→	var s strings.Builder
  2337→
  2338→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  2339→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#00ff00")).
  2340→		Bold(true).
  2341→		Render("📤 EXPORT SCAN RESULTS")
  2342→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  2343→
  2344→	// Format selection
  2345→	s.WriteString("Select Export Format:\n")
  2346→	formats := []struct {
  2347→		key    string
  2348→		format ExportFormat
  2349→		desc   string
  2350→	}{
  2351→		{"1", ExportFormatJSON, "JSON - Structured data format"},
  2352→		{"2", ExportFormatCSV, "CSV - Comma-separated values"},
  2353→		{"3", ExportFormatXML, "XML - Extensible markup language"},
  2354→		{"4", ExportFormatTXT, "TXT - Human-readable text"},
  2355→	}
  2356→
  2357→	for _, f := range formats {
  2358→		style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#888888"))
  2359→		if f.format == psm.exportFormat {
  2360→			style = style.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#00ff00")).Bold(true)
  2361→		}
  2362→		s.WriteString(style.Render(fmt.Sprintf("  %s. %s\n", f.key, f.desc)))
  2363→	}
  2364→
  2365→	s.WriteString("\n")
  2366→
  2367→	// Filename input
  2368→	if psm.inputMode == InputModeTarget {
  2369→		s.WriteString("Filename: " + psm.exportFilename + "_\n")
  2370→	} else {
  2371→		s.WriteString("Press 'f' to specify filename (optional)\n")
  2372→	}
  2373→
  2374→	s.WriteString("\nControls:\n")
  2375→	s.WriteString("  ENTER: Export with current settings\n")
  2376→	s.WriteString("  ESC: Cancel\n")
  2377→
  2378→	return s.String()
  2379→}
  2380→
  2381→// renderComparisonView renders the comparison dialog
  2382→func (psm *PortScannerModel) renderComparisonView() string {
  2383→	if !psm.showComparison {
  2384→		return ""
  2385→	}
  2386→
  2387→	var s strings.Builder
  2388→
  2389→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  2390→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#ffff00")).
  2391→		Bold(true).
  2392→		Render("🔍 COMPARE WITH HISTORICAL SCAN")
  2393→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  2394→
  2395→	// Get history entries
  2396→	historyEntries, err := psm.historyManager.GetHistory()
  2397→	if err != nil || len(historyEntries) == 0 {
  2398→		s.WriteString("No historical scans available for comparison.\n")
  2399→		s.WriteString("Press ESC to cancel.\n")
  2400→		return s.String()
  2401→	}
  2402→
  2403→	s.WriteString("Select a historical scan to compare with:\n\n")
  2404→
  2405→	// Display history entries
  2406→	maxEntries := min(len(historyEntries), 10)
  2407→	for i := 0; i < maxEntries; i++ {
  2408→		entry := historyEntries[i]
  2409→		style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#888888"))
  2410→		if i == psm.selectedRow {
  2411→			style = style.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#ffff00")).Bold(true)
  2412→		}
  2413→
  2414→		line := fmt.Sprintf("  %s - %s (%d ports, %d open)",
  2415→			entry.StartTime.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
  2416→			strings.Join(entry.Targets, ", "),
  2417→			entry.TotalPorts,
  2418→			entry.OpenPorts)
  2419→
  2420→		if len(line) > 60 {
  2421→			line = line[:57] + "..."
  2422→		}
  2423→
  2424→		s.WriteString(style.Render(line) + "\n")
  2425→	}
  2426→
  2427→	s.WriteString("\nControls:\n")
  2428→	s.WriteString("  ↑↓: Navigate  ENTER: Compare  ESC: Cancel\n")
  2429→
  2430→	return s.String()
  2431→}

    15→// PackageManager defines the interface that all package managers must implement
    16→// Implements Task 4.1 - Package Manager Abstraction Layer
    17→type PackageManager interface {
    18→	// GetName returns the name of the package manager
    19→	GetName() string
    20→
    21→	// IsAvailable checks if the package manager is available on the system
    22→	IsAvailable() bool
    23→
    24→	// IsProjectSupported checks if the current directory supports this package manager
    25→	IsProjectSupported(projectPath string) bool
    26→
    27→	// ListPackages returns all installed packages

    29→
    30→	// InstallPackage installs a package
    31→	InstallPackage(ctx context.Context, packageName, version string) error
    32→
    33→	// UpdatePackage updates a package to the latest or specified version
    34→	UpdatePackage(ctx context.Context, packageName, version string) error
    35→
    36→	// RemovePackage removes a package

    38→

    44→
    45→	// UpdateAll updates all packages to their latest versions

   135→// PackageManagerRegistry manages multiple package managers

   147→// NewPackageManagerRegistry creates a new package manager registry
   148→func NewPackageManagerRegistry(projectPath string) *PackageManagerRegistry {
   149→	registry := &PackageManagerRegistry{
   150→		managers:    make(map[string]PackageManager),
   151→		projectPath: projectPath,
   152→	}
   153→
   154→	// Register built-in package managers
   155→	registry.RegisterManager(&NPMManager{})
   156→	registry.RegisterManager(&GoModManager{})
   157→	registry.RegisterManager(&PipManager{})
   158→
   159→	return registry
   160→}
   161→
   162→// RegisterManager registers a new package manager
   163→func (r *PackageManagerRegistry) RegisterManager(manager PackageManager) {
   164→	r.managers[manager.GetName()] = manager
   165→}
   166→
   167→// GetAvailableManagers returns all available package managers
   168→func (r *PackageManagerRegistry) GetAvailableManagers() []PackageManager {
   169→	var available []PackageManager
   170→	for _, manager := range r.managers {
   171→		if manager.IsAvailable() && manager.IsProjectSupported(r.projectPath) {
   172→			available = append(available, manager)
   173→		}
   174→	}
   175→	return available
   176→}
   177→
   178→// GetManager returns a specific package manager by name
   179→func (r *PackageManagerRegistry) GetManager(name string) (PackageManager, bool) {
   180→	manager, exists := r.managers[name]
   181→	return manager, exists
   182→}
   183→
   184→// DetectProjectManagers detects which package managers are supported by the current project
   185→func (r *PackageManagerRegistry) DetectProjectManagers() []PackageManager {
   186→	var supported []PackageManager
   187→	for _, manager := range r.managers {
   188→		if manager.IsProjectSupported(r.projectPath) {
   189→			supported = append(supported, manager)
   190→		}
   191→	}
   192→	return supported
   193→}

   209→// IsProjectSupported checks if the current directory has a package.json

   216→// ListPackages returns all installed npm packages
   217→func (n *NPMManager) ListPackages(ctx context.Context) ([]Package, error) {
   218→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "npm", "list", "--json", "--depth=0")
   219→	output, err := cmd.Output()
   220→	if err != nil {
   221→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list npm packages: %w", err)
   222→	}
   223→
   224→	var result struct {
   225→		Dependencies map[string]struct {
   226→			Version  string `json:"version"`
   227→			Resolved string `json:"resolved"`
   228→		} `json:"dependencies"`
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); err != nil {
   232→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse npm list output: %w", err)
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	var packages []Package
   236→	for name, dep := range result.Dependencies {
   237→		packages = append(packages, Package{
   238→			Name:    name,
   239→			Version: dep.Version,
   240→		})
   241→	}
   242→
   243→	sort.Slice(packages, func(i, j int) bool {
   244→		return packages[i].Name < packages[j].Name
   245→	})
   246→
   247→	return packages, nil
   248→}

   250→// InstallPackage installs an npm package
   251→func (n *NPMManager) InstallPackage(ctx context.Context, packageName, version string) error {
   252→	var cmd *exec.Cmd
   253→	if version != "" {
   254→		cmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, "npm", "install", packageName+"@"+version)
   255→	} else {
   256→		cmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, "npm", "install", packageName)
   257→	}
   258→
   259→	return cmd.Run()

   319→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "npm", "info", packageName, "--json")
   320→	output, err := cmd.Output()
   321→	if err != nil {
   322→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get npm package info: %w", err)
   323→	}

   325→	var info struct {

   327→		Version      string            `json:"version"`
   328→		Description  string            `json:"description"`
   329→		Author       string            `json:"author"`
   330→		License      string            `json:"license"`

   332→		Keywords     []string          `json:"keywords"`
   333→		Dependencies map[string]string `json:"dependencies"`
   334→		Repository   struct {

   337→		Bugs struct {

   346→	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &info); err != nil {
   347→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse npm info output: %w", err)
   348→	}
   349→
   350→	lastUpdated, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, info.Time.Modified)
   351→
   352→	deps := make(map[string]DependencyInfo)
   353→	for name, version := range info.Dependencies {

   358→	}

   360→	return &PackageInfo{

   371→		BugTracker:   info.Bugs.URL,

   397→// CheckSecurity checks for security vulnerabilities in npm packages
   398→func (n *NPMManager) CheckSecurity(ctx context.Context) ([]SecurityIssue, error) {
   399→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "npm", "audit", "--json")
   400→	output, err := cmd.Output()
   401→	if err != nil {
   402→		// npm audit returns non-zero exit code when vulnerabilities are found
   403→		if exitError, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
   404→			output = exitError.Stderr
   405→		} else {
   406→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to run npm audit: %w", err)
   407→		}
   408→	}
   409→
   410→	var result struct {
   411→		Vulnerabilities map[string]struct {
   412→			Severity string `json:"severity"`
   413→			Title    string `json:"title"`
   414→			URL      string `json:"url"`
   415→			Range    string `json:"range"`
   416→		} `json:"vulnerabilities"`
   417→	}
   418→
   419→	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); err != nil {
   420→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse npm audit output: %w", err)
   421→	}
   422→
   423→	var issues []SecurityIssue
   424→	for pkg, vuln := range result.Vulnerabilities {
   425→		issues = append(issues, SecurityIssue{
   426→			Package:     pkg,
   427→			Advisory:    vuln.URL,
   428→			Severity:    vuln.Severity,
   429→			Title:       vuln.Title,
   430→			Reference:   vuln.URL,
   431→			PublishDate: time.Now(), // npm audit doesn't provide publish date
   432→		})
   433→	}
   434→
   435→	return issues, nil
   436→}

   438→// GoModManager implements PackageManager for Go modules
   439→type GoModManager struct{}
   440→
   441→// GetName returns the name of go mod package manager
   442→func (g *GoModManager) GetName() string {
   443→	return "go"
   444→}
   445→
   446→// IsAvailable checks if go is available on the system
   447→func (g *GoModManager) IsAvailable() bool {
   448→	_, err := exec.LookPath("go")
   449→	return err == nil
   450→}
   451→
   452→// IsProjectSupported checks if the current directory has a go.mod
   453→func (g *GoModManager) IsProjectSupported(projectPath string) bool {
   454→	goMod := filepath.Join(projectPath, "go.mod")
   455→	_, err := os.Stat(goMod)
   456→	return err == nil
   457→}
   458→
   459→// ListPackages returns all Go module dependencies
   460→func (g *GoModManager) ListPackages(ctx context.Context) ([]Package, error) {
   461→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "go", "list", "-m", "-json", "all")
   462→	output, err := cmd.Output()
   463→	if err != nil {
   464→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list go modules: %w", err)
   465→	}
   466→
   467→	var packages []Package
   468→	decoder := json.NewDecoder(strings.NewReader(string(output)))
   469→
   470→	for decoder.More() {
   471→		var mod struct {
   472→			Path    string `json:"Path"`
   473→			Version string `json:"Version"`
   474→			Main    bool   `json:"Main"`
   475→		}
   476→
   477→		if err := decoder.Decode(&mod); err != nil {
   478→			continue
   479→		}
   480→
   481→		if !mod.Main { // Skip the main module
   482→			packages = append(packages, Package{
   483→				Name:    mod.Path,
   484→				Version: mod.Version,
   485→			})
   486→		}
   487→	}
   488→
   489→	sort.Slice(packages, func(i, j int) bool {
   490→		return packages[i].Name < packages[j].Name
   491→	})

   582→// PipManager implements PackageManager for pip
   583→type PipManager struct{}
   584→
   585→// GetName returns the name of pip package manager
   586→func (p *PipManager) GetName() string {
   587→	return "pip"
   588→}
   589→
   590→// IsAvailable checks if pip is available on the system
   591→func (p *PipManager) IsAvailable() bool {
   592→	_, err := exec.LookPath("pip")
   593→	return err == nil
   594→}
   595→
   596→// IsProjectSupported checks if the current directory has Python files or requirements.txt
   597→func (p *PipManager) IsProjectSupported(projectPath string) bool {
   598→	// Check for requirements.txt or any .py files
   599→	requirementsTxt := filepath.Join(projectPath, "requirements.txt")
   600→	if _, err := os.Stat(requirementsTxt); err == nil {
   601→		return true
   602→	}
   603→
   604→	// Check for Python files
   605→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(projectPath)
   606→	if err != nil {
   607→		return false
   608→	}
   609→
   610→	for _, entry := range entries {
   611→		if strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".py") {
   612→			return true
   613→		}
   614→	}
   615→
   616→	return false
   617→}
   618→
   619→// ListPackages returns all installed pip packages
   620→func (p *PipManager) ListPackages(ctx context.Context) ([]Package, error) {
   621→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "list", "--format=json")
   622→	output, err := cmd.Output()
   623→	if err != nil {
   624→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list pip packages: %w", err)
   625→	}
   626→
   627→	var results []struct {
   628→		Name    string `json:"name"`
   629→		Version string `json:"version"`
   630→	}
   631→
   632→	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &results); err != nil {
   633→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse pip list output: %w", err)
   634→	}
   635→
   636→	var packages []Package
   637→	for _, result := range results {
   638→		packages = append(packages, Package{
   639→			Name:    result.Name,
   640→			Version: result.Version,
   641→		})
   642→	}
   643→
   644→	sort.Slice(packages, func(i, j int) bool {
   645→		return packages[i].Name < packages[j].Name
   646→	})
   647→
   648→	return packages, nil
   649→}
   650→
   651→// InstallPackage installs a pip package
   652→func (p *PipManager) InstallPackage(ctx context.Context, packageName, version string) error {
   653→	var cmd *exec.Cmd
   654→	if version != "" {
   655→		cmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "install", packageName+"=="+version)
   656→	} else {
   657→		cmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "install", packageName)
   658→	}
   659→
   660→	return cmd.Run()
   661→}
   662→
   663→// UpdatePackage updates a pip package
   664→func (p *PipManager) UpdatePackage(ctx context.Context, packageName, version string) error {
   665→	var cmd *exec.Cmd
   666→	if version != "" {
   667→		cmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "install", "--upgrade", packageName+"=="+version)
   668→	} else {
   669→		cmd = exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "install", "--upgrade", packageName)
   670→	}
   671→
   672→	return cmd.Run()
   673→}
   674→
   675→// RemovePackage removes a pip package
   676→func (p *PipManager) RemovePackage(ctx context.Context, packageName string) error {
   677→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "uninstall", "-y", packageName)
   678→	return cmd.Run()
   679→}
   680→
   681→// SearchPackages searches for pip packages
   682→func (p *PipManager) SearchPackages(ctx context.Context, query string) ([]PackageSearchResult, error) {
   683→	// pip search was disabled, would need to use PyPI API
   684→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("search not available for pip (deprecated), use PyPI web interface")
   685→}
   686→
   687→// GetPackageInfo retrieves detailed information about a pip package
   688→func (p *PipManager) GetPackageInfo(ctx context.Context, packageName string) (*PackageInfo, error) {
   689→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "show", packageName)
   690→	output, err := cmd.Output()
   691→	if err != nil {
   692→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get pip package info: %w", err)
   693→	}
   694→
   695→	// Parse pip show output (key: value format)
   696→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
   697→	info := &PackageInfo{}
   698→
   699→	for _, line := range lines {
   700→		if strings.Contains(line, ":") {
   701→			parts := strings.SplitN(line, ":", 2)
   702→			if len(parts) != 2 {
   703→				continue
   704→			}
   705→			key := strings.TrimSpace(parts[0])
   706→			value := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
   707→
   708→			switch key {
   709→			case "Name":
   710→				info.Name = value
   711→			case "Version":
   712→				info.Version = value
   713→			case "Summary":
   714→				info.Description = value
   715→			case "Author":
   716→				info.Author = value
   717→			case "License":
   718→				info.License = value
   719→			case "Home-page":
   720→				info.Homepage = value
   721→			}
   722→		}
   723→	}
   724→
   725→	return info, nil
   726→}
   727→
   728→// UpdateAll updates all pip packages
   729→func (p *PipManager) UpdateAll(ctx context.Context) error {
   730→	// pip doesn't have a built-in update all command
   731→	// This would require listing all packages and updating each one
   732→	return fmt.Errorf("update all not supported by pip directly")
   733→}
   734→
   735→// CleanCache cleans the pip cache
   736→func (p *PipManager) CleanCache(ctx context.Context) error {
   737→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "pip", "cache", "purge")
   738→	return cmd.Run()
   739→}

Analysis:
Looking at this conversation chronologically, I can see this is a session continuation from a previous conversation that reached context limits. The user provided a detailed summary of previous work on a Go terminal multitool application and asked me to continue from where the previous session left off.

From the provided context summary, I can see:

- The most recent work was analyzing component registration issues - specifically that only ViewSettings and ViewAIAssistant were registered in main.go despite other components having implementations

The key finding is a component registration gap: several ViewTypes are defined (ViewOverview, ViewPortScanner, ViewPackageManager, ViewContainerMgmt, ViewBashTerminal, ViewSystemMonitor) but only ViewSettings and ViewAIAssistant are actually registered in main.go lines 127-128. The system_monitor.go, port_scanner.go, and package_manager.go all have complete ToolComponent implementations but are not registered.

The user's final request was to create a detailed summary of the conversation, which is what I'm doing now.

     - Advanced port scanning with service detection, banner grabbing, rate limiting

     - Has security checking, dependency tree analysis, and package operations
